title: Only 1 day for the results! 🤩
body:
Wow!!  What a fast service!  

My son and I felt sick.
Yesterday, Friday, June 15, 22, I call early in the morning to make an appointment for the same day.  We took the test before 10 in the morning and today Saturday at 3:00 pm, I received the Negative results.  
The Wanda Winter Havennurse was excellent with us and even made us laugh. He was very kind and empathetic.  

Thanks for your prompt reply!!

Wanda I. Santiago and Humberto Quezada

title: Apps requires my iPhone passcode - Nope!
body:
My initial Covid tests (Rapid & PCR) results were negative, confirmed by this app.  Deleted this app from my phone.  

Five days later I get a call from FL Dept of Health to follow up with my “Positive Covid results”.  Tried to  d/l this app to understand and it’s now requiring my phone security code. No way, this is NOT secure. Do not recommend this app if your phone security code is “required” to install. Bad idea.

title: Poor Coordination with the Airlines
body:
Ok recently 3days ago took a PCR test from CVS Clinic in Fort Myers. 
I received a text message indicating I was COVID free. 
My issue was the airlines refusing to accept this website and instead was asking passengers for a paper copy. 
My stand was that I would believe the results from an electronic system run by the state than an individual hand over a paper copy to the airlines! 
This app will only be effective if the. State Government made it an official standard for airline’s. I did not fly…because at 4am there was not a store opened to make copies for a requirement not mention on the airlines website nort after 7 calls to American Airlines requesting they provide me a list of requirements to fly. Very good site but it will only work if it’s standardized and up held by state govt! Oh and state govt should make airlines provide a list of requirements to fly thru the airlines website, if they want to do business in Florida PBI..! Icefire244

title: Seems very inaccurate

Though I had my first ever test on 1/19/2022. Not sure how that’s possible. I live in Florida but still have a Chicago number and have been in Florida for 11 years. All the test results are in my name and show the state of Florida seal. Something is off about the whole thing and not sure if I trust this app or the test results I received on 1/19/2022.

title: Useless App

But alas, it is not. Now I have the pleasure of chasing around my county health dept to receive the results prior to an important trip I have to take and of course that’s a wild goose chase. It would be much easier if they would just text/email the results like they normally do. Not everything needs an app that you can barely rely on.

title: Useless without Exposure API
body:
For app-based contract tracing to work, it needs to be used by at least 60% of the population. Unfortunately this app is never going to hit that level of penetration by itself.

The Apple/Google Exposure API was designed to work across both Android and iOS devices by being built-in to the system (OS) level. This means more than 60% of the population is already set up to use the Exposure API—which works across state borders while maintaining privacy and battery in the process.

The creators here are looking to collect extra data (specifically GPS info) from users that is banned from being present in apps with the Exposure API. How does more data help if it means the user base is lower?

This app is designed to help the state, not help people. I’d rather use an app that doesn’t miss my interactions with the virus.

App deleted.

title: Privacy issues, no Apple/Google API
body:
This application still does not use the Apple/Google contact tracing API which is secure and effective. It instead relies on tracking your location.  There are huge privacy concerns and implications with this methodology.

You cannot even use the application without providing your phone number. 

In order for contact tracing applications to be effective, they must be in widespread use. A large number of individuals are concerned about the government tracking their location, regardless of the reasoning, and will not run the application as it currently stands.

I understand having location data will help identify hotspots, but having an effective level of participation is more important. 

You will not see and effective level of adoption using the current methodology.

body: App does not share details on how to get appt. and any arrival criteria - ie grace period. App restricts from logging in timely and two factor authentication fails repeatedly. App does not provide any instructions on where to go for appt. in large hospital.  Bad experience all round - calling you provider is a better way of getting information relevant to any appt.

title: Over charge No results
body: Went in for urinating blood. Three operations which I did not take so well. Over $90,000.00 in charges and they want to harass me for a charge they refuse to properly bill to the insurance/ Medicare for.  The insurance say I’m not liable and have Aurora properly re-bill it.  I’m still filling the toilet with blood.  Harassment for @ little over $23.00.       Poor care from Kenosha Aurora Hospital.

title: LiveWell and Me
body: Thanks for all your help in managing the many many things that need to be managed. You remind me to do things that happen to slip by me without being mean about it.

title: Killing it with tech integration
body: I absolutely love being able to get all my test results, reminders, visit notes, and make appointments through this app. Everything is in one place for all of my doctors. Really makes life easier!!

title: Messaging
body: Cannot message my primary with simple questions about follow up or a prescription. Calling gets me put on hold for 20 minutes. Your system is wasting my time and negatively affecting my care.

title: Not the easiest
body: This app has quite a few flaws when it comes to booking new appointments and being able to message providers. Missing a lot of “reasons for visit”. I’m sure someone me can take time out and make it better since healthcare is so important. App feels kind of thrown together which is why I just call my doctors directly now

title: NO RESPONSE
body: I messaged my doctor twice in the same week, and have not received any response. They say that they will respond within 48 hours, they don’t!

title: First visit
body: Great experiences with my first visit today.  The doctor and their staff covered all my symptoms and made sure I got treated properly with everything I need.  I had so much treatments over due since Covid which they all were covered and scheduled accordingly.  All processes are smooth.  Staff are helpful.  The LiveWell app works great for all communicating and retrieving records.  I am very pleased that I’ve found this office.

title: Very Easy To Use
body: Love this app, very convenient! Easy to communicate with my doctor and nurses, schedule appointments, check my lab results. I love it !

title: Can’t schedule
body: No easy way to schedule appointments

title: Can’t add my new insurance
body: Did precheck in thinking that is where I’d be able to add my new insurance. It did not prompt me anywhere to add it. Only to make an uninsured payment.

title: LiveWell app
body: The LiveWell app is very useful and allows access to your data as requested.

title: Trash App
body: App is trash. Can never schedule an appointment. Keeps saying call.

title: LiveWell
body: Extremely easy to use, stores all of ur medical information and scripts appointment reminders that u can put on ur calendar right away. But most of all u can stay in touch with ur doc weather u go in to see them or not.

title: No way to contact primary care provider team.
body: Why did you remove the feature to be able to contact my primary care provider team?

title: Scum of the earth
body: Aurora is a joke and their policies are garbage. You should not be allowed to charge people what they do. Such a money hungry company is the worst in the world. I wish I never went here.

title: Glitchy
body: The app is very glitchy and has said for several months that my connection isn’t “good” and some info can’t be displayed.

title: Think Twice
body: The app is easy to use and a fast way to get care. Aurora overall is the worst possible healthcare provider I have ever worked with. The app is good the providers with Aurora are not.

title: Doctors and Staff Absolutely Amazing
body:
I love the help
in every way it’s so helpful. I will recommend to any disabled person every time. I’m beyond thankful the experienced is so wonderful.

title: LiveWell app
body: Awesome

title: Logs me out
body: Stop logging me out

title: Best Healthcare App
body: I’m able to manage my medications, talk directly with my doctor as well as schedule appointments and do same day virtual visits if needed. Truly a great platform

title: Physical
body: So I got a specific set of instructions, along with direction and a special link to “schedule a physical or physical therapy.” All good. Except when I tried to actually schedule my pre-op physical. ‘Physical’ is not one of the choices on the “what are you scheduling” list ! What a silly system. NONE of the choices are even close … and no “other” choice at the end. 😡

title: Inability to send message to my provider
body:
Very frustrating, there is no way to send message to my provider even after answering two dozens of questions.
It is time consuming format and still it does not take you to desired area .
It is actually worst way to attempt to send message to your provider and get frustrated with not being able to do this .
It is 730 PM Sunday , and now all I have to do is wait untill morning to send message to Dr B Perlow. 
Thank you 
Mr Peter

title: App is very good
body: The LiveWell app does a very good job of showing my test results and upcoming appointments. I can easily message my care providers.

title: Very nice make life easy to easily access your health record. I love it

title: Scheduling

title: Unanswered Emails
body: I’ve sent two emails to my primary care.. to date no response. Since Advocate is a ‘medical care’ provider there should be a mechanism to flag ‘unanswered’ emails! Overall I’m very pleased with Advocate but this disconnect is too important not to mention. I will bring this up with my primary care doctor on 12/17/2025 so she too is aware!

title: Very difficult
body: So hard to get refill on medications.   Thinking about ditching advocate all together.

title: Review
body:
The idea of the app is wonderful. I find it difficult to navigate 
though.

title: I love this app
body: I hate calling my doctor’s office. It’s better to use the app anyway.

title: Good care
body: One of the best Care facilities in Milwaukee

title: Medicine
body: I would like to know when I have a prescription to pick up. Or when  approved . Apparently one prescription went to Walgreens, but they didn’t call me and I wasn’t told that I was approve.

title: Confusing
body: They bill  for multiple separate services on separate accounts, and it make it very difficult to keep track of payments

title: Difficult to re-find results
body: Leave the results alone.  Don’t hide them once reviewed.

title: Slow Code for verification

title: Impossible to enter text code
body: Thanks for nothing!

title: Easy To Navigate
body: The app is easy to navigate. I’m giving it 4 stars because they make it hard to message your provider if you have questions.

title: Livewell
body: I love using Livewell. I can see reports and messages from doctors, tests results and upcoming visits. I can even make appointments! I look at it everyday.

title: Amazing Transparency of process
body: Recently had a loved one get hospitalized and undergo treatment. This app showed exactly what process was going on, showed us results of the labs and even what medications are being given. The transparency level helped the family feel informed and in control. This is a great initiative and highly appreciated. In those moments of uncertainty the info provided by the app proved to be our glimmer of hope.

title: Loving the app
body: I just love LiveWell App.  It’s easy and convenient to use.  Thank you for creating this app!

title: It is buggy
body: As of recently when I open the app it just gets in to a loop showing the sign in page and back to the home page, I cant enter or click on anything.

title: Voice
body: No place to comment to a provider or to any other healthcare worker when I need to respond to a comment

title: Pre-surgery instructions
body: Trying to read the pre-surgery instructions is difficult for people with visual problems. It would be helpful if we were able to enlarge it slightly..

title: Great BUT..
body: Love and use the app for most things but of course Aurora at Home, the branch that supports my Cpap is not on the app. The service from Aurora at Home is always terrible and of course not on the app and never any surveys. Honestly makes me wonder if Aurora knows this and is trying to hide them on purpose.

title: Why not?
body: Why can’t I print out my health conditions at my convenience?

title: Hate
body: I hate this app. It makes you double verify and takes forever to send the code. Won’t let me turn it off. All I want to do is pay my bill, man.

title: Great App
body: Very comprehensive app with most info needed and ability to communicate with providers!  My only issue is that for my 2-step verification, it only works with my email even tho I’ve verified my cell number several times.

Very helpful; keeps all of my appts straight. Also I have been able to communicate with personnel very easily. And I always get a response. I really appreciate that. 
I actually use the ap quite frequently.

title: Contacting my doctor
body:
Poor service
Due to live well.    I have no way to contact Dr Daniel Lazar.  He told me to contact him so he can up my Trulicity dosage to 4.5.

title: Mike Glines
body:
Live well is an excellent IT strategy! 
I absolutely love it !!

title: Review
body: The Doctor doesn’t respond in timely manner. When i had my mammogram they said I needed to do again! I was afraid . I tried to reach out to find out what was wrong no response! It was a  week later and I don’t think  it was the doctor that responded. Now with my refill that i need! no response!

title: Live Well App
body: I like the app as it gives information that’s useful as far as appointments, visits, test results.  I especially like the message option as I most usually get responses to my messages to doctors very quick.

title: Login is stupid
body: Why do I have to press login to be taken to the login page. It used to not be this way. I should be able to login from the moment I open the app. The UI designer and all approvers should be fired.

title: Scheduling nightmare
body: I have had numerous bad experiences scheduling necessary appointments.  Most recently after an ER visit,  when trying to schedule the follow up appointment we were told to call a number which we did, only to fall into the rabbit hole where I wasted time talking with someone who was not able to help.  Very frustrated by getting messages that do not help me get the required task completed and waste my time

title: Dr office info

title: My Care

title: App review
body: I love using LiveWell  I’ve I read it, it gives me a reminder of what the doctor told me in my appointment. I love the graphs and charts it gives me comparisons from my previous tests. It is very easy to maneuver through and if I have questions for my doctor I can refer back to the charts and graphs. I find it’s very useful.

title: LiveWell app
body: I do love being able to leave messages for my dictators if I have to. Also requesting medication refills without calling the office. It makes it easier doing the things that I have to do. Like checking into the appointments before going to the appointments.

title: I love it!
body: I just love it! I would say all the important info is there for you to check all about what happened in your appt, what to do etc

title: My Chart

title: Autopay

title: Constant Changes
body: I am so sick of wasting my time trying to get somewhere on this site.  They keep making changes which only makes it impossible to follow their instructions to get the info you need!  It used to work just fine, but then they keep adding more things and then nothing works right.  Why didn’t you leave it as it was?  If it ain’t broke, don’t fix it…but, “no,” they just keep making changes just to get more work from the provider.  Now the site is garbage!

title: Very Impressed

I am also VERY impressed with my LiveWell app. It keeps me up to date and I always know what appointments and tests are coming up. Thank You

title: Very slow , took long to log in

title: Log in page
body: The app used to open on the log in page but after an update you have to click on the log in button to get to the log in page. It was so much easier when the app went directly to that page, for some it is a matter of accessibility.

title: LiveWell
body: Very convenient

title: A+
body: So convenient!

title: Live Well app
body: I enjoy being able to use the Live Well app to see test results, communicate with my doctors, and that I can link it to other doctors I seen that are at different locations ( offices, Hospitals). I feel it’s important that all my heath care is visible to multiple providers.

title: Billing nuisance
body:
App repeats too much
It’s quite irritating to see the Billing show up consistently for such a small amount. I called in and told them I would take care of it when my next paycheck arrived because I only received the bill a few weeks ago. I get repeated phone calls every day, and this app is a real nuisance. I’m paying them tomorrow when I get my paycheck.

title: Disappointed
body: This app is worst the firat work just fine. Every other app out there you open it with Face ID but this is like a 2 step Face ID which is time consuming and then you add on the fact that it’s slow and doesn’t have the straight forward experience as prior to this redesign

title: Difficult to navigate
body:
A lot of bullet items do not apply so I end up choosing a non applicable one. Always have to fat finger a long code in or use 4-5 keystrokes to copy and paste. It always asks if I want to have the code stay there for 14 days but that is no longer an option. 
Can’t message physician unless it’s about something they questioned and need a reply back. End up going to customer call center which is a bigger headache. Not sure which moron came up with that idea.

title: Wellness site
body: This site provides an online service for all Aurora doctors and facilities that are needed for my health care. Thank you, report availability is at one point and no need for printed media to be wasted.

title: Too many texts
body: Bothersome

title: Good when it works
body: I’ve been using the app for a while and I’ve noticed that 90% of the time I have issues with the app. I have been able to schedule so many appointments online and it’s really convenient as someone who works at night to be able to use the app to schedule because I can’t call during the day when I’m asleep. The main issue is half the time that I try and book online it says there’s no availability whatsoever. I believe that the system goes down at the times that I’m Realize that it is pretty consistent, but is something I’d like to be addressed. Otherwise great app four out of five star.

title: LiveWell App
body: I have been using the app on my phone for several years. I love that I can see my upcoming appointments and do the check in before I arrive. I also like being able to see the results if testing and compare to previous tests. All my medications are listed so when I need to show someone a list it is right there.

title: Dr. E Hartl M.D.
body: Excellent service from Dr. Hartl.  She’s very attentive and thorough. She listened to my concerns and addressed each one in a way that made me feel heard. I can’t say enough good things about her.  Staff is also professional.

title: Extremely frustrating app
body: I would like to score this higher since I am able to access my medical charts and text results however, when you are trying to set up an appointment with a new provider, the specialties they offer is extremely limited, even with a referral. You would assume that if you receive a referral, you should be able to click on the new provider’s name, that should take you to a scheduling screen. This is not the case at all.  The app is not designed to be user-friendly and seems to be a very early version.

title: Enjoy the app
body: I enjoy the app and the info available.

title: Survey about my Doctor’s visit -
body:
I’m very pleased with Dr. Kathrine Cross. She was very helpful kind pleasant and understand’s my medical conditions. 

I would like for her superiors to be informed of her exceptional care. And I would appreciate you telling her about my review of her services.

title: Entering email address

title: Logging out

title: Great App

title: Notifications

title: Great for info
body: I really like the app because you can check on appointments, make an appointment, have your meds and their dosage all in one spot (as long as you update!) and you can message your doc (non urgent question or statement). It’s very handy.

title: Not a gorgeous app but it gets the job done.
body: I don’t like being judgy but here goes: the LiveWell app *feels* clunky and it ain’t real pretty. So not fun or interesting or engaging to use at all in my opinion HOWEVER I can get everything done I need done which is the most important thing. Message a doctor. Fill a script. Pay a bill. I’ve tried looking through recipes and other add-on type features in the app (health “tips”) and frankly I am not interested. Wait. I did find one recipe for a chicken chili with corn and cilantro a couple years ago that I still make here and there because it is yummy.

title: I just love the app

Also find it very annoying that after you have read one portion of the checkout information and responded to that no more seems to be there to finish so I won’t be using this again. Thanks.

The appointment august 14th. I could not understand lady’s lingo, must be Chicago? I said forget this unpleasant appt request. I’ll call Froedtert. Whoever choose this phone system should be fired!

But can't remember my login as each provider makes you use different usernames and passwords. Tried resetting and not getting any emails:( It's unfortunately for comparing old lab work to new lab work.

title: Update is frustrating
body: Trying to message my primary care provider. I can’t figure out how to message her. Not one of the options in the drop down screen. Don’t like update. For seniors it would be nice if we could navigate the app easier.

title: Personal Life Changer
body: I enjoy using the LiveWell app, because it has all of my medical info at the touch of a button, I’m able to make  appointments, cancel appointments, pay any fees due, and get quick results for doctor visits, also you can communicate with Doctor or Nurse, directly in the app

title: Cut off my message
body: I use this app a lot and usually find it quite helpful. Today I was replying to a message from my doctor. It was a long reply, but the app said I still had 300 words left on the limit. Then it cut my message a lot when I sent it. App said it didn’t save the draft and it was too much to retype on my phone, so my doctor didn’t get all the info I wanted to send. :-(

title: Takes too long to sign into account
body: It’s frustrating to have to be sent a code every time I use LiveWell. I’ve requested that my devices be remembered, but it never happens.

title: Scheduling
body: The ‘appointment’, scheduling tool is horrendous, unless you want a virtual visit, which is only for minor health issues. Everyone else has to call in. It’s a backward step for a mobile application. Your update is more like a downgrade.

title: Can’t leave well enough alone.
body: It was great before and useless now

title: New format
body: The new format is less than optimal, especially for those of us that have been using the app for a long time. In particular, I find it unusual and very frustrating that upcoming appointments are no longer showing on the main page. I’m getting tired of having to search for standard features again and again because of format changes. At a minimum, please put the upcoming appointments back on the  homepage.

title: Happy patient
body: Love the app it makes accessing information fast and easy. Also the ease of scheduling appointments and messaging my doctor is fast and convenient.

title: Room for improvement
body: The prescription section could be improved with the ability to turn on-off automatic refill. Would like better setting for notifications. I would like them for appointments but not prescriptions being mailed, but they are either on or off. Frustrating. It’s really not that user friendly.

Dangerously incompetent 
Since being compelled to use mail order for prescriptions my anti depressant medication has been not refilled / then refilled/ and currently comes up as not refillable. 3 day weekend

title: Excellent app

Speaking from my experience with the group as a whole. I could not, would’ve never thought for it to have been any better than the most courteous, well preformed  treatment days that I received. 
The staff “HATS OFF” both kind and professional people, more than just workers. 

Thank you all for all your hard and dedicated support..

Lolita Marshall

title: I deleted it
body: Read the new privacy update. They require access to your location, Access to your microphone and photos. I would reconsider it if could have access to the doctors, and staff location and other personal info.

title: Great website!

title: I love it

title: abc

title: Staff

At this point I’m not satisfied with the level of care that I get from the staff mainly the doctors. I really want to leave. I think I might go back in to that hospital/doctor’s office.

title: Enjoy ability to access my Info any time
body: Significant positive difference to have the ability to read results and communicate with providers online.  System is efficient, immediate updates and last minute information is always available; Anytime, Anywhere.

title: Documents
body: I just got a message that I can’t see.  It’s too small and I can’t make it bigger. Such a waste of time.

title: Locked out of account
body: Purchased a new iPhone and got locked out of my account. Although I supplied the correct password, the two forms of authentication requires I use a number sent through text message. Since I no longer have the phone number I created my account with, I have no access into getting into the app. Typically your email address is proof enough for two forms of authentication, but not for this app. It is very hard to get into your account even with proof of email. I reached out to customer support through email and the only option is to talk to customer support their support phone line. This has been miserable as I requested a call back twice this past week when calling and never got a call back to my current phone number. Today I waited over an hour on hold and received no call back. The customer support to get into your account is horrible and the support team is helpless through email. Logging in with email/pass word should be enough. My bank accounts are easier to get back into if you’re locked out than to check on my blood work through this app.

title: New format
body: I really found the old format to be more user friendly. I really don’t like the new format.

title: Convenient
body:
Dr. Shah and staff do their best to make sure you get your needs met. They are nonjudgmental and accommodating and they make sure you get your needs met. 




I’m

title: Kim Savage Review
body: My Healthcare Team is Amazing and Prompt!!!!

title: Making an appointment
body: The app is supposed to be easy and friendly to use.  I’m supposed to be able to make an appointment on the app, I should be able to pick the day, that works for me and the available times should come up and let me pick what time is best, put my name & phone number in and the appointment is made. Done.  The app lets you chose a day and either am or pm, then it goes to someone else to decide what time.   What if the time  you chose for me isn’t convenient?  This process isn’t very customer friendly and it take the choice out of our hands.

Live well

title: I absolutely love this love App!!!❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️
body: I love this app so much. What did people in the old days do without this app?  I can message my doctor.  My doctor can message me back.  Does that makes it so you don’t have to go to the doctor so often your doctor can just message you a treatment.  And when you do actually have to go with the doctor, it will give you directions to the doctors office. Both on my phone and because my Apple Watch is linked with my phone, it appears on my watch and vibrates my wrist. If I wasn’t driving, I think the directions actually show up on my watch too, but I couldn’t look because I was driving.  This app is amazing.  You can read doctors notes about your appointment. You could find a doctor if you really needed to.  You can make an appointment you can cancel Appointment. I canceled the rehab appointment once because I couldn’t sleep and was like too sick to go with the next day it was 10 o’clock at night and I simply hit cancel appointment. Then try to get to sleep. Because I have an iPhone, I can sign in with Face ID.  Also, this app has pre-check in I got to the doctors office and there was like six people waiting to check in. Then I sort of skipped the line by using pre-checking in. This is the best app ever invented for patients and doctors.  I would recommend all Aurora patients get this app.  I would rate this app five stars.  I would even give it a six star for a good magic. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

title: Too difficult to navigate
body: This app is far too difficult to navigate.  Very difficult to know where one might go to find the info they’re looking for.  Having a “Live Well” app and a “My Chart” app together doesn’t help.  I can’t imagine how an 80+ year old would ever be expected to use this.  Really too bad at how little the developer of this software cares about the usability of their products.  My suggestion… fire the software company, find one that understands GUI’s and try again.   On the brighter side… they certainly make the payment section easy to find and unambiguous.     To the point (and incorrectly) implies you need to PAY NOW!   Which may be the point of the app anyway.  In which case.  5 stars all around!  Maybe make the rest of the app as good as the bill collecting part.

title: The good news and the bad news
body:
This app is an interesting tool to help to me manage my health. 
However, in my experience, receiving test results via this application is a mixed bag. The good news is turnaround time between the test and getting the result is minimal. However, many times the results of the test are confusing, conflicting, or difficult to understand without the knowledge and expertise offered by a caregiver in person or on the phone to provide context and implications. The results can muddy the waters more than provide clarity and that can be troubling. A possible solution to the difficulty in understanding test results, would be to provide a clear and easily accessible statement of what is normal and then clearly identify the category that the test results fall in: normal, almost normal, nowhere near no normal, etc. However, I am extraordinarily grateful for the addition of meditations - a wonderful tool. I have used them in the past with great success, and I’m delighted to find them so easily accessible on LiveWell. Great idea. KN

title: Byzantine, terrible app
body:
Providers use messages through app as written communications for legal reasons so that they can reschedule your appointments without patients direct confirmation, and avoid culpability. Upon any complaint, (ie - seeing your on vacation provider’s staff reschedule to 23 days sooner to a weekday 8 am) your ability to reply can be turned off (??) making it so that I the appointment I just said doesn’t work for me is now set in stone??

I’m not sure I have the energy to be frustrated anymore, but I do wish regulators would investigate this app.

title: Great app! Terrible hospital system
body: Just stay away from Aurora doctors! I promise you, they do not care one bit about you. They must be coached to get in and out of appointments as fast as possible. Every Aurora doctor I have seen comes flying into the office, sits down, asks me to tell them what’s going on, gives me room for one sentence, cuts in, diagnoses without any kind of exam or test, prescribes something, and sends me on my way. Every single visit with every single doctor. If they do end up wanting a sample or something then make sure you are on top of reaching out once the results are in. They won’t remember to go back and review the results. You will end up suffering for days waiting on them to finally get back to you. When they do, do not expect any kind of apology or sympathy. They are taught not to admit fault. You will be told, even if you have the results in front of you and can read them clear as day, that the results really didn’t show much but if you are still having symptoms the doctor will prescribe something. LIARS!!! I LOATHE liars!!! They will absolutely lie to you. I have caught their doctors in lies multiple times. The Aurora doctors are not to be trusted! Please switch to another system. These people almost killed me. I’m not exaggerating. Please.

title: This is a mess
body:
First issue: I got a letter from my doctor regarding a prescription and how to take the medication. It was shown on my home page. I opened it but did not have time to read it. So I closed it assuming I would be able to go back into the app, find it, and read it. It is nowhere to be found. I have checked every single place in the my chart. It is not in messages. It is not in the billing, appointments or anywhere that I can find. I have no idea where to go to find it. That’s the first issue. 

Second issue: The doctor who prescribed the medication is a new physician to me. And the medication prescribed is too expensive. So I thought I would send a message to his office to see about getting a new prescription. When I went to the messaging link on my chart, there is no way I can send a message to this doctor. It only lets me respond to messages already received, and this is a new doctor. So apparently I have to call my doctor to get any help with this, when a message would be far easier for me and for my doctor. 

Final Issue: where is there a place to ask questions for this app. No place in the app is there anywhere to ask about where to find anything on the app. (See issue #1) What do I have to do, call my physician? (See issue #2)

Honestly, navigating on Live Well is a nightmare. It is making me consider just changing doctors and the Aurora system.

title: How to report a bug?
body:
For outstanding Bills, there is a link to “contact cx support”. It opens a form to type your issue. If you leave the app (for instance to copy some critical note to paste into support form) and return, what you already typed is lost.
No draft is saved. You tap “customer support” in the same bill and have to start all over. Everything you already typed is lost. This might be a HIPA thing, but the app should auto save a draft. Hugely frustrating to have to recompose everything you already typed.
And then I can find no link for “bug reports” for the apps. So I’m leaving it here. I am using the iOS version on iOS 17.

title: BAD Visits filter between iPad & iPhone apps.
body:
The iPhone app recently seems to only display “Appointment” & “Office Visit” type cards for “Future” and “Past”, BUT NOT for other appointment & visits types like “Telephone”, “Lab Services”, or “Nurse Only”. 

THEREFORE, on the iPhone app, I cannot view “Notes” and “After Visit Summary®” for those— which is CRUCIAL. There is no user-controlled “filter” to include these types in the view, as far as I can find. And a menu search does not reveal other ways to dive deep in the hierarchy.

Second reason to rate low star review is that there is NO direct app/developer support to report bugs beside this apple store review path. Bad CX, team.

Hope you’ll listen to this and others’ experiences here.

title: Dysfunctional Notifications and Customer Service
body:
Notifications now take 11 hours to appear after labs are completed when it used to take 5 to 10 minutes.  I then messaged customer service per below and got a canned reply that didn’t even address the problem.

“ Topic: Technical Support.
 
Yesterday at 2:24 PM I had lab work performed at Aurora in Summit.  Although the results were available approximately 15 minutes after that, I never received email notifications that they were ready until 1:12 and 1:27 AM today.  Those notifications used to show up within minutes and now it takes around 11 hours.  
 
This delay started in the past 2 weeks or so.  Can this please corrected?  
 
Thank you.”

THEIR REPLY

“ Hello
 
Thank you for contacting LiveWell Support.
 
 
You may choose to 'Opt Out' of some types of email notifications from LiveWell.  
 
Log into your LiveWell account.
From your computer, select the 'Menu' drop down.  Or from the LiveWell mobile app on your mobile device, select the Plus Sign – MyChart icon at the bottom of your screen.
Search for and select Communication Preferences
Each category tab can be expanded to show options. A blue envelope or message icon signifies that it is turned on, and you will receive Notifications on that topic in your preferred method. A white envelope signifies that it is turned off, and you will no longer receive LiveWell Notifications on that topic. 
 
NOTE: Envelopes with a padlock signify which notifications are locked, and therefore you are not able to opt out. 
______________________________________________________________________
Below are the instructions to send a message to your clinician:  
 
Log into your LiveWell account.
From your computer, select the 'Menu' drop down.  Or from the LiveWell mobile app on your mobile device, select the Plus Sign – MyChart icon at the bottom of your screen.
Search for Message Center 
Select Ask about an existing medical concern in the pop out window. Important: If you select "Ask about a new medical concern" option is chosen, you will be directed to the scheduling screen to schedule an appointment.
Select appropriate answer for What type of medical question
Choose a Recipient from your Care Team list.
Enter a Subject.
Enter your message in the Message window.
You may attach an image if pertinent. Images or documents submitted should relate to you, or to the patient for whom you have proxy access and are sending the message on their behalf. 
Select Send.
 
Attaching Images:  You may attach up to five files. The allowed file types are BMP, JPEG, JPG, PDF, PNG, TIF, and TIFF files. The file types .doc is not allowed by the vendor (not our setting) as it is unsafe (data tampering). Message size maximum is 4MB.
 
Important:  Any images or documents you submit will become part of your permanent medical record. When attaching images or documents: make sure images illustrate your medical concern only. Do not submit sensitive images. Do not submit family photos, photos of anyone other than the patient, or photos not related to the patient's medical care. Do not submit images for your dermatologist unless they have requested you do so as part of your follow-up care or for reassessment for an ongoing concern.
 
Note:  Patients cannot message NEW clinicians until 15 days before their scheduled New Patient Visit with that clinician. 
 
LiveWell Support”

SINGLE. title: Transforming Time Management with Color, Clarity, and Compassion
body:
As a mental health coach, staying organized and engaged is essential for supporting my clients and managing my online presence. Structured has been a game-changer for me! The vibrant colors make planning enjoyable and keep me motivated throughout the day. I love how easy it is to track my time and daily activities, which helps me stay on top of client sessions, content creation, and online engagement.

I also love recommending Structured to clients with ADD/ADHD—the colorful, visual layout helps them stay engaged and better understand how their time is “structured”😉. One of the most valuable aspects is how it allows both me and my clients to realistically assess our schedules. By visually mapping out our days, we can identify when we might be setting unrealistic expectations for ourselves and make adjustments to ensure our plans are actually achievable in today’s fast-paced world.

I’m especially excited to explore the AI features to further streamline my workflow. Highly recommended for professionals and anyone looking to boost productivity, maintain a strong online presence, and approach time management with greater self-compassion and realism!

title: Habit tracking
body:
I really enjoy using this app. It has helped me learn and stay consistent, and the experience is smooth and motivating. However, I would love to see a habit tracking feature added in the future.

A habit tracker would help users monitor their daily progress, stay consistent, and build long-term routines. For example, tracking how many days in a row I practice, setting daily goals, and seeing progress statistics would be extremely helpful and motivating.

This feature would make the app even more powerful and useful for users who want to improve consistently. I hope you consider adding habit tracking in a future update. Thank you for your great work!

title: What Happened to Wakeup/Bedtime?!
body:
I love this app - I’ve been using it for about a year now. It’s so helpful for me to actually see how much time I have available throughout my day - I tend to overcommit myself sometimes but this layout helps make the amount of time I have visual. Recently, I opened the app and my wake up time and bed times completely disappeared. I’m not a paid user, but I’ve never had to pay for this feature before. Now I have to manually put in the time I wake up and go to sleep each day which is pretty inconvenient. Please help! I don’t know if this is a personal issue or a new feature, but it’s definitely taking the appeal of using the app away from me.

- I was able to reset the app in order to fix it! Thank you for the support, much appreciated :)

title: Best planner by far for ADHD brains
body:
I love the features of this planner. It is the closest to how I set up my bullet journal. Being able to replan tasks (and decide whether they are valuable anymore) or pop them to an inbox so I can clear them out of my metal clutter but not lose them entirely are excellent features.  I love the idea of “energy expenditure” as it forces me to really consider whether I will have the energy for something, not just the time.  If I run out of steam before finishing all my duties in a day, I can make a more realistic plan in the future, given energy constraints, rather than kicking myself for being “lazy.” So important for mental health AND for getting the work done. The UI is excellent.

I enter things into my Apple Calendar and then use Structured to check things off through the day, so I have noticed two items which might improve this further: I wish it could pull travel time from my Calendar, so that I don’t add tasks into space where I need to be driving; and it would be nice to be able to add an unexpected event or task (let’s say run to the grocery store for 30 mins) and have everything bump forward accordingly, rather than having to hand slide it to eliminate overlaps. If this were added, having some events able to be toggled as “locked” and un-bumpable would be important (for example, a class that happens at a certain time).

Otherwise, really outstanding planner.  Thoughtful updates and amazing attention to details that work well for ADHD brains!

title: Practical Support for Staying on Task

This app is designed to help people stay on task and focused, especially ADHD minds, and I’ve found it genuinely helpful for managing my TBI-related focus and organization challenges. Having my tasks, routines, and calendar in one place reduces mental overload and helps me follow through on what I need to do.

The integration with Apple Calendar is especially helpful because it lets me see my time realistically and plan tasks around actual commitments, instead of feeling scattered or behind. The clear structure and focus tools make it easier to stay present, break tasks into manageable steps, and complete them without feeling overwhelmed.

title: Best app I’ve ever used
body:
I’ve only been using this app 3 days and I already love it! 

I’m starting to up there in age and am finding my present digital calendar a little overwhelming. Don’t get me wrong I still like Google’s calendar. Especially the fact you can invite people to events. We use it as a family calendar. 

But…. For my personal use I wasn’t finding it all that useful anymore. There is just too much information on it. Especially when using on my phone. 

Structure is different. The format is perfect for phone use. I can scroll easily from day to day and I get reminders on what’s coming up. It sinks with my family Google calendar, so I can keep up with what everyone else is doing. 

The best feature for me is the mailbox. I’m keeping my grocery list there. I created a grocery task and am keeping it the mailbox. It just sits there off to the side and I add items as they come up. If you add them as sub tasks it essentially creates a checklist. When I’m ready to go shopping I just add it to that days tasks and can check off my items as I shop. My daughter thought it was the cleverest thing ever.

title: No copy button

title: Structured changed my life!
body:
I’ve been struggling with ADHD since childhood, and I’ve tried countless apps, systems, and productivity hacks to fix my planning and follow-through. Structured is easily the most helpful tool I’ve ever used. 

The AI feature is a game changer. It’s not 100% perfect but it’s so close and once you learn how to phrase things it becomes incredibly effective. When I’m busy or honestly just feeling stuck, I can basically ramble out what I need to get done or how my day has changed, and it can turn that into an organized plan with brakes. It genuinely starts to feel like having a real Assistant.

The syncing with my calendar and reminder is also extremely useful and saved me from double entering everything. I also love the energy level feature, it helps track how much effort different tasks take, which makes it easier to understand why some days still exhausting and how much I’ve actually accomplished.

That said, there are a couple areas that could improve. The documentation was hopeful, but it could be better organized, and easier to find, it didn’t seem like everything was available directly in the app, but instead on the website. Even on the website, it seemed disorganized and kind of hard to follow, which could’ve been solved with a better table of contents to feature maybe. I’ve also hit a few bug bugs like the Apple Watch app crashing when I tap certain buttons, even though notification still work reliably. There also been some strange error messages that appeared occasionally but nothing serious and I’m sure it’ll get corrected in the future.

Overall, even with a few rough edges, structured has made a bigger difference for me than any other app I’ve tried, and I genuinely recommend it, especially for anyone who struggles with planning, timelines, or executive function

title: Money Hungry
body: I was so excited to get my life on track so I downloaded this app and got extremely disappointed, you see, it looks adorable and it was incredibly motivating so i created a everyday morning and night schedule when I clicked to add it to everyday it needed me to pay for pro. The app is not “free” if you need to easily use it I can’t individually build a schedule EVERY single individual day. Do not recommend unless you got money to spend or time.

title: Stupid Paywalls
body: The fact that you can’t even have a repeating event without paying is absurd, when many free calendar apps have this integrated automatically. I don’t have a problem with the in app subscription to get some special perks, but to have a repeating event is such a core feature of any scheduling app and it should not be pay walled.

title: Great app - missing one feature
body: I’ve been a paid member of this app for about a year now and the subscription is completely worth it. The only thing that isn’t working for me is the focus timer. I tend to move through my scheduled tasks in order for the day, but sometimes I don’t get to them exactly in the timeframe I’ve scheduled them, and this means that I can’t use the focus timer. I’d really like to see this feature adjusted so you can click into a task and use the focus timer even if you’re working on the task outside of the timeframe it was originally scheduled in. This would make the app perfect for me.

title: REALLY Wish You Had These 2 Things!!!
body:
1. A way to organize and delete suggested tasks. Yes, I went on a generous number of dates with a person in this town. No, I do not want to be reminded of this terrible person with a task related to them when I am filling out my work schedule for the week. Others have said similar about being reminded of dead pets with suggested tasks related to them. I’m honestly shocked it isn’t possible to do this since it seems like an obvious human factor

2. PLEASE create a view mode similar to google calendar with visual BLOCKS of time, as well as the names of the tasks and the times scheduled for them!!! My ADHD just doesn’t get anything from circular icons dragging down my screen that forces me to look at it in another view mode I didn’t want in the first place just to see what the task even is. The google style would be absolutely amazing in month view, week view, and day view. Can’t say how many times I’ve heard other users comment on this lack of a good visual overview design. Seeing it in this way would make it so much easier for me to quickly see at a glance what I can fit into my schedule and what I can’t!

I love what this app has done for me so far (suggested tasks other than the mentioned issue is GREAT, the minimal yet colorful design is appealing) but until I see these 2 major improvements I will continue to look at alternatives.

title: I Am In Love
body: This is thee most profound program I have ever discovered in all of my life - truly a Holy Grail!  Thank you for your existence!

title: Ideal planning
body: The app does a great job allowing you to be as detailed as you want when creating each task. You can personalize each one of your “to dos” with its own color or icon, adjust when it should be repeated and how many times, add sub tasks, as well as write a note or description. You can visually see your week with all the tasks planned out, unscrambling everything you have in mind.

title: Overall Good
body: I’ve tried many todo apps and this one is definitely one of my favorite. I used it for a little bit and love the UI and layout customization! It’s so easy to make a task and the idea of the timeline is amazing. The AI feature is just like any todo app AI and works well. The only thing that I did not like is that you have to pay to make a task repeat?? I understand that the app has no ads and has a lot of features given for free but considering repeating tasks is a feature that’s free with your iPhones reminder app, it in my mind should be a given to not charge for this. Other than that, not bad!

title: Great tool for neurodivergent brains & different budgets finally
body: Was pleasantly surprised at al the features the app had but a little discouraged on the pricing for the Pro/ Lifetime which are the best. I can say though, that compared to other apps it is worth downloading giving it a try as it helps with organizing. As someone who does have a necessity for tools like these, paying for subscriptions on top of many other things to manage my condition can get expensive so I do wish there were codes or discounts… Or maybe hopefully the free version will improve with time to include more things. As the Free version can get tedious with repetition of writing the same tasks. But overall the app is very well thought out, fantastic how it links with several other things. I would recommend it to students, parents, etc, anyone who wants to get their life together! I am thrilled to see how they incorporate more AI updates into the platform in the future and love how they are open to suggestions and hear their customers/ users since they care about making a change. Thank you Structured Team!

a few suggestions/critiques areee

i rarely use the ai bc it's not very advanced

title: Perfect productivity app for students with ADHD
body:
Can we start with the fact that this app has a one time fee for lifetime pro? In the age of subscriptions, I am so happy to see an app that allow a one time fee. I have this app since March of 2022. The lifetime fee used to be less than $6! I’m so happy I gave it a chance back then, because it has been a part of my routine on and off since. The lifetime fee is $70 now, but it is worth it. It is constantly being updated with new features, even with AI to help you stay organized. 

It gives you a visual view of your daily schedule. The colors and calendars are customizable. You can linked your Apple reminders and calendar to Structured. It will sync across all devices from your phone to your iPad.

title: I wish I bought the lifetime membership when the app was new
body: I remember using this app a ton while in high school and loving it and after being out of it for a bit I felt like I needed to plan my days better rather than just trying to keep a vague idea of how long I’m going to set time aside for things in my head. So I remembered I used this app so I redownloaded it and immediately felt depressed with it. So much of the features I need are locked behind a payment that’s too expensive for me right now and I can’t just remake my schedule by hand every day like I used to when I was in high school and the app was new because I just don’t have the time or energy to do that anymore. I wanted to get the lifetime membership when the app was really new and only like $5 or something but my parents said it wasn’t worth it and wouldn’t let me get it. Is there a way to get it for that price again or is it just too late? This app is perfect for what I need right now to plan my day and other day planners in the AppStore don’t feel right but if I have to either deal with that huge price or huge inefficiency with the free version I’ll have to find another app.

title: So poorly made
body:
Used it for 15 min and uninstalling it. For some unclear reason developer decided to set up two default events/tasks that I are impossible to change or delete - “Rise and Shine” and “Wake Up!”
And when I marked “Wake Up!” As completed it immediately automatically created a duplicate of it. Marked that completed and it created another duplicate. If you tap on this task it opens a window to create a new task, not to edit the one you tapped on. 
All in all it seems most positive reviews are paid ones, no way such a poorly made app has such a high rating

title: The best routine app out there
body:
I’ve used Structured for about a year, and it’s a total game changer. I get distracted easily, so the reminders before each task are worth their weight in gold. I love seeing exactly how much time I have for a task—it keeps me from endlessly rescheduling. My life feels so much more organized, and I never forget anything because I add it right away. Only wish: a search feature for tasks and maybe a habit tracker to go with the period tracker. 10/10!
With the new update I’m not a fan of how many steps it takes to make a task, there should only be one window in total to make a task but more I have to go through 2.

title: Color Selection No Longer Works
body: When I first got, and paid for premium membership for, this app, the color selector was a standard HEX color picker where you could get any color you wanted. They've updated it, and now you can only pick these bleak, depressing colors. You can go through the trouble of looking up the hex code of what you want, but don't bother, because once you hit "confirm" on your task, the color changes for some reason. I brought this up on the app's forum: no response. Organizing tasks by colors helps my brain a lot, and it really disappoints me I can't customize it fully anymore. All the dull colors run together in my mind.

title: Surgery recovery simplified!
body: I’m a regular user of Structured. I’m currently unemployed and have ADHD and I’m giving Structured credit for me finally adding, wait for it, structure, to my mornings. However, it was immensely helpful during my current nasal surgery recovery. I was prescribed an hourly saline spray in addition to meds. Day one of recovery I wrote out my schedule in my planner, took a pic and uploaded so the AI feature could do the work of getting it into the app for me. Day 2, I was super groggy, so I just voice to texted what I needed including “meds after lunch” and “meds after dinner”. Structured’s AI appropriately placed those items even without being given a specific time. The hours are going by SO fast that I KNOW I would have only done the spray a handful of times if it wasn’t for Structured’s reminders. Highly highly highly recommend this app. I feel like there used to be a “snooze” option a few years ago. I’d definitely use that feature if it was available.

title: One little thing…
body: I love the app and the little symbols and color choices. I even purchased the subscription after the first day. One thing that bothers me as an organizer is the shift in tasks when they overlap. If tasks overlap they don’t get smaller or stack side by side (like Google Calendar). They just stack on top of each other which visually throws me off. I have to open the day individually to see which task I can start first before starting the other. If this gets fixed it’ll definitely be way more enjoyable to review and handle multiple tasks at once. As a stay at home wife and mother, it’ll be easier to review what I can take care of simultaneously without stopping to review the little details.

But now almost every time I touch the day planner it pulls down to display a  day visualization. Completely unnecessary. Extremely sensitive to touch. title: Exceptional app, but latest update wasn’t great
body:
I’ve used Structured Pro for years and it’s made a huge difference in my life. My child and I both have ADHD, and this app helps me stay organized and on track, and remember things. No other app ever helped this much. Unfortunately, with the new update it’s clunkier and less intuitive to add and edit tasks, and they got rid of the ability to set an alarm for the end of the task! That was one of the most useful features for me, since I use it as a focus timer all the time. It’s a frustrating change for no good reason.

Update: the changes are worse than I thought - not only can you not set an alert for the end of a task, you can’t even set one for the start of the task any more! The closest you can get is to set an alert for one minute before. And you can no longer set any alerts for events imported from the calendar! This is very disappointing - for someone with ADHD, knowing when something is scheduled for is a lot less helpful without consistent reminders!

title: Life changing
body:
I spent days researching various to-do list organizers, task managers, calendar apps, etc. and probably tested a half-dozen but none of them did what I needed in the way that Structured does.

Within 24 hours of downloading Structured I knew I had found The One and paid for a lifetime subscription.

Now I use it literally multiple times a day to manage my work and personal schedule, keep track of future to-dos, and generally run my life.

Data privacy is important to me and Structured is transparent and definitive about not selling my data. Being based in Germany and being bound by GDPR also helps with that.

This app won’t meet everyone’s needs but it certainly met mine.

title: Go back to your roots
body: When I first downloaded this app, it was the most efficient way to plan a day. I pressed the plus, typed the task, it defaulted to a time, and automatically placed it right behind each previous task. I was able to quickly schedule my day in the most efficient way possible. Then somewhere along the way, the app decided to think for me. When I typed the first letter of a task, it auto filled, and defaulted to previous name, and time, that I had used the day before, or past history. This change meant I had to look at the last task I wanted to do in the new schedule and figure out the time that’s supposed to go behind it, because it wouldn’t automatically place it behind the last task scheduled. This change negated the simplicity that I so loved. I emailed the developers to see if there was a way to turn off history and that’s a no. This last update: I thought that might have been fixed, so I downloaded again hoping. I was saddened to see that instead of opening to the simple schedule KISS, it not opens to a weekly (very busy screen), schedule. Finally found that the simple blank screen I loved is able to be reached by pulling up at the bottom. The simplicity of this planner was scheduling by the day and autogrouping as many tasks as I could fit in, in an 8 hour period. If you’re wanting a weekly planner, this may be what you’re looking for. However, it has passed its usefulness for me. When I first downloaded about a year ago, it was the best I had ever used as as a daily scheduler. As a long range scheduler, which seems to be the path the developers are taking; there are much better ones, in my opinion. I am sad this is the direction that the developers have chosen. Now, when adding a task, it more or less behaves as a full screen pop up covering the schedule, with no way to look at the schedule for the times already entered, making you choose a time rather than 15 minutes putting it directly behind the last task as it used to do. This is totally opposite of the direction I so loved. Developers: if you change it back to KISS as in the beginning, let me know and I will happily reinstall. Also, as another comment for the autofill, I tried uninstalling hoping it would clear the history, but it didn’t. There is no way I found to clear the history or to prevent the autofill, and this was confirmed by email by developers. This last update was not the direction I needed, and was my final decision to uninstall. Just doesn’t work for me anymore.

title: Amazing & Honest, Just Needs Two Things.
body:
I love this app. I have been using it for the past few months, and I am on the Yearly Plan, soon to hop to the lifetime because I absolutely love it. It is pretty simple to use, and I love that there are time bubbles, a fun feature I tried to find in MANY other apps but unable to. I also love how customizable the app is, down to the icon, because aesthetic for my tasks is weirdly important to me. While the free version isn’t the best, that is because they want to sell the app, not your information, which is where a lot of other “free” apps get their money. And I love this new update they added to the interface, it is very nice! It is also EXTREMELY mobile friendly, with fun and unique ways to make tasks, like a time wheel and personalized icons.

   There are only really two things I would like changed, though. The cycle tracker, for whatever reason, marks the period part as one day, so it messes with its synchronization, and there is no way for me to manually fix it. It would be nice if I could manually input data into the app so it could work from there. I would also LOVE if I could share my schedule with other people on the app, so they could see what I have going on, since you can already sync it with your email. But other than those two things this app is perfect for my needs and ENTIRELY worth the money.

title: Last Patch Update = Terrible
body: DEVS: please, please revert some of these recent task-related changes. Most recent update ~Sept 2025 is a horrific leap backwards. The UI layout isn’t just “different”, it’s strictly more tedious/annoying to do basic things like update tasks, especially when changing the time. There is so much more user friction and interference in daily use. It genuinely takes 3 separate clicks followed by a poorly optimized time-selector.

It used to all be one screen (change color, add name and amount of time, etc) and now I have to go through multiple different pages to edit a task. I don’t know that I will continue to use this app if it stays with the current format.

title: Perfect for the way my brain works
body: As someone who struggles with ADHD, depression, severe anxiety and panic disorder, having a place to “brain dump” all of the things on my to do list that are overwhelming me, and then be able to either schedule them for a specific date and time or just list them in an order that makes sense to me, is invaluable. Having the option to switch between a fully expanded timeline and 2 other more minimal views, in addition to having color aesthetic options and an interface that is nice to look at, with tons of options for the task icons, makes my neurodivergent brain happy. It has just enough stuff to mess with to make it look the way I like and be calming, but not so many settings to adjust that it’s overwhelming. I have tried every single planner app, ADHD ‘to do’ app, and list making app that I can find, and this one is the only one I have stayed with and feel like I have to have. Thank you Structured for helping me get my life together!

title: MUST HAVE APP!!!

title: Great App! But…
body: Structured is a really amazing app. It’s helped me get motivated and stay on task, and it has an adorable design and aesthetic. My work assignments used to take a full week for me to do, because I would get into a mindset that it was really hard and that I could only do one or two tasks a day. For the hours of free time I didn’t realize I had, I would end up scrolling on my phone. But when I got structured, I could see all of my free time and schedule things in it. It lowered my screen time average significantly, and now I get my work assignments done in two days! The only issue I have is the paywall. While I understand that they need money to fund their organization, it’s blocking features that are really essential to the app. Without the repeating feature, I have to make time every day to plan out the next day, so that I can schedule out every little task individually every single day. Also, not a huge issue but some of the icons that are locked behind the paywall would be very useful, as well as the AI feature. I’m sure that the app is AMAZING if you have pro, but I don’t want to spend money every month on a PLANNER app…. Also, sometimes the schedule can feel a bit cluttered when you’re planning it out, but that’s probably just me. Overall, I would recommend getting this app, especially if you’re willing to pay! ✅

title: Few more simple fixes
body:
Structured 
- The ability to re-organize "all day" tasks and set them in an order for one event or all future events
- The ability to edit the wake up time and have it push forward all the other tasks for the day. For Example, have an option for time adjustment according to when you tap your first item so that all times adjust to the new "start," time. for people who work on call this is important since some days I don't get to bed until super late so I can't necessarily get up at the same time every day.
- The ability to tap on "all day" tasks and have them "finish" like the scheduled tasks. unless they have subtasks; then show them finished when each sub task is completed
- have the sub tasks pop up when tapping to complete a task to confirm sub tasks are completed before completely completing the main task (rather than having to tap on the task in order to see/edit the sub tasks). Also add note subtask here...as explained in the next suggestion
- add a way to add and view notes (The ability to add a note daily to a scheduled item without having to edit it. I.e. set it as an option for a task to include a note upon completion. See "Habit".) Examples are "weigh in" then the note box pops up and I enter my pounds. "exercise" I love checking off the tasks, but I have a note box below my stationary bike so I can enter the mileage and the minutes. Or for other exercises I can enter reps. For "water my plants" I can enter which ones need special attention or which ones I didn't water because they seemed too wet. Any kind of detailed note to help.  
- A section where I can review notes
- Swipe options for tasks (ie swipe left to to to add to inbox OR TO SKIP a task skipping is important --because some days I miss or need to skip but don't want to delete because I want to review what's working and what's not). 
- An option to have the schedule as a task list only  that can be re-ordered if necessary (rather than a scheduled itinerary) for people like myself who work on call and can't get up at the same time every day. 
if these could be made an option, this would be the perfect app

UPDATE:
Developer responded, but the response did not address what I was suggesting. First, "Replan" just gives you an entire list and the only options you have are to throw it in the inbox, which does absolutely nothing or swipe the other direction to market as complete.
I am looking for an option that would allow my items to stay in the list with an option to swipe to just skip for the day. 
Secondly, I went in and tried the AI as suggested; again, not what I was looking for at all. 
I still had to go in and individually accept the new time on each, and every one of the scheduled tasks which defeats the purpose of what I am suggesting. 
I'm looking for the option to be able to tap on the first item for the day and according to whatever time it is, it would automatically shift everything forward.
Seems like it might be just a simple coding command. But I'm not a developer... 
Thank you anyway for your response; maybe someday.

title: SlanderedNanny
body:
I rarely ask my clients for a review for the app. However, I had 3 five star stellar reviews that were kindly posted by some of my previous clients.
The real saga begins in late May 2021. I was called by a cordial mom, given a phone interview about nannying her toddler right there, then asked to email my several positive references to her. I had all references on my phone (don't have a computer), mom called again next day and I explained there was a big glitch in the phone and I would have them emailed to her next day-pronto. And I did. I apologized again for the day delay. She seemed fine. Now, keep in mind, these are the same references that have gotten me several nannying positions. I then went for an in-person interview/orientation for the position which was starting in a week. The parents were great, their little boy and I hit it off, plus mom and dad were  going to be working from home which I'm used to and comfortable with.  We said our goodbyes and I left excited about the new position really looking forward to the summer..
Well, unfortunately and tragically on Saturday  my 94 yo father had three major strokes, was in critical condition. My 90 yo Mom is still at home with moderate sometimes severe dementia, and my 59 yo special needs brother also is at home.
I take care of people for a living. Yes.
 But, this was my family!!!!  They desperately needed my presence and my assistance. 
I rarely call in sick at my jobs.So, imagine how I felt calling her to cancel??? She was understandably upset and in a quandary. But, this was my father- dying. And my brother and Mom in need of my help. My place was with my family.
Unfortunately, the mom  was not only mad, but she must have felt vindictive too. She took the time and composed an exaggerated account of the timeline. And then????  She wrote  that she had called my references. She outright lied and defamed my reputation by saying that one family said that I had endangered their child's life several times. WHAT???? Never.
Then she concluded with the scathing remark that I shouldn't even be on Care.com because I'm a danger. This slanderous comment can't be refuted on your site yet,  I can't get ahold of a person at Care.com to get this damaging, defamation of my reputation  deleted. Oh, and to make things even better, two of my 5-star reviews are suddenly gone.Hmm..My attorney has affidavits from ALL my references.NO ONE made that horrible child endangerment claim. 
Please advise. Because my attorney will show you the affidavits and the missed job opportunities because of an unfounded, over-the-top, despicable review.
PS it wasn't a work review. I met her once. Spent 90 minutes with them. How can you let unsubstantiated slander just stand??

title: Just keeps getting worse
body:
I’ve been on this platform for about 13 years. Unfortunately, because of how long it’s been around, most families know about it and it’s therefore the most utilized. If I could get the same level of leads on any other platforms, I’d jump ship in a second.

It baffles me again and again how non-user-friendly this platform is. I have taken a lot of time and effort to carefully curate my profile, and accurately list all of my availability and specify exactly what I am seeking. Time again the preferences on my profile are changed, and I do not change them. Why would I ever change my profile to something that I don’t want? Time and time again this has led to families reaching out to me and us beginning a conversation, only to realize that our basic needs don’t line up because of misinformation they read on my profile. Similarly, I will apply to a position with a pay range in line with my desired rate, only to later learn in messaging with the family that the pay rate listed on the their end was inaccurate.

Further – and I have written about this directly to the company - they recently changed the platform so that the messaging service is only available through the app, and not through the desktop version of the website. This means that anyone who has a job interview through the Care.com platform must conduct their interview on a phone.

I can go on and on, but I deeply hope that people will start turning to other services such as Urban Sitter or Sittercity for their childcare needs. Care.com has gotten worse and worse, and now charge fees that seem very exploitative to childcare providers who already make very little money.

title: The App Itself: Terrible. The Service it Offers: 10 Stars
body:
I’m not sure how this app has such great rating. It seems the Tech Team keeps jumping from offering features on the website to the app and a lot of stuff gets lost in the fray. Currently, there is no way to change my hourly rate for one time sitting, which impacts my pay. There is no way for me to change my preset text, which makes editing a redundant and unnecessary step. The app has many glitches; every time I talk to a parent, it loads with information from other listings, which gets confusing. Then, despite my perfect 5 score rating, some hacker faked a review and I was lucky enough for them to delete it, but usually they won’t delete falsified reviews. All of these concerns just arose within the last year or so. Since they’ve transitioned from the website, which has its own array of problems, the user-friendliness has gone downhill. 

The app itself offers terrible glitches which makes it extremely difficult to use. 

Otherwise, the actual service that Care.com offers is extraordinary. I always feel safe connecting with families. I love the review system and the fact that I can advertise my services for a small annual fee. I love the safety precautions the Care Team makes to ensure our safety: pushing safety protocols, protecting us in our conversations, even providing articles full of advice: attire, signs of unsafe behavior, Do’s and Don’t’s. I always recommend Care.com to friends and families, alike.

title: This app charges an excessive 10% fee per booking

title: DON’T Waste your $$$
body:
I Cancelled DAYS before the 30-day period was over. 
I was told I would not be charged further . So you can imagine my surprise when a charge appeared days after I spoke with an agent to cancel my account. 
The agent  refused to refund fraudulent payment they took AFTER I called to cancel. 

Why did I cancel? 
Read every review about the horrible glitching in the app and all the features that simply don’t work. 

I wouldn’t wish this app on my worst enemy

title: Shady billing

title: Not there yet.
body:
People look for roles on the go (standing in line, in a waiting room, etc). 

Then, they come back and apply when they have free time & a clear head. 

This app gives you no way to favorite jobs or families once you’ve found what you’re looking for.  

You must search all over again, from scratch, every time. 

- 

This app gives you no way to discourage bad behavior. Job posters say “recurring job, 1 child, 0-11 months” and then you click the role and it’s a 1-time babysitting gig for a family with 4 kids. 🙄

It’s awesome that there are protections for the parents and families. What about for the caregivers?

-

2 stars instead of 1, because the distance toggle works very intuitively. High five to whoever did that.
