title: Worst customer services
body:
Care.com’s customer support is completely unreliable and misleading. I contacted them about a booking issue and was told after over a week of delay that the “client canceled,” which was simply false. I attempted to accept the booking within seconds of receiving it.

I was later informed that Care.com is removing the booking request option from the app, yet customer support failed to acknowledge this and instead closed my case with an incorrect explanation. There was clearly no real escalation, despite claims otherwise.

Slow responses, inaccurate information, and zero accountability. If you rely on this platform for work, be prepared for confusion and poor support when issues arise.

title: User interface for app is terrible…
body:
I just signed up recently so I haven’t been working yet, but this review is in regards to the user interface of the app. It is absolutely terrible… The jobs are listed horizontally instead of vertically, which is awkward. You have to click to expand the most important details, which are often for jobs that are completely irrelevant to what you are looking for… Why??? 

You cannot bookmark the jobs you’re considering applying to, there’s no feature for bookmarking so you have to apply or decline immediately! 

Most importantly jobs that are completely unrelated to my profile keep appearing like tutoring for languages I do not speak or areas that are incredibly far away. There’s also no way to sort through the different categories of what you offer so if you offer pet care, childcare and tutoring, they are all lumped in together like some big conveyer belt going horizontally at the top. 

They will also email you the most recent job listings, but when you click on the link of the listing that you are interested in from your email, it prompts you to open the app, then takes you to the App Store to open the app and then when the app opens, it does not lead you to the job listing that was listed in your email. Chaos!!! 

It’s a nightmare trying to navigate this app…

I had 2 bad experiences with families, offering me money in exchange of services and made me feel very uncomfortable and were very rude unfortunately I wasn’t able to leave a review on them to alert other Nannie’s of this family that later was back on care.com. PLEASE allow us to leave reviews on family and do background check on families looking for a caregivers not just the nanny’s.

title: Good but it could be much better
body:
The app is overall good because you can find one time and long term positions. However it lacks in:

-Reviews one-sided: you can only review workers, not families. And reviews are able even if you didn’t get the job. Just by messaging, which makes a lot of families with bad intentions to burn some workers profiles because they didn’t like the rate or something.

- If you accidentally apply or need to cancel a job, you can’t until the family answer you, which can cause misunderstanding and bad reviews later. You have limited access to families profiles/talk to them.

- Filtering families: even if you set the days you are available the app will give other days. Filters are really not accurate it always shows jobs that already past.

- Families also don’t use it 
properly because they put random days and time and then they get upset when you can’t the ones that they want on interviews

title: Disappointed
body: Extremely disappointed with the way they choose to do random checks and things. I should be asked to complete background checks prior to messaging any families, not after a month or so. I’ve now been blocked for days while in the process of this random background check and have been unable to message families with whom i have meetings with. I’ve essentially stood them up at no fault of my own, but all care.com’s. After multiple calls with different service reps, i got an overwhelming amount of different information from each. One said i was apart of a company-wide system error. Another said the issue would be resolved in less than 24 hours (it wasn’t). I’ve now stood up a few families because I have no way to message them back on the app. Do better. This isn’t fair to your paying customers. I’ve lost a lot of credibility as someone trying to build relationships with families. Fix your system, get your customer service on the same page.

title: The app is very poorly developed
body:
Using this app has been a headache. The interface works very poorly, it's slow, and often doesn't respond to commands. I've had multiple problems applying for jobs or accepting offers when I receive them. This is truly disappointing because you have to pay to get the app, and once you have it, you have to pay again to get the "pro" version. If you don't pay for this pro version, you basically won't get any job. I've been using the app for a while now, and it just makes me angry every time I use it. 
Every time I go to the live chat to ask for help, they just end up sending my case to technical support, and days later they respond saying they haven't found any fault with my app. It's clear that they don't invest anything in building the app and just keep all the money they make. So bad.

title: Getting scammed
body:
My name is Gloria, and I’m honestly really upset and disappointed with Care.com. Yes, the app has helped me find jobs, but I almost got scammed, and that’s something that shouldn’t happen on a platform like this. I was sent a check that turned out to be fake, and it was scary and stressful to deal with.

I pay $20 every single month because Care.com is supposed to be safe. That’s the whole reason people trust it. Getting scam checks from people on here is not right at all, and it made me feel unprotected and let down. Isn’t this app supposed to be good and secure for caregivers who are just trying to work and make a living?

Care.com needs to do better. There are real jobs on here, but the fact that scammers can reach paying members is unacceptable. When you’re paying for safety and trust, you shouldn’t have to worry about being scammed.

title: App fails if tracking is disabled
body:
Review:
I downloaded this app for the first time and was unable to use it at all. When iOS prompts for app tracking and I select “Ask App Not to Track,” the app gets stuck in a continuous login loop and never allows access.
There is no valid reason for basic login functionality to depend on cross-app tracking. An app should work regardless of whether users allow tracking outside of the app. This feels like a design or privacy issue that needs to be addressed.
The website works fine, but the app itself is unusable unless tracking is enabled, which should not be required. Very disappointing experience.

title: I like the service but not the app
body:
I’ve been using the care.com website for years, but now they force you to download the app. Main issues
1. Sort feature: the sort feature is broken when searching for jobs. If you select “newest” it shows them all out of order and you have to sift through hundreds of postings instead of seeing the recent ones
2. I wish there was a way to see approximate location. I know it says something like “5 miles from you” which is probably ok in suburbs. But I live in NYC and 5 miles could be in a different state, plus I have no idea if the client is located near public transport. Something that showed approx location (like the map on Airbnb) would be beneficial to users while still allowing privacy 
3. Editing profile is clunky on app

title: Buggy
body:
I’ve left a review before mentioning my annoyances with this app and it’s still the EXACT same as it’s been for years. 

This time I just wanted to add that I have to change my VPN to a USA based one. Fine, no big deal as this is a common issue. The app has so many ads and trackers though!! In two minutes, with no apps in the background and a fresh IP, my vpn has blocked 18 ads and 3 trackers.

This app sucks and it’s a shame that they’ve taken away so much from their website making this invasive and mostly useless app the only option. It never displays my profile picture, the notifications don’t work, they still show a saved drafted message that I can’t find a way to turn off or edit anymore (mines from two years ago) and the app just has many other laggy/buggy features.

I, like the many people I’ve met and worked for through Care, mostly just have complaints. Maybe one day there will be another option for us caregivers as well as those seeking out support.

title: Thoughts on the app
body:
This app needs some serious work. The “read receipts” are too hard to see. The blue check mark is barely perceptible and too tiny to see on a phone. It should be changed to say “read” when the message has been opened by the recipient. 
Also, when you go to the filter option on messages and press “unread” it says no messages even though I can see that there is a message that has not been read. 
Also I regularly see jobs where the start date has already passed. 
I know that the person who posts the job is responsible for closing it once they hire someone, but maybe a reminder should be sent to them to actually take the job listing down. 
Some of the job listings will show a start date with the incorrect day of the week highlighted, I’m not sure if this is a user error but there should be a way to correct it before they post the listing. 
Also, the job posters should be encouraged to write more descriptive details about the job they want to hire you for. 
The stock descriptions of “cleaner should bring their own supplies” is not helpful. 
I’m very disappointed in the app especially because I pay for the premium account and I do not think it is worth the money.

title: Unfair Review Due to Client’s Scheduling Mistake
body:
I faced a challenge where a client got confused about the interview date—he thought it was on Saturday instead of Sunday. As a result, he got upset and left a negative review on my profile. I called him to clarify the misunderstanding, but he responded by threatening me.

I reached out to Care.com and provided all the evidence, but unfortunately, they said they couldn’t help. Instead, they told me to contact the client directly to ask them to remove the review.

It’s disappointing that the app doesn’t protect the rights of caregivers, even though we pay for background checks every year.

title: So glitchy/full of flaws
body: This app is such a mess. First, I can’t open it from the app icon, it just gets stuck with three dots bouncing. I have to go to the website and click “open in app”- THEN it will open the app. Sometimes it will correctly sort jobs by distance or date posted, sometimes it won’t. It’s a crapshoot. Often, when I click on a job, it will take me to that listing, but have some or all info from a job I previously clicked on- creating these weird Frankenstein monster posts where the title and days don’t line up with the description and none of it lines up with what I tried to click on. I go days without getting any messages, then suddenly get several, leading me to believe some of them were stuck in some kind of purgatory. I had one family track me down through facebook cuz their messages weren’t getting to me. I was also just charged for Premium, when I decidedly DID NOT sign up for premium. I’m so close to starting my own app, cuz anything has to be better than this…

title: Worst Platform to work if you want to be PAID
body:
Have waited for pay for 3+ weeks, still no deposit in my bank account. Several emails and endless chats to ‘customer service’ provide no solution or support. When I called, service was actually WORSE. Didn’t think it was possible, but the gentleman asked me what I didn’t understand, after insisting that my bank declined a deposit IN to my checking account. Yeah, Chase Bank doesn’t accept money in anymore….. So frustrating to experience this during hard financial times. 
Bottom line- If you are a care provider, Care.com does NOT care for you.

title: Waste of time and money
body: Honestly, I had such high hopes for this company as they are the biggest caregiver job finder. But the only client I ever got booked with was someone who was a scam. The job posting for this person said they had a three year old and lived locally. The description said they needed a sitter for the said three year old. Turns out the address that was on the app for this “client” did not exist and the person I was messaging back and forth were caught on many fibs. There was also no profile picture for this person until after I had canceled the job and blocked them. And the profile picture they added did not match the description of the children I was told about. The app also has many glitches that made it difficult to apply for multiple types of jobs (pet sitting, childcare, etc.) and here recently when I redownloaded the app, I never reactivated the subscription, but woke up one morning with an email from care.com saying they tried to bill me. But my app said I was a guest… this app is fishy to me and I don’t like the lack of protection it provides for caregivers. I believe every client should have a profile picture and should give more information to confirm their identities. And honestly, this app is a huge money grab and you’d be lucky to get more than one job unless you live in a busy area. Good luck!

title: Dysfunctional app
body: This is app, if it manages to load when you open, is near impossible to navigate because it glitches and/or times out with almost every function. You can decline the same job 10+ times in the same session - it just keeps reloading declined jobs every time you return to the home screen. It took me five attempts to get the app to sync with the plan I paid for on their website and I was stuck in an endless verification loop. The layout is not intuitive and a lot of the jobs seem to be for maintenance companies instead of individuals or families looking for help. I signed up for a subscription and I’ll finish the month but I’ll likely cancel before the next billing cycle because I do not trust the app’s reliability to any degree at this point. Yikes.

title: Can’t unlink a portal
body:
I don’t need to stay connected forever to an urgent care in another state. Just unlink it, right? I can’t figure out how. Contact support, right? I can’t do that either. The app support goes to the website and there’s no phone number or email ability to contact these people as a patient. It’s not managing my health care if I am unable to stop sharing and unlink.

UPDATE: I received a response directing me to a feedback intake form. The the link actually took me to a survey that advised me I wouldn’t get any response from providing information. The response directing me to this survey already acknowledged my issue with unlinking a connected Urgent Care from my account. Why bother responding to me without providing instructions and ask me to fill out a surgery that will yield no response? Make any of this make sense.

title: What privacy?
body:
App shows no sign that the provider has any interest in patient privacy. For example, why is my name and birth date included _everywhere_?

Upcoming appointments?  First information listed is my name and birthdate. I know that;  I have to read down 4 lines to find what it should be telling me; when is my next appointment. 

Library of links to educational materials?   EVERY LINK has my name and birthdate. 

The equivalent web page does not have these flaws.

title: Simply fantastic Work
body:
Hi, my name is JOREL I am a 47 year young blind person. I have been going blind since birth and was diagnosed at the age of eight. And have never let this limitation. Overcome my goals. Although. Without the certain low vision aids or technology that I did not have while growing up. I have never been able to read File manage my own personal documents This day now is a milestone With the help that Apple provides with their accessibility settings and now the accessibility team that works building the platform at Athena healt I have been given the option andh freedom with a sense of an empowerment and independence i’ve always been a strong learner, dedicated and driven to overcome my visual impairment but when this happens, not just, I but confidently speaking for the whole of the world as it would be coming from a blind or visually impaired person this very software used at Athena health is groundbreaking it gives a sense of hope to those that may have none a link where the chain was broken and independence absolutely words cannot express the gratitude I feel knowing that this is just the beginning for accessibility and a new beginning for those who may have lost their way thank you so much for making my life a lot easier
Sincerely
JOREL S

body: My account page asked me to verify my phone number by choosing if it’s a cel phone or landline from a drop down menu tab but that tab is frozen and page freezes each time I’m on it so I have to force quit and log back in. title: Toland Sr
body:
I would gladly give The App a ten-star review, it's effortless to navigate  . However I would not give my Clinic the same. When it comes to care they are the best, ( Ten Stars) however when it comes to utilization of all this app has to offer I give them 1 star. I really would appreciate it if they would manage their back office as well as they have managed my care for the last 10 yrs. The back office does as little as they are allowed to do by management, and when I say little that's being generous. If you have to depend on the billing department and/or the people tasked with providing info to the app to do their Jobs correctly, that's not happening. I've been going to this clinic for 10 years, so I feel I have a right to evaluate them. And no I'm not afraid of them retaliating against me for speaking out because it would be impossible for them to do their Job any worse. 
It's always baffled me that from the appointment setters to the techs ,nurses Doctor and all the care staff they are second to none, but even they know how bad it is but are afraid to confront them or assist you in doing so. However don't let this stop you from getting the best care in the world, just watch your account and quickly force the corrections on the spot don't take “ oh well fix it on the next statement because they never will.

title: My doctor & nurse
body: Hi, I just want to say how thankful I am that Dr.Laurie was brought in my life many many years ago. From the beginning of her first prescribing Suboxone. She has saved my life as well as my son’s life. I tell her all the time she needs to share her success in her patients lives and journeys. I don’t believe she truly understands how important she has been and still is to not just me but to most of her patients. She is the best doctor I have ever been with, and I missed her when she had to take time away, but again I called her up and in a bad shape due to life circumstances and she accepted me and took me in and I really couldn’t be where I am today w/o her. And same goes for Jess, she is very sweet and kind and they both actually listen to me, and always respond quickly. And I have watched my nurse work so hard, and herself in pain, no she doesn’t talk about it but I can see her hurting,with her back, this is what you want in your doctors and nurses, like if I could write a piece and out in a paper I so would because nowadays it’s rare to find doctors who have the time to care about our lives. I mean I get it, the world’s changed,doctors are having more and more patients,due to whatever is going on with our healthcare systems.

title: Bad coding-terrible app
body:
9/1 update: I have sent the requested details to the URL provided. I hope they are true to their word and will fix the payment portal issue. 

This is one of the worst healthcare apps ever created. Aside from the bleak and 80’s looking interface, the major issue is that I am unable to pay my provider bills on the payment portal using my only credit card, American Express. I have been trying for weeks to get someone to review the payment portal to re-code and fix the incorrect format for AmEx cards. No one knows what the other is doing and they blame each other. It’s a clown show and the payment portal is a joke. At least fix the payment portal to accept the American Express credit card! The AmEx card has 15 digits and a three digit security code. The portal I use only accepts a 16 digit card number and a 4 digit security code! What are these developers thinking?

title: One Word: Jennifer
body:
NP Jennifer is who I would refer ANYONE to. My wife and I both see her and while I had had my fair share of psychiatrists in my adult life, she hadn’t, but when I tell you her and Jennifer get along like best friends during their sessions as well as keep it completely real/professional while getting down to the knitty gritty, I’m not exaggerating. I love Jennifer, she knows her field and does her job so well, I’ve never left a session feeling like my feeling weren’t valid or that I was WAY over the top. 
Thank you Jennifer for all you do for my wife and I and hope we can continue to be your patients for a long time!

title: Not functional enough
body: Your website offers far more information through the Midi Health login compared to Athena. The Athena app is essentially just a basic portal with messaging capabilities to your provider, and that’s about it. It would be great if the Athena app included features like direct messaging for same-day responses from an assistant or customer care to answer general questions, prescription refill requests, a list of available and filled prescriptions, billing details, the ability to make payments and change providers if your not happy with your current provider.

title: WOW unbelievably
body:
I was at a point of giving up on pain relief and assumed nothing could be done till I tried Clearway Pain. I had been to other pain centers. I had a temp trial of a peripheral nerve simulator put in for pain, relief in my shoulder from a reverse shoulder replacement causing me pain. I noticed a difference within a day. It was like a miracle. My pain level was zero. Of course it’s the trial work. I’m going ahead with the implant. I can’t think the doctors and nurses at clear way enough for their help. I stick with you the whole way explain everything. You feel very comfortable. But the decision to go ahead with the implant is up to the patient. I am definitely going ahead with the implant and can’t wait to have permanent pain relief. I can’t express my gratitude. Honestly, it’s like a miracle after years of pain.
If you’re looking for relief of pain, I can’t suggest strong enough to check out clearway you will not be disappointed and maybe have in two years of suffering like me

title: Why I love my Health Care Team!
body: The wonderful staff here at HSI East Montgomery are absolutely amazing! I came to them a while ago with some health issues and concerns, I did not have health insurance at the time but I needed to see a health care professional. That is when I meet Adrianne Turner CRNP. But before I met her, I met the wonderful people on the phone. The professional and courteous staff blew me away. I honestly was not expecting the great support and encouragement and enthusiasm because I was a self pay patient. As time went on, I was able to acquire health care coverage and was given an opportunity to choose a health care team. WITHOUT HESITATION-I STAYED WITH ADRIANNE TURNER AND THE BEST STAFF I HAVE EVER ENCOUNTERED! They worked with me just as vigorously when I was a self pay patient and struggling. The choice was a no brainer. I truly love my health care team and would recommend them to any and everyone looking for the best team in town ❤️❤️

title: This app makes communication with my medical team a breeze!!!
body: AthenaPatient app is the app you didn’t know you’d love. This is much easier to use than the web version. It’s optimized for devices (the website isn’t optimized for PC). I’m using my old and true iPhone 12 Pro. And it works fantastic on here. Before I reach for the phone, I’m use the app. I only use my phone if they call me, virtual visits, or if it’s something paperwork related/trivial. Great job with this app AthenaPatient. I highly recommend it if your medical team offers it to you AND you use devices this is a wonderful match. If you prefer the holding times and going through customer service centers to speak with your medical team this isn’t the app for you. And yet, I’d still say if you have a device(s) disposal and try it. It’ll change your mind. God’s blessings on you all. May He grant you peace, strength, perseverance, and ease. Āmīn

title: Update to my initial review
body:
You responded with “Hello, thank you for sharing your experience. We require login to the app each time to protect your health information, similar to how a bank app functions when it logs you out when navigating away from the app. We'll share your feedback with the development team. - athenaPatient Team"

i believe you completely misunderstood my complaint. I understand how logins work and their purpose. I’m saying why does your app offer face id login and yet still ask for the normal login credentials in addition, AFTER I’ve already passed the Face ID challenge…?

body: I absolutely love this calendar. I have tried so many over the years and once I found this one I stopped looking. I love the I can set 5 reminder for one event. It has saved me from not only forgetting events but also helps me planning ahead. For example I can put in a person’s birthday and set up reminders in advance so I give myself enough time to buy a present, order the cake, book a reservation, etc. I love to color code so I appreciate having several colors available. I do wish that I could create specific colors using RGB but that is my only possible complaint. If looking to use daily without being super complicated, this one is great. Also truly appreciate seeing events on my Apple Watch and tablet too.

title: Love it

title: Well presented and easy to use
body: I have used apple mail for over two decades but was not satisfied with the week view just showing dots when I had something scheduled requiring me to switch back to day view and then scroll to see what I had for each time of day.  Calendars present at least some information for each event even in the Month view.  This saves me multiple “extra” steps many times a day.  It is also aesthetically pleasing and easy to use.  It does a lot MORE and I look forward to delving deeper into its capabilities.  Works flawlessly on my multi computers and devices (Mac Studio, iPad, iPhone).  I would definitely suggest checking it out.

title: Erasing

title: SO upset.
body: I have used this app for about a decade and loved it. This morning I woke up and my entire calendar - past, present, future - is GONE. All of my important appointments, events, work deadlines, birthdays, etc. I restarted/updated my phone, followed the IT support instructions several times, tried WiFi and cellular data to refresh the app, and it’s still completely blank. Beyond frustrating.

title: A Step Back for Long-Time Users
body:
I’ve been a loyal user of this calendar app for years, and I’m honestly very disappointed. Editing tasks used to be a free basic feature, but now it suddenly requires a paid subscription. I really don’t understand how a core function that was originally free can be moved behind a paywall.

It feels frustrating and unfair to long-time users who have supported this app for so long. Is this change meant to push existing users away? I truly can’t understand the decision. I hope the team will reconsider this update and think about the loyal users who helped the app grow.

title: Worth the money, convenient and efficient
body: I love this calendar and have been using the free version for years. When they started to charge, I continued to pay to use it because it works so well. I love that I can color code events, that the tasks are integrated into the calendar, and mostly that is syncs so beautifully with my Apple Calendar, with is back up continuously, using my iCloud. So, that if something happens, I have a back up that has incorporated everything I have in my calendar and task list. It’s so much nicer than just the Apple calendar, due to its task integration and color-coding which the Apple calendar lacks. Also, every time I’ve ever had a question or issue, Readdle gets back to me super fast and always has answer.

Very frustrating to deal with- as I’d make an edit and would not be able to remember the time adjustment I made. I’ve been trying to fix this schedule for the past 2 hours and keep running into the same issue…yet I’m supposed to be paying for this? Yeah ok. Would not recommend.

I’m probably forgetting something. It’s a great calendar I have had it for years, it’s totally worth every penny. Advanced calendaring user here with 10+ different calendars for work and home.  I’ve been looking for an app to replace the default calendars app for a long time.  I’ve used every other app and have been stymied by one feature or another.  Calendars has the best gesture system I’ve experienced.  So easy to swipe left and quickly show or hide calendars.   Easy to drag and drop appointments around.  Easy to swipe by day or week.  Loving all of that. 

My one nitpicky feature request is that I’m missing Travel Time from the default Apple calendar app.   Love that feature for getting to appointments on time.  Please add that for Calendar Perfection 😁🏆🚀

I’ve tried adding all my Gmail accounts and can’t recover any calendar items. This is devastating as I didn’t have them written down anywhere else. Be sure to record somewhere what account you use to sign in, otherwise years later you might lose your entire calendar and have to start back from scratch. I’m not sure why it worked all these years without me logging in, and now suddenly my stuff is gone! Such a weird thing and devastating.

title: What a disappointment!
body:
UPDATE:
Just got a response from the developer asking if I could send them details about the problem. Hilarious. What don't you understand about this review?!

ORIGINAL REVIEW:
I canceled my subscription immediately after discovering that, for some bizarre reason, they chose to use the old-fashioned scrolling wheel date picker instead of a quick and easy month view, like every other advanced calendar has. When you scroll the day number column, it's impossible to know what day of the week you're on until you stop scrolling and it shows at the top. Just see how long it takes you to find next Thursday, or the third Tuesday of the month. This is absolutely insane. It brings productivity to a screeching halt. A month view date picker lets you see all of these options at a glance and allows you to select what you want with a single tap. For heaven's sake, ditch, the stupid scrolling wheels!

Also, when entering a new task, you have to save the task first and then go back into it to edit recurring and other options. Again, a total waste of time.

Huge disappointment from Readdle, whose Spark email and Scanner Pro I own and use every day.

title: Switched away but came back
body:
A couple of years ago, I downloaded a handful of calendar programs and worked with them for about a week before settling on a different program than Calendars, with Calendars a close second.

Well sir, my first choice went from an "only pay for upgrades” pricing model to an annual subscription, costing $90/year for a family subscription to unlock the features I’ve been using since I started with it. I re-downloaded Calendars and WOW! Lots of upgrades and new features since I originally tried it out — and with 3 pricing models; Basic, Subscription and Lifetime. 

Rated 5 stars for the features I need and a user-friendly pricing model. Good luck to that other company, because Readdle leaves them in the dust.

title: Many syncing issues
body:
I have been using this calendar for years but it has been having new issues lately. 1) It inconsistently and randomly syncs events from my iTalki calendar, causing me to miss events. Even when I manually sync calendars, it randomly selects which ones to sync. 2) Also when I create events from within the app and select which email it should be associated with, it fails to update to that Google Calendar. All new events created in the app remain local. Very huge issue for people who need integration between platforms and apps. 

Today I was trying to resolve this syncing issue and saw an option in the Readdle Calendars menu to force sync. Unfortunately this just meant it deletes anything that it hasn’t successfully synced to associated calendars. I just lost about 200 calendar events. As a project manager, I don’t see myself using this app anymore even though it has the most beautiful interface. Completely unreliable.

I’m just so disappointed that I’ve got to find something else now. Because I refuse to pay for basic functions I can get for free elsewhere.

title: Filled with bugs that will never be fixed
body:
This app COULD be good. It’s a good idea, and it has quite a few potentially-useful features. Sadly, it’s really not good. It’s fille with bugs, there are numerous pointless and avoidable shortcomings, and many of its most unique features didn’t have the user experience fully thought-through.  First, there are numerous shortcomings. For example, anytime you write something that it THINKS is a location, it basically has to tag it as a location, and makes it very difficult to back out of. The word ‘at’ is unacceptable. 
Another example - you can’t add events and tag them as ‘not busy’ like all the underlying supported web calendars. Think you’re going to use this to manage a work/business calendar and still allow appointment scheduling? Think again - they refuse to support that.

But the worst part, is that it’s filled with bugs that will never be fixed, just like their Spark email app. 
Titles disappear when saving nearly every time — it’s even worse on the iOS app. Sometimes they’ll disappear right away when you remember and can try again to save, but sometimes they’ll disappear hours/days later… good luck trying to figure out what that random event was!
For whatever reason, syncing stops working for several days to a week every couple months, but it doesn’t TELL you that it’s stopped working…they just don’t show up. 

In all, there’s no possible way to recommend Readdle apps any longer. Their developers don’t work on making the apps work well, only adding new and useless features that likely also won’t work.


Update: As you can see, the developer responded. However any attempts to contact them via support channels lead to them absolving any responsibility for keeping the software operational, and insisting it’s the user’s fault, or just telling the user ‘yeah we don’t support that industry-standard functionality, and never will.’

title: Feedback on Masterclass Features – Habit Tracker & Personal Reflections
body:
I recently purchased the Masterclass with the habit tracker and personal reflections included, and I wanted to say that these are excellent additions. Thank you for providing such valuable tools.

I do have two suggestions that I believe could make these features even more effective:
	1.	Personal Reflections Across Calendars
Currently, the personal reflection option isn’t available for all events in my other calendars. Since I use different calendars for specific tasks, it would be very helpful if the reflection tracker could integrate across all of them.
	2.	Habit Tracker Widget Order
The widget items for the habit tracker don’t appear in the same order as they were added in the app. Because many of my habits are tied to specific times, maintaining the correct order is essential for tracking and usability.

Thank you for considering these suggestions, and I appreciate the continued improvements you’re making.

title: Sad It Went To Subscription
body:
It’s not very fair to give this 1 star because I do enjoy the app and have been using it for years. 

However, I refuse to support switching to a subscription model. I used the last free year I had, so a genuine thanks for that! 

I can get behind a one time cost like they had for the previous version, but with so many other daily life expenses and subscriptions, it’s unreasonable. 

If I were to use this app for the rest of my lifetime, and let’s say they actually keep the cost at $20.00 per year which is doubtful, that would be $1000 bucks I spend to use a calendar! Outrageous! 

In theory $20/year seems reasonable, extrapolated it feels incredibly silly when there are plenty of options I don’t need to pay annually for. 

So while the product has been lovely and useful, thanks but no thanks!

title: Be wary, bought original app and they keep wanting more
body:
To be fair is the best calendar app I have found. If you don’t mind the cost, then go for it. 

However, I bought the original app, and while they did grandfather me in to the new app for a while, I am very disappointed that they took away the “repeat” option and made it a “pro” feature. 

To get that basic functionality back, I have to subscribe or buy a “lifetime” package. The “lifetime” means they can add future features and those won’t be included. I’d normally be okay with that policy… except for the fact they I already feel swindled from paying for the original app and what I consider a “basic” feature is not included now. 

I get they need to make money to continue offering this great app, but I find it shady that a normal calendar ability like “repeat” is considered “pro” feature.

title: Powerful Calendar and Task App
body:
I was a long-time user of Fantastical, but gave it up because it was just way too expensive. I went back to the stock Calendars and Reminders app, but there were a few things about the native calendars app that bugged me, namely, an inability to use Google Maps with it, not being able to color-code appointments, and not being able to time block with tasks. 

Calendars by Readdle solves all of these issues for a very reasonable price. The interface is great, and I can now drag and drop my appointments and use a time-blocking approach. Calendars syncs perfectly with Apple Reminders, which is great because that is how I like to manage some tasks for shared lists. But I am finding for many tasks I can do everything right in the Calendars by Readdle app.

I am really glad to see that these developers have continued to do such a great job improving and developing this app. It functions like a really nice digital planner on my Mac and iPhone, and it has helped me better organize my days.

title: Taking away functionality
body:
27Jun2025 addendum: Changed to 5 stars. After extensive email conversations with Tech Support, this issue has been favorably resolved. My experience with Readdle customer support has always been superlative and this time was even more so given the circumstances of war in which they find themselves. 
***********
I've used this App and its predecessors for over a decade. My iPhone 7 is limited to iOS 15.8.3. CalendarsPro requires ≥ iOS16. Prior to the second week in June my app had the following functions available: location, rooms,attendees, video call and note. Now according to messages at the bottom of an appointment screen, I no longer have the attendee and video call functions without upgrading to Pro. But my iPhone7 cannot be upgraded to iOS16 and therefore cannot be upgraded to Pro. And apparently I'm also locked out of sharing appointments with my wife who has the same app and iPhone7. Sharing appointments is the only reason we have been using this Readdle App for a decade. Why did you TAKE AWAY functionality and require an upgrade for devices that cannot be upgraded?
*****************

body: I’ve always used this app. Why is everything all of a sudden gone?? All my appointments and reminders are gone

title: My opinion
body: I find this calendar a little difficult. I liked the older version much better it was much easier to schedule and review, set updates and reminders.

title: Love this app
body: I love being able to actually read what is on for the day when I open my calendar. Love the multi colors I can use to highlight activities.

title: You messed up
body: You had a good product. You’ve totally screwed it up.  Can’t get rid of the daily weather. Calendar screen too busy. Wants permission to every calendar in your phone. Yahoo’google, Microsoft etc.  without giving it total access, it’s useless. Not happy!!!

title: Long-time user!
body: I've been using their calendars for many years!  The initial reason I started was because I could see the appointments in month view — the native calendar doesn't allow that. But there are so many other things I love about it. This new version posts the weather on top of the week. Of course all my appointment purposes are color-coded - any color I choose; I can choose to have the week number posted; it keeps my to-do list under the Planner; and so many other awesome features. I'm a dedicated user because I truly like this app!

title: Constant beg for money
body: Just downloaded hoping to put a simple calendar separate from built in calendar to schedule days I volunteer. App constantly pops up adds to upgrade so can’t get a single task done. Got into settings and had to close app to get out of settings back to calendar. Too pesky to function.

title: Very glitchy and moves things on its own
body: This app feels frustratingly unstable, with glitches that disrupt the user experience in unpredictable ways. Whether it's elements shifting without input or settings mysteriously reverting, it creates a sense of unreliability that makes even simple tasks cumbersome. Instead of streamlining efficiency, it feels like a constant battle against the app's quirks.

title: One time purchase - Subscription Service
body: This is my favorite calendar app that I’ve been using for years (when the logo was yellow and was called Calendars5) I bought the app when it was one time purchase, but recently the app moved from a One time purchase to a subscription model. I lost my premium features of the one time purchase premium app when moved to a subscription model. Deleted the app :/

title: Can use without sharing calendars.
body: I want a separate calendar, can no longer do this as you cannot opt out of sharing at least one entire calendar.

title: Keeps me organized and on track
body: Love this app! love the ability to color code and have all my to do lists on it. Also love that I'm able to import my meal planning app with it

title: Great app
body: Great app!

title: Love this Calendar
body: I have been using this calendar for a few years now. Love all the features. I love being able to easily add things that I need to do daily, weekly and monthly. I like being able to color code things. I like the task feature and that I can check things off my list. I like the new features with the weather forecast for the current week. I look at weather to make my planning for the day.  I also like the new search feature if I am looking for an appointment. I like the fact that I can look at this on my iPad or my phone. I also can share events with my husband.  I was sure about going from a paper planner to a digital one but now I like having it at my finger tips.  One things I would maybe suggest is the option to add stickers to the calendar to make a day stick out on the month calendar.

title: How to pay by monthly
body: Every time I click on upgrade it only give you option to pay a whole year. There is a description saying only $1.83/month, but I can’t choose it. I want to pay monthly, but don’t know how.

title: Could We Have 2 Choices For Customizable Alerts?
body: Love the app. Works well. I would like the option of having two options for customizing alert times instead of just one.

title: Lost most of my calendar
body:
I “upgraded” to the new app, and for some reason it only transferred my repeating events, nothing else. The school year is about to start, and so much has been wiped away! I’ve been using this app for years, and this is so disappointing. Use at your own risk!
Edited to add: I tried again with the new version, and it was doing fine for a long time, but now it has events that I’ve created on my phone that aren’t syncing with my laptop. I love this app so much, but it’s worthless if it loses my info. :(

title: Modifications needed
body:
I thoroughly enjoy using this app to keep track of all my appointments and special dates.
I would also like to see a “search” feature so I can easily find events and people.
I would like to be able to print my calendar. If this feature already exists, I haven’t been able to find it.

title: Doing good
body: Better than other apps

title: An Outstanding App Backed by Exceptional Support
body:
The Calendars app by Readdle is hands down one of the best productivity tools I’ve ever used. It combines smart features, smooth design, and flawless sync options — making it a must-have for organizing your life.

But what truly sets Readdle apart is their amazing customer service. I reached out with a question and received a thoughtful, helpful response within just a few hours — not an automated reply, but a real solution.

Even more impressive is that this team operates from Ukraine, a country still enduring the hardships of war. Despite everything, they continue to deliver world-class support and maintain the quality of their products. That kind of dedication deserves recognition and gratitude.

Thank you, Readdle team — for your innovation, your kindness, and your resilience. You are an inspiration.

title: Couldn’t use
body: It would not let me log in on my computer. I changed my password to make sure I was correct but it would not let me use it. No matter what I did. It’s confusing to download and try to add stuff on the phone alone. So as someone with ADHD I hated this.

title: So far so good!
body:
Everything is great- I love the “List” function! 
Question though- how do I share with my husband so we can edit and write notes etc. ?

title: Easy to use!
body: The best!

title: The Best Calendar you can have
body: I love the fact that I can see my tasks in the same app and I have it on all of my 4 Apple products.I wish, I wish they would add “Traveltime”. Let’s say an appointment is at 9:30h, but I need to drive there and it would take 1 hour. (Apple has it, and I would love to see that feature as well).

title: Everything Was Erased
body: i’ve been using this app for the longest time now and i’ve never had any issues up until now. it’s simple and well organized which is why i choose it over others, but last night when i checked my events and birthdays on there, it was all deleted. good app besides this issue.

title: Calendar Planner
body: Perfect App!!!

title: Not user friendly for the ADHD’s
body: I really like you can color code events and activities however too many color options, not easy to click on date to add event. 7 day trial is not long enough to actually get a feel for the calendar. Not too satisfied with this purchase.

title: Hang on…
body: I have used and loved this app for YEARS (at least ten- definitely longer) and today I got an email that I need to choose my subscription plan because my free trial has ended?! I paid for the app. I did not have a free trial. Readdle, this feels sneaky. Don’t get me wrong- the $20 a year is worth it- but don’t gaslight me into thinking I had a free trial. I’ll be looking for something else.

title: Good Enough
body: I prefer the layout of this app to other digital planners I’ve tried, but there are a few things I wish could be incorporated. I wish events or tasks could be expanded with sub-tasks. For example, I’d like if my ‘night routine’ event could have a checklist of tasks within it (skincare ✔️, shower✔️, etc.) rather than a separate inbox of tasks. There is no way to relate any task to an event. There is a notes/description section, but it does not offer any formatting. I like my planner very organized, and this app feels a bit sloppy. Still the best option I’ve found, so hopefully some changes can be included in coming updates.

title: Calendar
body: I love using this calendar.  It has replaced my paper planner because I can add task lists (goodbye sticky notes) to remember all the things I need to do.  I can add all the meetings I need to go to and it sends reminders to me.  Plus it integrates with the calendar on my phone, so when I add a doctor’ appointment on my phone calendar, it automatically updates on my iPad.  It’s just a great app.

title: User for years, now ready to change
body: After years of using this app, finding nothing else that measures up, I’m almost ready to walk away. The basic functionality of typing “12:30p” or “12:30PM” or any version of that, and having the time of the event set to 12:30PM seems to now be a pro “feature,” and requires a subscription.  Honestly, that is such a rudimentary function, if I need to pay for that, I’m going elsewhere. I bought an earlier version of this app outright, and would do so again. But a subscription? No. No thank you.

title: Terrible UI redesign
body: Look. I get that Readdle wants to push more subscription services to us. But I can’t believe they took away the weekly scrolling from the bottom of the Daily view and instead replaced it with a menu touting their useless premium features. For shame!

title: Years deleted
body: i have been using this app for many years now. haven't purchased a thing. i loved it until a few days ago when i got in to check what appointments i had coming up for work and everything was gone. been running around in circles with the support team getting no answers. do not waste your time getting invested into this app if you depend on it for work or personal appointments.

title: reset my whole schedule
body: App usually works great, i’ve been using it for about a month. It was working fine up until about an hour ago. I opened the app and it completely reset EVERYTHING. I lost all my previous records & have to fully redo my schedule. Terrible

title: Love
body: I love this calendar

title: Help
body: I really loved this calendar until I just lost everything I put in it.  All my data is gone.  Help!!!

title: The best
body: This is the best calendar. I can make standing appts and 3week appts. It’s so versatile. I’ve had it for years. I love the color coding. Just the best!

title: Best calendar app
body: I have been using the Calendars apps by Readdle since the app was first developed. I have tried many calendar apps over the years and never found one that I liked better than this app. It has many features, a lot of flexibility, and is very clean and easy to use. I recently upgraded to the new Calendars app from the previous Calendars 5 app. The new app has many more features than Calendars 5. Not sure yet how many of them I’ll use, but I will never go to a different calendar app. This is simply the best! And their customer support is amazing!

title: Glitch
body: I love the new planner section, but sometimes it goes into a screen promoting an upgrade that won’t let you back into the main part of the app

title: Greedy scammers. Don’t waste your money on them.
body:
Sold Calendars 5, and then abandoned it without warning out of nothing but GREED.

These devs don’t fix bugs. Prove a bug to them with video, they say they’ll investigate it and then they do nothing. Years go by with bugs not being fixed.

They post platitudes in review responses saying how they’re committed to customer satisfaction, but the only thing they are committed to is GREED, taking away functionality to move it behind a subscription so that they can get more money every month for doing nothing.

Do not give these people a penny for a subscription.  I never will.

title: Holidays
body: Why do the holidays appear more than once on the calendar the same day? You can’t delete them.

title: Love it
body: I absolutely love the calendar, I love that I can be signed on to more than one device. Love how many notes I can add. Just wish I can add a picture onto a day, like if I had an invite to a party I would love to be able to click on the day and see the actual invitation.

title: Scammers
body: Paid for calendars 5 then they removed all of the functionality for this subscription based scam.

title: Fraudsters
body: I paid for the Pro version and now that’s canceled in favor of a subscription. Apple should ban developers who do this. Complete fraud.

title: Used to be great
body: When this first came out it was the best calendar I could find. But now they charge you for the stuff that was free in 2020 and is still free with the apple calendar that comes with the iPhone iOS.

title: Over time, this has become a great app!
body: I now use Calendars: Planner & Organizer on my Mac desktop, iPad, and iPhone. I especially like the shortcuts that I can insert into my calendar to time block any and all activities. Functionality of this calendar system has improved over time to the point where I don’t use any other (goodbye, Informant 5!) All my calendars (gmail, Exchange, iCal) feed into Calendars and bring my personal and business planning into one interface—extremely convenient! I know improvements will continue over time and hope a second week view option will be introduced to give another option besides the vertical column view currently in the app.

title: Calendar planner & organizer
body: Owesome calendar I enjoy everything about it!

title: deleted my entire work week
body:
I methodically entered and saved all the info for every gig I had scheduled this week, and then this app magically forgot all of it. There has been a massive downturn in User Interface on this app recently. I’m not sure what’s going on with the owners of this app, but I wouldn’t be surprised if it’s own by some greedy tech pig company who recently laid off staff, and now have a subpar product because of it. 

I actually work for a living, and I need my calendar to actually reflect the info I’ve programmed into it. It’s so convenient how this happens only a few days after my yearly subscription renews. You have my money, now fix your app.

title: The best
body: My only requests are: 1) a more simple black white app icon, 2? better alarm sounds 3) minimize calendars on menu settings screen! 4) same compact list view that's available when clicking on day from month...also be a regular view option

title: Have been using for many years.
body: Fantastic calendar app that continues to be made even better with each update. Easy to use and has a great look. Honestly should be listed as one of the best apps.

title: Best ever
body: I’ve been using this app for many years. I’ve never had any issues at all!  Couldn’t be happier with it!

title: Best Calendar
body: This is the best calendar of all the ones I’ve tried. What helps me most is the tasks, the ability to assign them dates and times, and having them show up on the schedule. It’s easy to organize, and it remembers recently used locations for ease in adding addresses.

title: Love it ❤️
body: Calendars is an amazing calendar app! I really love its design and how convenient it is to use. The latest update, with the timeline task planning feature, is fantastic. It’s great to see how the product is evolving visually!

title: Amazing
body: I’ve been using this app for several years and initially started with the free version before upgrading to the paid version. Both offer excellent functionality and user experience. Recently, after upgrading to a new phone, I encountered a bug likely related to a recent Apple update. I reached out to the support team, and they resolved the issue within the same week by releasing an update through the App Store. They also followed up with a direct email to inform me. Exceptional support and an outstanding app overall.

title: I love it but..
body: Overall this is a great app and the interface is not only easy to use but aesthetically pleasing. However, for school I use my IPad and nothing ever transfers over from my phone to my IPad on this app which is really disappointing and inefficient.

title: Love this app
body: I’ve been using this app for years to run my business. Recently I ran into an issue after a software update that made the app impossible to update. Customer service was very responsive, I do appreciate that so much! It took a week or so to fix but I honestly am glad I hung in there because after trying numerous other apps to attempt to run my business during the down time none compared to this one. Great app!

title: Cant have same calendar on iphone and mac
body: It is annoying that once i downloaded the app on my mac i cannot have same calendar as on my iPhone despite using exactly same iCloud

title: very happy longtime user
body: I’ve been using Calendars for about 10 years  and i will always recommend over the standard phone calendar. it’s so good

title: I can’t access anything!!!!!
body: Since the update I can’t access my calendar! It keeps saying something like “go to privacy, give all access”. I have looked everywhere and there is no way to enable this feature! SO FRUSTRATING!!!!!!

title: Update Eliminates Option
body: Recent update(s) make it impossible to create repeating entries without “pro” version. Existing repeating entry rendered useless. Searching for replacement app.

title: Excellent App!
body: I have been using this app for several years and it just keeps getting better! I love it!

title: Best calendar app
body: I use this app every day, it’s much better than the default iPhone calendar app.

title: Calendars after an update (March 2025)
body:
In the beginning of the March 2025, the update had some scrolling issues, but after addressing it was fixed!
Thank you!!!

title: Best Calendar / Planner i've used
body: The interface is exactly what you need. The widgets are perfect. The workflow is fast. Great app, super happy with the new update.

title: Everything gone
body: All of my events are gone and I can’t get them back. Will be deleting the app. Very unhappy.

title: Crapped out on me today
body: The app has a lot of glitches. I type in one time and day and it ofyen siwtches it to another time and often the same day it is (ibstead if the one I input.) today it randomly deleted all of my past tense and upcoming events :(

title: Latest update ruined App for me
body: I’ve used this app for years and recommended it to everyone, but after latest update, U can’t move reoccurring tasks anymore. Looking for new app. What a shame.

title: Overrated
body: After logging in all my info for the week, I was trying to separate my iPhone calendar from this app and suddenly all info disappear. An entire hour gone.

title: Coming from Calendars 5.
body:
I have been using Calendars 5 for years and I was avoiding to leave it and switch into this subscription model one but eventually I had to do it. Aside from that I will have to pay for this, the features and benefits is not making much difference for me. And couple of issues came with the new app are;
Somehow I have lost all the configured tasks and their repeat cycles and I had to go through and configure them again.
Syncing is not great between IOS and Mac version as the tasks list does sync or act on their own.
On IOS, clicking the task in monthly view to to open it always marks the as complete. I had to switch to landscape mode because of that issue. This was never an issue in the older app.

I’ll stick with this as I dont want to go through Calendar app search again, but this is somewhat not superior to old one bug wise.

title: New update is 🔥🔥🔥
body: I have been using Readdle Calendars for many years. It’s always been my favorite calendar app for iPhone, but this new update is the best it has ever been. It’s so much easier to read and use. Looking forward to what is coming in future updates.

title: Task not working
body: Latest update broke the task function.  Updates and changes to tasks are not synching and updating with IOS.  All day tasks are not showing in the day or list view.

title: New Update
body: I used to love this app & I’ve paid for a subscription for 2…3yrs+ I recommended it to everyone and used it as my personal calendar along with keeping personal records… today I went to check my schedule & all my reoccurring events are GONE. My classes, GONE. My weekly meeting, GONE. My monthly check ups, GONE. PAST PRESENT & FUTURE! I’ve canceled my subscription & I’m leaving this review as warning. DON NIT WASTE YOUR MONEY! I’m so so so upset ):

title: Syncing
body: I have to turn off the sync and then restart to keep up to date.  Very cumbersome and worthless.  Supposed to be time saver and I have to repeat each time

title: What the heck?
body: I paid for this app when I first downloaded years ago and now all of a sudden you have a “pro version” that I have to pay for again? Oh and my task list is gone??? What did you do?!?

title: Love the update
body: I’ve been using this calendar app for YEARS and I love it. Over the years it’s updated so much in great ways! More colors, more functionality! It’s great. They listen to the feedback for sure, I can tell.

title: Life SAVING
body: This is the BEST calendar app I have ever used!

title: Even MORE Robust!
body: 5.35.1 is SO great so far! I've used this app for a lonnnnng time, because the flexibility of suits my workflow so much better than the native iCal app. And now, the thoughtful additions to functionality in the last few updates have made it even more valuable in my day. Love it!!

title: Unpleasant surprise
body: On opening my app this morning I had an unpleasant surprise. It took me 5 to 10 minutes to figure out how to get to my calendar, because I did not realize the side bar was open and all I had to do is slide it to the left. If anyone else opens up their app and notices all they can see is the menu just slide it to the left to get back to your calendar.

title: review
body: I love how I can color code my important events

title: Very cool app
body: Easy to set up and automatically syncs with another colander app

title: Great calendars app & planner
body: It’s productive and useful for me for plans I need to make or if I forget something.

title: It’s really helpful!
body: Been using this planner for over a year now and I really like the different options and ability to customize things!

title: Good but please change back
body: Firstly, I love and rely on this app for organizing my life. With the new update, tasks become completed with a tap and can only be edited with a long press, then ‘edit’. I find myself editing tasks a lot and it was more convenient/easier to simply tap towards the left of the task to ‘complete’ and tap towards the right of a task to get to the edit menu. It was also easier that I could simply tap to read the full task entry since the month view (my default view) has small boxes and only displays like 6 letters at a time. I ask that you please bring back that feature. Ty for your work

title: Amazing
body: I really do love this calendar. App is helped me so much. It’s different from the others because I can change the colors of each event and task. I just truly love this calendar.

title: Love the 5.35 updates!
body: Thanks for making some great improvements and more to come. I use Calendars to manage my small business as well as my hectic daily life. Although recent software bugs were a bit annoying the new format seems to be very user friendly. I look forward to using Calendars to improve my productivity.

title: Moves dates I never change
body: I update my Google calendar and sync it to calendars. For some reason the dates on calendars change a date forward or back and I NEVER change the dates on Google calendar. It’s incredibly annoying.

title: Used for years, walking away now
body: I loved the simplicity of this app…but it’s reset on me so many times out of nowhere. It’s so frustrating to lose your entire planner and pretty much ruins my life for weeks every time it happens. I’m done, this app cannot be counted on.

title: Just freezes up
body: Crummy, just freezes up syncing with google on install and no option to keep going

title: do not get this app.
body: i had so many events and important days for the next few months on this app, i loved it. it gave me reminders for things and was easy to use. it then deleted every event i had put down. so if you want to actually have a planer, don’t use this app.

title: Birthdays
body: I love my calendar 📆 but I wish it would populate the birthdays to the following year. 🥰

title: So dumb
body: I already have an IOS calendar. I was wanting something separate but I have to connect it and this pulls everything from the other one making this useless for my needs.

title: The perfect replacement for Fantastical
body: I’ve been a fan of Fantastical for years, and loved using its many features, most important of which were the ability to use natural language when making events. Like, “Make sure to complete that program, March 20 12 pm” or “Don’t forget to do that thing, Friday 12 pm every week.” But it got too bloated and added too many features.

title: Glitches, lots!!!
body:
I’ve been using this app for years and don’t want to switch, but it has several major glitches!! One I contacted the app creator about last summer (today is March 2025) and sent them screen recordings and descriptions and they still haven’t fixed it. When you go to edit an event it switches back after it’s saved and reverts it to prior to the change. It’s caused me to miss doctor appointments and be hours late for work (first time in over 20 years!!!) and be late for appointments!!! 

Another one is when selecting a time sometimes it just bounces back and forth the end time and date, then you re editing and switches around the beginning time/date, and go back and forth, back and forth, re editing over and over (at minimum 5 times), before it will eventually do what’s it’s supposed to do!!! 🤯

Please fix the glitches in this app so it can be a reliable calendar!!! I pay for pro and you’re causing major problems in my life by not fixing these issues!!! Also, please go back to the calendar version to select dates instead of the dumb scroll wheels (in edit mode)!!!

title: Can’t live without
body: I love this calendar. I cannot live without this! It helps keep everyone in the  family’s tasks, appts, events organized.

title: Love the app, One Request
body: I love the app and its features! It is exactly what I was looking for. However, I’d like to be able to change the color of the headers and the widget. I’m not too fond of baby blue, I’d much rather have the option to change it.

title: Not free
body: I've had this app for a long time and all of a sudden today I can't get into it because it's locked me out unless I subscribe to an annual fee! Tried to reinstall and lost all of my appointments and information I had on the calendar for the last year! I thought this was a free calendar! I'm so angry! I've lost everything that was on that calendar! What happened?

title: Horrible Purchase
body: After purchasing for one year, the upgraded calendar, every few days everything is erased from the calendar. What a mess!

title: Temperature
body: My temp is set to centigrade. How do I set it Fahrenheit?

title: Best Combo of Calendar And Task in One App
body: Very excited to engage with UX of Calendars and schedule events. it has a great set of utilities and features that boost my productivity.

title: Tries to be smart, but is just stupid
body: If i put "today" in my task it tries to make it all day. If i put a place it tries to localize the place even though that is not my intention. Just keep it simple.

title: Love this app!
body: I have used several apps and this is the first time I have been able to sync all relevant calendars and tasks in a way that seamlessly works with my MacBook and other apple devices.  Look and user interface are also fantastic.  Love it.

title: It’s Stuck!
body: I loved this app before the migration. I cannot get it to move past the initial sync. It’s been stuck for days now.

title: Needs to be shareable and then would be perfect
body: I really hate that you can’t share this app with your family for everyone to view calendar, events, and invites. This is the one major thing this app is missing.

title: Everything disappeared!
body: Whay happened? I have used this app for years. All of a sudden all my calendared dates are gone. Subscription is up to date.

title: Can’t unsubscribe to Japanese calendar
body: It would be a good app but for some reason it region locked me to Japanese calendar, and I can’t do anything about it unless I pay to change it. Mind you I live in California 😂😂.

title: Deletes all my events
body: I love this app but this is my second time experiencing everything simply delete from it. It is great at keeping everything organized for me until I go to look at it and all the events I’ve added have disappeared, I might have to stop using the app because I am always worried I’ll loose everything I’ve added.

title: We were baited
body: We were baited with a free app on Calendars 5 (5) only to be abandoned like a step child. 5 will no longer be supported, instead we will be required to SUBSCRIBE to a monthly/yearly membership. Everything is turning into a membership and this app decided to follow suite. Original supporters should be allowed to continue using Calendars free of charge. I will not be subscribing, time to look for another alternative. I’m sad and disappointed.

title: Great app
body: Makes my life easier. Very simple to use

title: Calendar
body:
The best app of all time. Simple as that .
Continues to be App # 1 in the world

title: Can’t set a repeat event for 10.5 days. It has to be a full day increment.
body: I use a Dexcom G7 blood sugar monitor sensor and it lasts 10-1/2 days. I would consider transferring from Calendar5 to Calendars if it did this. I also don’t want to pay a monthly subscription fee.

title: LOVE this app but…
body: There are times that I update an item and it just reverts right back. Happened too many times that it’s gotten annoying and I need to stay on the app for a minute to make sure nothing I edited gets reverted back.

title: After long time use, NOT working correctly
body: I wish there was customer service line that worked because my search function is not finding my events that I’ve verified are there. Even did a test on another calendar and it finds my events over there.  Tested and just will not pull the event up.  I use that function when I need to find medical events etc. To remember when things were done.  If I delete app to reinstall, it says will delete all info.

title: User & fan
body: I really wish I could edit holidays

title: Poor Decision
body:
Do not trust this developer. Their fallback response to critical reviews is that they offer a lifetime option. 
So developers—Where is it?
The developers list 10 pricing options which include add-ons that should be free! 
No disclosure anywhere on the App Store or at their website of the price for the lifetime option they keep mentioning or even its existence. 
The only way to find the price is to first migrate data to the paid app and once that process begins it’s too late.

I am not going to pay you twice for the app! This is a money grab. Calendars 5 Premium users should have their purchase restored in this new-named version. I have financially supported Readdle for many years and I would continue to pay Readdle for its apps if the company had just been honest and stated it needed to raise money. Choosing to gaslight posturing the change benefits your users vs. your company is a deceptive business practice. I urge you to reconsider charging Premium users a second time— because judging by the high number of negative user reviews your company may be losing far more support than it gains.

title: Text message reminders
body: I would appreciate a functionality that allows reminders via text messages to your invites. This will grant this app a 5 star!

title: Deleting data
body: I have had this app for years and years. Loved it and recommended it to all of my friends. Over the last couple of months though all of my events that I have saved in the calendar have been erased twice. I’m talking hundreds of events. My work schedule, a friend’s work schedule, two child custody schedules.. things you HAVE to have. Just gone. Very disappointed.

title: War Politics Don’t Belong
body: War politics don’t belong on a calendar app.  Plastering your Ukrainian flag on this app is cheap, unprofessional and reeks of partisan propaganda.  It’s also a constant reminder of how billions of my U.S. tax dollars have been squandered by the Biden regime with no positive results for anyone.  Keep your stupid flag off my phone.

title: Disappointed
body: I’m very disappointed that I purchased this app under the impression that it would be a lifetime purchase but now they are asking me to pay to upgrade.

title: Best calendar app there is!
body: I’ve used this for years, and it just keeps getting better. It’s easily customizable for personalization. There are tons of options for colors, app icons, layouts, etc. The features are detailed but simple to use, and there are never any technical issues.

title: Amazing! But some bad things
body: I LOVE this app! But it could use some work. Like when you make a To-Do list, it saves for the next day and doesn't re-mark it off. But I downloaded this a long time ago and are just checking it out now, so I don't know if things have changed.

title: Нацики
body: Война проебана, верните поддержку российских пользователей

title: Haven’t changed my mind
body: Appointments disappear. Entire calendar deletes itself. When this was free I had absolutely no issues. Now that I will be paying for it I have all kinds of issues and actually need a handwritten backup calendar because I cannot trust this one to keep my appointments. There is nothing like logging in to check a specific appointment only to find your entire calendar gone! Not just once either but multiple times. Then customer service uses the excuse of not being able to get back to me to help resolve the issue because of the Russian attacks on Ukraine. I used to be able to speak with someone at Microsoft. Now it is email only and that’s if they even reply. I’m still waiting and it’s been well over a week. I’ll be shopping for a new calendar before my payment is due in June. I would not recommend to anyone. Do not waste your money or your time. I’m so disappointed.

title: Very Clean App, One Suggestion
body: I switched over to Calendars from Fantastical. Both apps are better than the stock MacOS calendar, but Calendars gives you the most value for money. The interface is clean, quick and reliable. The desktop widget is also wonderfully designed. I do have one small nit: It would be really nice to have a ‘list’ pane that could be set up on the side. The iOS version has a ‘list’ view and it is pretty much all I use. I’d like the MacOS version to be able to display both the month view and a sidebar list view. Thanks!

title: Already purchased app- now subscription???
body: I purchased the calendars app years ago for full price and have enjoyed using it. Until I got the notice that although I already paid for it, it’s turning into a yearly subscription- for $20/yr for a calendar?? And I’ve read the stock/scripted reply you’re trying to give to all the other long time customers who have commented the same. Why would we keep the old app that you won’t be supporting anymore? I’ll promptly be canceling/deleting this app when the trial is done and be moving onto something else.

title: Best calendar app I’ve found
body: I have five different Google calendars I have to abide by and it’s a lot to look at. This app does the best job of visually organizing and allowing me to select and deselect various calendars and then also add and remove appointments.

title: Long time user
body:
I’ve been using this app since it came out I think?! I remember being in middle school using it for assignments and now I’m a mom of a kiddo and still use it! It’s perfect for everything. At first everything was free but obviously as it got more users and got more streamlined some features had a small fee. But this is fine and the free version is still beautiful! I have a feeling the new iPhone calendar and google calendar took inspiration from this app😉 thank you to the creator- you’ve changed my life! 

As a side note this is perfect for neurodivergent people- so many easy organization features and connects with other calendar apps!

title: Super helpful!
body: As a busy college student, this helps me a lot planning my weeks, and months. Very helpful, 10/10

The app will crash and close and at one point I thought of just purchasing the pro to get it to stop doing that but it does not let me upgrade. Every time I press the upgrade button on the app it will freeze and close the app. Very sad because I used to love this app. :(

I had understood that I had bought the app and future upgrades at the time which would mean I wouldn’t start paying annually.

title: Ruined a once great product!!!
body:
I was a happy user of the Calendars product(s) for years…. Then they decided to get “clever” and go to a subscription constantly paying plan…. It had always been a “pay once and have it” product, at least by version.

If that wasn’t bad enough, when I reluctantly moved forward with paying for it on the subscription, they also “broke” the product at the same time!  What had always been a super reliable product that merged multiple different calendars (and types of calendars) into one convenient place just stopped syncing correctly!  My calendars became an unusable, unreliable mess!! 

Goodbye, Readdle…. You have definitely lost me as a customer….  But now I have find something else that actually works!

title: Best app

I have

title: Feedback
body:
Question

Why, in the current free version of the Calendars app, would a feature/capability like setting up a new event to repeat on a periodic basis, be “taken away from users” and only be available in the paid version of the app? This seems like nothing more than a “money grab” and an insult to loyal/longtime users, who previously had access to this functionality.

This type of change makes a previous loyal user look to switch to another calendar app that treats its users equitably and does not remove previously free features.

If it were possible to Rate the current app version a “Zero”, I would do so, as you earned it.

title: Great on Apple Watch
body: I wanted an app that I could look at all my calendars at once, and this is great for that. However, the best feature is on my Apple Watch. It shows where the time is on your calendar. For instance, if you put a meeting from 9 - 11 am, at 10 am, it would show a status bar in the middle of the reminder for the meeting. I’m very happy with this subscription, and I think it’s worth the price.

title: Meh
body: Simply put, I’ll keep it while it’s free. I won’t pay for it when that time comes, I will find another basic calendar. This App has nothing special about it

title: Default error
body: Here’s my wish for 2025 - that the calendar would have a better way of *transitioning* between setting up new appointments/items  and switching back to the present day and time post-insertion. It generally remains on some future that and time unless I select the today selection.

title: Calendar
