title: Please consider…
body: As a devoted Calendar 5 user, please consider offering a onetime purchase of the full feature new calendar program and reminders. I will not do recurring charges, but will do onetime purchases with upgrades for life. I just hate the constant popup and would consider paying to make it go away on login. Thanks.

title: Missing a crucial option
body: Love the layout, but this calendar is somehow missing a crucial element: there is no way to select a new event as “free” or “busy”.  NO option to set “show as” makes this app useless for me.

title: Was a great app
body: I loved this app and had been using it for a very long time but I feel like it was ruined with the most recent update adding ads to it and terrible loading time

title: The absolute best looking application for a Calendars in the App Store
body: Calendars offer a wide range of features that meet most needs, but there are two key enhancements that could elevate the application even further. Introducing travel times and the exceptional planning feature available in the macOS version would significantly improve user experience and functionality.

title: Frustrated for the first time in years…
body: Up until this last update, I’ve been more than thrilled with this product. Now, it’s suddenly showing birthdays I have listed in my Gmail contacts. I don’t want this, didn’t have it a few weeks ago, and I can’t find where to turn it off. I’ve loved this calendar because it kept all the clutter away, and now this. Please help.

title: DONT UPDATE THIS APP
body: I have been using this app for several years and always loved it. I specifically loved that I could keep this calendar separate from all of my others. Well recently, after an automatic update, it now forces you to sync your other calendars to it, just like every other calendar app. It wouldn’t allow me to access my calendar at all unless I synced it. So I was forced to sync it with the only other calendar I have which is my Google calendar. Upon doing so, it WIPED out my entire precious calendar. Everything. Every appointment I had schedule, event, etc. it DELETED IT ALL. I had a panic attack and am currently still trying to remember all of my events to add back to it. Do yourself a favor DO NOT UPDATE this app unless you want to lose everything you’ve planned and added to your calendar. Or better yet, don’t use this app and risk it. So disappointed because I loved this app for so long until now.

title: Lost everything
body:
I used this app for several years and loved it. It had ALL my events, client sessions, workout tracking, my whole life organized and color coded as I run a small business. Yesterday I woke up to everything being gone. Disappeared. I contacted support and they told me to sign into one of the main places where calendars are supposed to be synced, but none of my stuff was there either. I reached out again, twice , and no one is getting back to me. I am extremely frustrated and I feel like the rug has been pulled from underneath me .

Update: despite their response to my review, they never got back to me when I sent them yet another email. Thoroughly disappointed.

title: PLEASE HELP 😭
body:
The new update keeps changing the event times to all day events.

This happens when the event title contains a specific day… in my case, I added ‘on Friday’ to the title, and it was immediately highlighted and switched to all day. 

Even if my original event title and times were saved, if I open the event and to edit the title etc, it overrides the previous event times when it automatically selects all day event. 

Please correct the issue or provide help if I’m missing something

title: Don't love it
body: I won't hide it: I'm disappointed that Readdle replaced Calendars 5 with Calendars. It's the same app, but now another subscription. I don’t see the point in paying subscriptions for email and calendar apps, and even though Readdle offers nice design and functionality, I always felt Calendars’ workflows, tools, and views were a bit awkward. The date picker is odd, and so is the inability to re-size the day view. I've given up on their apps and support because they are stubborn and don’t want feedback from their customers. I guess this is goodbye, Readdle.

title: Great App, too expensive.
body: I lover the Calendars App. We’ve used Calendars 5 for years. This summer I switched to Tick Tick and was disappointed that I couldn’t share that  App with my husband and children without paying on each iCloud account. I used Tick Tick for a few months, and found it to be over the top for us. I’m guessing it’s a great App, but I wanted a return to something simpler. Sadly, Calendars 5 will not continue to be supported and after downloading the new Calendars App, I find that I cannot share my purchase with my family. Ridiculous! More than $80 per year for a family of 4 to have the same  calendar! If I am wrong about this, please point me in the right direction.

title: Great but

title: Minor repairs needed
body: Great app. It has an AI of its own that assists you when writing the title. It’s great. It just comes in trouble when you want to write down a task such as “Hanging out with Joe” and it will try to access your contacts and try to drag Joe into it. Or perhaps you want to say “Prepare for the June 4th event” but you are scheduling it for June 3rd. It will lock it in for June 4th. So the ability to disable that would be helpful

I did check to make sure the all my email accounts linked up correctly.

title: I love this calendar app

I love that I can plug all of the event info into the title and it’ll auto-fill the time, date, location, contact... especially if I’m logging an event on the fly or mid conversation. So much more clicking for the basics and fewer options with the other apps.

title: Change isn’t always good
body: Loved my calander 5 I can’t even tell you how many years I had it.. I want to say close to 10 years. I fought myself over purchasing it at first and it has been a good investment. Now here comes the new and improved version which usually just means more money and more headaches. I really don’t mind buying it for a lifetime like the Calendar 5 was suppose to be and if it’s a year free trial I will have to decide whether to cancel and use what my office uses since they will not pay for this. I was lucky they allowed it in my work phone without a big hassle and the app has been good to me without many issues. Please keep the fees minimal and only for add ons.

title: Deleting this app

title: Widgets not appearing on MacOS 15
body: When I try to add widgets to the desktop or Notification Center, calendars app is not even an option. I don’t know where the widgets went. I even tried reinstalling the app but that didn’t solve the problem. As of now I cannot use the widgets on MacOS 18.

title: Dear Readdle
body:
I just realized you are going to start charging me to use your new Calendar app. I have had Calendar 5 for a few years and gotten along okay with it. Now you want to make me pay a monthly or annual fee so I can change background colors  and have dark mode?! Woopie! What a wonderful deal for some multi-millionaire CEO! 
When I have to pay annual fees to use software I have already paid for is the day Readdle get deleted from my devices.
Good luck on your new money making venture. I’m not helping you with that. :(

title: Terrible bug
body: If you drag one of the recurring schedule, it will ruin all your schedules, what a terrible thing! It takes me half a day to reorganize my schedules, and there might be still errors!

title: Don't buy.
body:
There's absolutely zero guarantee they won't do the exact same thing with yet another price increase. Calendars 5 was also sold with a "lifetime" purchase. 

Why would I ever give you money for a "lifetime" purchase again? Nothing is stopping you from just screwing over people again a few more years down the road.

title: Review
body: I find this app backward to use

title: New Version and lost all my entries!
body:
I’m not very happy right  now!
I upgraded to the new version, no problem!  NOW there is a problem!

All of my entries have disappeared!  The only ones left are Birthdays!  Is there a recovery technique in this new version???

title: Rockstar!
body: I’m loving the clean and simple interface. Had been using Busy Cal for a few years. The Readdle calendar seems just a tad cleaner and quicker.

title: Ancillary Holiday Markers
body: I really wish this app would allow you to delete ancillary holiday markers like Columbus Day. It really clutters up the schedule especially, for some reason, when it gives you 5 different notifications for the same exact holiday. I can’t seem to understand how the premium subscription offers any bonuses apart from the standard one. What am I paying for exactly? More color variations? The app isn’t bad but it’s just annoying that we have to be nickle and dimed every which way

title: Money grab
body: I'm quite disappointed to learn my Calendars 5 Lifetime purchase will soon be rendered obsolete as the formerly trustworthy Readdle has manufactured a classic "bait and switch" by not supporting it any longer. Their focus will now be on a product that is virtually the same but the name is slightly changed so they can charge customers either a subscription or attempt to again extract money for an alleged lifetime purchase. I won't be fooled twice.

title: Serious cursor glitch
body:
There's a serious glitch with the hovering cursor in the latest update. The cursor arrow flies all over the place when hovering over any events in the calendar. It makes the app unusable on my iPad Pro 2024 with Magic Keyboard. It just started happening a couple days ago.

Please fix this ASAP and I will change the rating.

title: Better
body: The app freezes after I type the first letter of the email of any guest that I try to add to an event. Please explain.

title: Terrible
body: I’m so annoyed. You took away the old app that I payed for and now, if I want some of the same features I have to pay a huge subscription fee. Really seems unethical. I’m very disappointed.

title: So close to perfect but not for $
body: This app and its widgets are mostly great. I got the app because I wanted a vertical weekly view. Love love love it. Very easy to add and manage events. But there is no way to print the view you see. Weekly printing is only landscape, which I could do with free apps. Over all it’s decent app, but expected more with subscription. Won’t renew.

title: Zero continuous vertical scroll
body:
I love this calendar, BUT…… I have asked for YEARS for the added ability for a continuous vertical scroll in month view. All to no avail. Many times you just need to be able to see more of the next month and at the same time see the current month. Now they have updated with colors and extraneous things. Very disappointing!

PLEASE ADD THE ABILITY TO SCROLL CONTINUOUSLY VERTICALLY. Most calendars have this ability. Even the useless Apple calendar has this function. It’s really such a simple thing that would add better functionality.

title: Great Calendar App
body: Amazing calendar app better than all the others.  It is easy to use and has everything I need in a beautiful format.

title: Dishonest App Developer!
body: I have already paid for my Calendar 5 but now have lost my life-time status in Calendar 6 and have to pay again! This is a dishonest move! I will not pay again and will move to either Apple or Google calendars!

title: Issues with IOS18
body: Been using this app forever, purchased the 1x $5 fee for the pro upgrade and it won’t let me use any pro features. If I click to try to rebuy.. it just says I’m all set and thank you. But I can’t do any of the pro things. Might need an update to fix bugs

title: Menu button
body: Where is the menu button and back button ? Swiping from the edges of the screen just to back to calendar from menu ?

title: Need to see the date
body:
I love the different ringtones however, when the app stands alone on the Home Screen you can’t just glance at the calendar to check the date like the one Apple phones come with.  You actually have to open the app to see the date which is annoying when you’re in a hurry.
That’s the reason for the 3.

title: Warning!!!!!
body: I just updated my software and it completely crashed this app. It deleted my whole calendar and I cannot figure out how to get it to sync. As a busy mom this is infuriating.

title: Enable ability to add attachments
body: Would be great if attachments could be added to events to be viewed along with the event. Overall the app is great.

title: Love this!  One thing's missing...
body: Fantastic app for keeping me on track with what I have to do in my day.  What I do notice, however, is that there's no "TODAY" button for the calendar section on the 'mobile' app.  Add this, and there's another 5 star rating to have in your review.

title: Calendar
body: Keeps me organized.

title: I only gave it 3 stars because really on the right page but needs to be way more fun and icons etc.,
body: Hi, I only gave it 3 stars because really so far on the right page..!) but needs to be way more organization/ way more , totally more fun and icons etc., It's in the right direction..!)

title: Options
body: I don’t want to upgrade to premium and it won’t let me opt out. It’s very frustrating.

title: The most Intuitive app I’ve come across
body: So easy, smart and intuitive. Speaking from someone using email for years (and years 😀). I recommend it.

title: Best Organizational App
body: Calendars has kept my head on straight for the past 3 years! I first got it because I liked the way it was laid out visually, for a week at a time. But I also love how customizable it is — from individual calendars to recurring events, it’s so versatile and fantastic. I cringe to think about how I’d keep everything organized without it. :)

title: Very disappointing update
body: I’ve been using this app for many years. The update that I’m forced to use (to continue using it without new app interruptions) costs me $20 a year. Ok. The problem is I deleted my wife’s free OG calendar app and replaced it with the new app, I paid for it right? We purchase share all apps amongst the family. It appears family purchase sharing is only available with the $59 lifetime version. That lifetime option doesn’t look available to me now. What the heck? Now my wife has a limited use calendar app from Readdle. Nice job. This is all very lame.

title: Easy calendar
body: This one is one of the better calendar.  Easy to navigate on phone and don’t have issues trying to add to a already busy day.  I use it for personal to keep thing separate. Great calendar.

title: Good not Great
body: I love almost everything about this calendar app EXCEPT how it changes the date on my schedule!!! When i create a new schedule and put in, for exp, sept 15 to sept 20 and then need to chg the starting date to sept 16, this app automatically changes the end date to sept 21! If i don’t catch it then i’m screwed!!! And my client isn’t too happy either! PLEASE i beg of u, FIX this! This major problem (for me at least) deserves a 1 star rating but bc the rest of the app is great i gave it 3. Lots of ppl have seen & asked if i like my calendar; i wud love to tell them it’s a 5 ⭐️. My wish list is that the app wud offer more colors. FYI i’ve had this premium app for a few yrs. Thank you. 🙂

title: Can’t even get started
body: Downloaded it and immediately it required me to connect with other calendar apps on my phone. I tried to give it permission to access the main calendar app and it told me I needed to change the settings. Settings were already what they were supposed to be. App refused to acknowledge that it had access. App deleted. 10 minutes wasted.

title: Used to be great, continues to get worse
body: This new update breaks the color coordination on google calendars. The events no longer have the correct colors! On top of this, the previous update broke the solid vs outlined color orientation for all-day events vs specific timed events. That still hasn’t been fixed, and here they are breaking more stuff!!!! It really angers me that these updates have basically ruined a great app, and I have no way of going back to the old version.

title: Simple and exactly what I needed
body: Solid app. Looked for a while to find a simple calender that worked for school. This one is pretty great

title: Horrible
body: worst app ever good going maybe let me make my own thing without adding a calendar like obviously if im downloading this i dont have a calendar so im trying to make one so use ur head and dont make me sync it

title: No Microsoft?
body: Why can’t I connect my Hotmail calendar?

title: no microsoft integration??
body: can't sync it with my hotmail calendar. keeps giving me server  error. did i waste my money?

title: Poor Subscription Model
body: I bought the app once several years ago and absolutely loved it!!! It’s been the lynchpin of my professional life! But now that they’ve transferred to a subscription model, it feels overvalued for what you get. I now need a subscription access for basic features like recurring events or to send my appointments to others! I really wish Readdle hadn’t done that. But what can i do?

title: Love the month view
body: The large widget needs to show more items. If my calendar has only one appointment for the next day, the large widget is empty. Needs to go out more days to fill the widget. This prevents me from leaving the Apple calendar widget completely.

title: problem with my purchase
body: I have been usuing the pro version for more than 2 years now but since my last purchase my account hasnt got pro although the purchase has been made and each time I tap restore purchase but it is not working and it has been more than a month now that my account is basic while I have paid for the pro version, I couldnt finf any support contact option or email so I am writing here hoping this bug will be fixed,

title: calendars app
body: i love this app but it could have more colors but in all it is a very good app!!

title: Pay for recurring
body: I think it’s ridiculous to make me pay for a basic feature like recurring events, deleted

title: EVERYTHING GONE
body: I have been using this app for SEVEN YEARS and today I opened it and my calendar with all SEVEN YEARS of events is GONE!!!! Everything is gone!!! I had linked everything to the calendar not to an account or email and I can’t get it back. SCREW YOU GUYS

title: Updated review:
body: The company was very responsive to my emails, and did restore my previous purchase.  I had left a bad review initially, but I am changing it to 5 star because of the immediate response, and willingness to honor previous purchases.   The paid version does let you have multiple alerts for each item you put in.  Highly recommend.

title: Appointment
body: Can’t put appointments across multiple calendars

title: Updates to widget -great app
body: It be great if the developers kould add a interactive agenda with dual week action widget. Would be great to not have to open the app just to see the next day or two days ahead.

title: Not as good
body:
The newer version does not seem to allow the color coding on iPad that I enjoy with the Calendar 5 version. And while I used to be able to push color coding from my phone to the iPad via FUll sync, that doesn’t work.
Guess I will use the old one (for which I had a lifetime subscrip) until it doesn’t work anymore

title: subscription rot
body:
i purchased Calendars prior to Readdle's switch to the sub model. their model is their right. they should get to make money and develop exciting things. But that isn't what had happened. i draw the line at thumbing their noses at previous owners who decide not to buy into their sub model. i mean canceling the ability to freely schedule repeat activities? really? taking an ESSENTIAL functionality for any calendar/planner and hiding it behind a paywall? that's just gross on top of being unethical in my opinion. zero stars for Readdle if I could.

I have tried restoring my previous purchase multiple ways and while I get messages saying the restoration was a success, I also keep getting tossed back to another message telling me to upgrade if I try to schedule a repeat activity. 

i own Spark by Readdle and subscribe to PDF Expert as is. it's time to review my participation in the Readdle ecosystem. disappointing and disgusting.

title: Love the app
body: I’ve been paying for the app a couple years and might i say its the best app. It’s fun. I like the the fact that you can change the colors to suit a specific life event. Keep it up guys i love love love lovvvveeeeeee this.

title: Best update ever - more colors to chose from
body: Been using this app for several years and it is amazing. Their latest update added the ability to chose from dozens of colors for entries in the calendar - absolute gold and makes the app a solid 5 stars all day long!

title: Money grab
body: After paying for the calendars5 app, this just smells like a money grab. They pretend and try to explain this as something else but they took away significant functionality unless you want to pay again for the same features.

title: Subscription is a rip off
body: I have been using Calendar 5 for many years and I paid for the app.  Now you want a yearly subscription fee — everyone wants a subscription fee.  You are told there is a one time purchase, then later switch to subscription — typical bait and switch.  How is one to afford subscriptions on everything?  the app prompted me to migrate to the blue calendar and now I am stuck.  I have to look for a new calendar that does not have a subscription fee.  I am so disappointed in Readdle Technologies.

title: It's "only" OK.
body:
This is a basic calendar app that I so badly would like to love, but it doesn't allow the use of any extra features. For example, there's no way to add "Interesting calendars". I assume that would be for the paid version. However there's no way to add In-App Purchases anywhere in the app. I sent an email to the developer to ask for help, that was ignored and never answered. Customer service doesn't exist. Too bad. This ould be a nice app. 
I have the iPhone version as well, that works really nice. The Mac version needs a lot of improvements.

title: Perfecto
body: Great

title: Why take away the alerts?
body: I liked when you can set multiple alerts for one event. Why was that taking away?

title: Stickers
body: This is a wonderful app, I share and keep up with family and they keep up with me. I would love to see more stickers added such as the 4th of July, Easter, Thanksgiving. Would also like to see babysitting or grandbabies stickers for boys and girls.

title: Groot Calendar
body: Daily

title: Revert Memo placement
body: Extremely annoying to leave memos for my family now. Not an upgrade at all to the UI we are all used to. An absolute downgrade in my honest opinion as someone who works in the same industry.

title: Oh my beautiful calendar
body: What a wonderful calendar. I literally have not a single unkind word to share about this app. It is so easy to use, lets you be very specific on times, reminders, color coding, etc. I used to LOVE the shared calendar feature which was really nice for me and my friends. It’s still there I just don’t use it anymore. Anyway, the MOST IMPORTANT SENTENCE OF MY REVIEW: this app doesn’t push ads, makes you buy anything, and is cute/easy to use. 100/10

title: PERFECT CALENDAR
body: Great for keeping up with friends!

title: Calendar
body: 5 stars

title: Doesn’t sync for family.
body: You will have to put everything in manually and then it will show twice on your own calendar. Takes all the convince out of a shared calendar. Wish I’d known before I paid for a yearly subscription

title: Great app
body: Very well put together and helpful. Keeps me and my girlfriend informed on our schedules and can easily know when each others work schedules in order. Good platform

title: Horrible update
body: I hate this update. The (+) option is in a really bad position. It’s obscuring part of the calendar. I also miss the search icon on the bottom tool bar where it’s easy to find. Please don’t copy Apple with the photos app starting with iOS 18. If it ain’t broke, don’t fix it.

title: What a wonderful Tool.
body:
I encourage Leaders to teach their people how to use this application both on their phones and online. It helps bring forth understanding and transparency when it comes to compliance
and agreement. Reminders, memos, multiple alerts and alarms are just the tip of the iceberg. PDF’s, pictures, GIFs and messages can all be shared through different departments.

title: Gustavo calendar
body: Yacht

title: bad new update
body: i enjoy this app a lot but the new update is just not it. i don't like the minimalist stuff and also did they get rid of memos??? what are we doing here.

title: Easy to use!
body: Organizes all our family events on one calendar!

title: Updated UI is absolute rubbish
body: I used to love using this app for our family. It let us easily keep track of events for each family member and used the memo section for things like groceries and to-do lists. With the updated UI trying to navigate the app has become a chore and a frustrating experience. Cannot state how much I hate “minimalist” UIs that bury all its user features. Where it used to be a single tap, things are now buried in sub-menus. Terrible update, a real step backwards for a former favourite.

title: Love it
body: I love this app. I wish you had a digital wall calendar that we could use with it!

title: Love the Simplicity of this App!
body: From the beginning of using this app, I have immediately just been very pleased with it. I love the design of the Calendar and everything, but mostly the simplicity of using it. It is so quick and easy to add something on the calendar and then edit/remove it, just as fast. My time is precious and this really works well for me. Just to add, I struggle badly with my memory and ADHD. I have not missed one single appointment since I started using this App. My friend put me on TimeTree. Thank you Bestie!

title: Love It
body: My favorite calendar app so far, and I feel like I’ve tried them all. I share with my mom for babysitting purposes, I share with my husband for life collaboration, and I share with my best friend bc duh. My only wish is that there was a way to mark off the days that are past, like one does on a physical calendar!

title: Help me cancel this app!
body: I thought this was something else and trying to cancel but having great difficulty

title: Great family schedule tracking app!
body: Great for keeping track of my mom and dad’s medical appointments. Family members are updated in real time which is really convenient.

title: I recommend this App!
body: Simple to us and it has never given me issues.

title: Awesome
body: TimeTree is really good, reliable, and easy to use, just put the date and time you are going to a certain event and you can also put fun reminders of (remind me 1 day before, 2 days before, 1 hour before, etc. you can do all of them at once if you want :) so it’s super easy. And this is a calendar app where you can invite people to it so you can all see the same events ❤️ it’s just very goood (there’s multiple colors you can color your events too.

title: Amazing
body: This is an amazing tool and the most organization I’ve had in years. I’m part of several organizations, plus a mom and employee. I love this calendar.

title: Love it !!!
body: I like how we can link it up with family. So we are on the same page.

title: update is terrible
body: please revert back to old version. where is the last time we checked the calendar part?? i loved that part. it sucks now. i hate it. i’ve had this for YEARS. why ruin a perfectly good thing?

title: Great app for calendar sharing
body: I love this app for sharing a calendar with my partner and our employee. We can overlay our personal and business items, get notifications, choose custom alert timing, and color code everything for easy, at a glance scheduling. I have ADHD and have always struggled to keep a calendar, but I’ve been using TimeTree successfully for almost two years now. It’s a great app.

title: User since 2016
body:
I will first say I love this calendar app!  It has truly worked for my needs without overwhelming my OCD or ADHD! I love the fact I can have multiple calendars and a separate shared calendar between family members! 

One thing I will say is hopefully they add to premium option to change color of events. I mean $45 a year USD and doesn’t give you that option is NOT worth it. I’d never be a premium member without that option.

title: Js a friend
body: Me and my friends love this but it would be super cool if we could comment on the pictures
Very handy-

title: Great for the family
body: Found this calendar to be great for the family to add events and have all the family activities in one place.

title: New UI is poor
body: Been using time tree for years, but the new update is definitely a down grade. I can normally intuit new interfaces, but this one is just so not user friendly. Everything is less convenient than it was before, which is not what you want to achieve with an update.

title: There are free versions of this
body: Lame calendar, it does the same as other calendars but this one you have to pay for to make it actually functional. Not worth $4.99 when I can get a different app that’s ACTUALLY FREE!

title: Family’s label list colors.
body: Please bring back dark orange.

title: Literally the best app I’ve ever used
body: I have never ever rated an app but I love this calendar so much I stopped what I was doing to rate. It is the most intuitive, easy to use app but it has every single feature you can want. It’s so easy to coordinate with your family and share what you need. I also tried other calendar apps and none of them compare.

title: convenient
body: I use this app all the time with my friend so we know when we are both available to hangout. It is so convenient and easy to use. However, they had nice features that they took away which takes some fun out of it. For example, they took out a feature where you can add little stickers on the calendar, it made it cute and fun. It’s disappointing seeing them take it away.

title: Getting bad ads now
body:
Getting video ads that take up a lot of the screen 
Never had ads before at least not bothersome to the point where I noticed it

title: Hate new update
body: annoying

title: Useless after the update
body: The app became absolutely impossible to use after the last update. How do you not have an easily reached “search” button in a calendar??? And navigattion between the calendars got so much worse too. We keep getting downgrades instead of upgrades. Our whole business depended on this up but I really don’t know how we can continue using this. It’s a nightmare

title: Can’t get much better than this
body: I use this app to communicate scheduling for my food truck works out. Great easy to use. Text updates communicate communicates to the whole staff.

title: Hard to read/search
body: We have used the calendar as a family that comes on your phone for years. It works but lacks some functions so we decided to try this one about 7 months ago. It is just so hard to read which really defeats much of the usefulness. It takes a minute to even find the current date as the calendar is so small. It is very frustrating.

title: Change it back
body: Memos are much harder to access. You now have to click a tab under the initial calender that opens with the app. Then you have to select a calendar (I only have one anyway, my family's) then you click another tab under THAT calendar and THEN you can access memos. It used to be a one click process.

title: Good and all, but omg the ads
body: I like this app, but OMG WHAT ARE THESE ADS?!

title: Useful AF
body: Use it for the family to keep track of everyone’s appointments, school events, and other things.

title: Still the Best App – Please Bring Back the Old View
body:
I’ve been using TimeTree for over 5 years and honestly I don’t know what I would do without it. My entire management business, cleaners’ schedules, team coordination and guest bookings depend on this app.

I’m leaving 1 star only to get attention — this is truly a 5-star app for me. The latest update made it very hard to find all calendars. The previous version, where all calendars were visible at the top, was much easier and more efficient.

If possible, please consider bringing that view back. Also, an automatic Airbnb sync would be amazing for reservations.

Thank you for such an important tool in my daily work.

title: Genial

title: Difficult to search for entries.
body: Use this app daily but disappointed with update. Can no longer search while in calendar view. Will be looking for another option.

title: Perfect for Families!!!

It keeps us so organized and we honestly could not keep track of everything each other has going on without this app!! HIGHLY RECOMMEND

title: Good app

title: Time tree
body: I love this app to  organize calendar app  update app to make it easier

title: So organized
body: Makes scheduling time in relationships so much easier than a calendar

title: Not useful at all
body: So at first I was thrilled with this app. Finally, an easy way to keep my husband and I on the same page as far as scheduling. Then eventually we realized that the events I am entering into the calendar aren’t shown on his calendar. Or at least, not all of them. We can’t for the life of us figure out the rhyme or reason as to why some events are shared, while others aren’t. But it renders the app entirely useless. And there’s no way to contact customer service to trouble shoot as far as I can see. Very disappointing.

title: Please consider changing this

With the latest update of having to have the IOS 17.0 or later update, I can no longer use this app and I’m extremely upset over it. I do not have the storage to update my phone therefore I cannot get the latest version of the app. There is a consistent notification popping up telling me i need the new update everytime i open the app. Please consider changing this as I would love to continue to use the app but currently can’t do so.

I’m going to try to work with it but it’s really hard to give this all the stars I want to due to the app issue on iPad. Works fine on iPhone since it’s built for it.

title: Put it back.
body: Great app, works just fine, PUT IT BACK THE WAY IT WAS BEFORE THR UPDATE! The formatting is horrible!

title: Perfect for staying organized!!

title: We don’t update around here
body:
If you did not want me using this version, then you should have never released it. Only release the final version when it is ready, then no update ever has to be conducted. 

So, you can stop asking me to update, we don’t do that around here. We come correct the first time; otherwise, we don’t come at all. Now, you do the same.

title: WAS Great
body: Their terms of service has been updated and includes AI now, absolutely useless. It really sucks, I loved this app

title: Amazing app
body: Easily the best calendar app. I’ve used it with my frisk friends for the past 2 years. Literally nothing compares!

title: Old version not supported
body:
My entire life is on this app. As a family of 5 with specialist appointments, 2 kids in school, conparentong, full time job, bills and family dr visits this is my go to app EVERY DAY. 

It is now not supported by apple without updating. My phone is older and I do not have the money to purchase a newer phone and the app has become unusable. Not only is this very disappointing but it’s going to be A PAIN finding a different app and transferring all the information to that one. I understand updating for better usage or features, but it would be nice to have access to a simpler older version instead of starting over.

title: Valnbillm
body: Absolutely LOVE this. So easy to see family schedule and allow us to schedule appts and family events without conflicts.

title: Not loving the new design
body: Its a little crowded and too much going on. I use this app because its simple. Might be looking for a new family app.

title: It’s good, but there’s much better out there
body: I’ve been using time tree for about 4-5 years, and have decided to make a switch. I’ll outline both the good and bad. Good: easy to create shared calendars that others can edit and access, friendly user interface, and good customization options for evens. Bad: ads. Ads all the time. I don’t want to pay for a calendar app so unless you want to see them, look elsewhere. Export limitations. When I decided to switch to a different calendar app, TimeTree will not let you export your events, only import from others. I know why they do it but it’s annoying. Hopefully this helps someone, good luck!

title: Love it
body: Great for keeping multiple schedules! We have 6 people to keep track of and this calendar is fantastic!

title: bluh ruh
body: good

title: love it
body: it’s nice being able to share a calendar with my friends (even if they won’t put their schedules on it 😒) i also love that it saves event names and times. it makes putting in my work schedule so easy!

title: Best Calendar EVER
body: This is the best digital calendar I have ever used. It’s the only one that actually helps me be organized. Only thing I would love to have is more color labels and widening the calendar. Highly recommend for people that has a hard time writing down their plans and need something fast.

title: Best Shared Calendar Ever
body: I’m able to create a custom Calendar for work and share with my husband. Then create one for family and friends out of state. title: Amazing Developers
body:
I use this app with my boyfriend and has been extremely useful in preparing plans and being informed on each other’s schedules. We used to live an hour away from each other, but this app made it easy to make plans to see each other without having to ask the other about availability. 

It’s super easy to use, and there are a variety of options to make events more specific- but not too many options to overwhelm you. 

It’s free, and the ads aren’t in your face. Frequent updates as well. There’s a great team behind this app!

title: I don’t know what my life would be without this app
body: I’ve used this app all throughout high school and now college. I have three calendars to help me balance my life between school, relationships, and work. I first discovered it in high school while trying to make plans with friends. We had a shared calendar which made it easier to see who was free when and have not gone back since then. It is the first app I clicked when I open my phone, wake up in the morning, or need to set reminders. I also love that the premium features is an add on and NOT a must have (as many other apps do this) and the free features are more than enough to help me organize myself. They do have ads but it is on top of the app and doesn’t get it the way. Most of the time I don’t even notice it. I will say some of my friends do love it, and others don’t find calendars very helpful. This is just my experience and I have loved it and found it extremely useful to the point I don’t know how I would go on without it. Very grateful. Thank you to the TimeTree team.

title: Honestly the WORST
body:
I only use this because my boyfriend started using it when he built his company, and now his entire company uses it just because it’s difficult to transfer data out of it. Timetree doesn’t communicate with any other calendars (Gmail, Apple, Skylight, etc) which is INFURIATING. 

It’s also insane that you have to manually copy existing events if you want to transfer them to another one of your Timetree calendars. You can’t just click a single button or tick box to tell it to apply the same event to X, Y and Z calendar within Timetree. And any adjustments made to that event aren’t carried over to the other calendar. Ughhhhhhh!

I’m trying to make my life easier, not more difficult 🤦🏻‍♀️

title: Recurring Event Setup Needs Clarity
body:
While setting up a recurring event, I noticed the weekly repeat option includes a field labeled “[number] Repeat each week.” This phrasing is confusing and not very user-friendly. It made me question whether entering “2” means the event happens twice a week, every other week, or something else entirely.

After testing, it seems that entering “2” makes the event repeat every two weeks — biweekly. But the app then displays “each 2 Wednesday,” which is ambiguous. Is that shorthand for “every second Wednesday of the month”? Or does it mean “every other Wednesday”? Either way, it’s not intuitive.

Suggested Improvements:

• Change the input label to “Repeat every [number] week(s)” — this is clearer and matches how users naturally think about scheduling.
• Display a summary at the top like “Repeats every 2 weeks on Wednesday” to confirm the logic.
• Use consistent phrasing like “Repeat: every [number] week(s)” to avoid confusion and improve readability.

title: Best Calendar App
body: I have a big family with five kids ranging from baby to preteen. My older kids are active in multiple sports. My husband and I also have different activities we do. For example, he coaches middle school football. This is the best calendar app I’ve tried, and the only one that I found that truly works for our family. Some features I love are the ability to scan images, add attachments, add addresses via a map, and assign users. I can add all relevant event details in one place. I also love the ability to toggle different calendars.  I have one calendar for events, one calendar for meal planning, one calendar for finances, that tracks bills, expenses, and paydays. Being able to toggle calendars means that I can see I have a full day of little kid doctor appointments, followed by running around to get older kids to activities, and maybe my husband also has something on his plate, so I can also toggle and see that somebody gets paid that day, and can put dinner pickup on the meal plan. This app seriously is a lifesaver- and allows us to structure our finances and lives efficiently. The only thing I would change is, I wish we could have slightly more users. With the additional space I would add pets for things like vet appointments, monthly flea and tick treatment, etc.

title: Love this app but…
body:
Can you guys PLEASE make it so that if we are adding an event, and we’ve already put in the info once, that the LOCATION INFO would also be copied over? It’s irritating to have to always select the repeated event, but be required to redo the location each and every time. Or be forced to actually COPY the event to other days in order to have the location already in. 

There are so many great things about this app. It’s made sharing my calendar with my fiancée much easier. But the problem listed above is quite annoying with how often I have to put in events I’ve done previously and cannot just put them in quickly due to having to search, or if I have no internet when I put them in, I need to remember to go back and put the location data in later.

title: Date selection
body:
Often times, I have to edit my event almost immediately because, let’s pretend it is January 1, and I want to add somebody’s birthday to my calendar for January 22.

So I advanced my calendar to January 22, and I tap on that date and then tap on add event. I type in the title and select all day. Then, because I was looking at January 22 in the first place, I don’t select that date during the entry process. So it appears on January 1.

I have to go back in and edit the event to change it from January 1 to January 22. 

I would love it if the app would allow me to tap on January 22 and add the event immediately to that date.

I think that’s my only complaint.

title: The Most Helpful
body:
I have been looking for a new family calendar for several months now after using a different one for over a year. While the other calendar was helpful, I found it to be quite basic for what I needed and lacked any design to be aesthetically pleasing— it was boring. After trying out a few, including TimeTree, I have found that TimeTree is the most functional for what I need. I currently have 2 calendars in rotation with TimeTree: 1 for my immediate family, and 1 for my mom’s cancer treatment and support team. It has been so helpful to coordinate so many appointments and schedules and the beta calendar decorations function is sooooo fun and adorable. My only suggestion would be to have even more calendar decorations! A Home decoration and a Pet decoration would be so helpful for scheduling chores that aren’t cleaning or grocery shopping, and for vet or grooming appointments.

Love this app, just get it already

title: My daily calendar
body: I absolutely love this app and use it 24/7 to plan everything. I have multiple different calendars with different groups of people which I love. However, I wish there was a bit more to the app. For one, I would love to be able to access my calendars from my MacBook, but it is currently only available for iPhone and Apple Watch which is unfortunate. I also wish the events overlapped, so I can see where people’s events conflict with one another visually and how long they are from a viewpoint rather than just reading the timeframes. I know it’s available in the premium version, but there are free calendar apps that have this feature which have tempted me to switch over to them. Just a thought! I also hope to see more color options added to the tags or maybe even a color picker to better customize every specific event. With so many people in one calendar, there needs to be more tag slots to label events. Otherwise, I absolutely love the app and its usability !!

title: My lifesaver. No joke.
body:
Hi guys! I normally don’t give reviews, but this is a special occasion. I am a BUSY busy girl. Plagued with a strong case of ADHD and not enough time on my hands, this app helps me keep it together! I have a few calendars that help me separate my categorized events: work, gym, band, and my personal one. The work one I have shared with the coworker I carpool with. That way, we know the days we can ride together and the days we’ll have to make other plans! For the gym, TimeTree makes it really easy to adjust days that I previously scheduled but need to change to work around other things. I can also put in notes of what I have to do that day, wether it be running times or gym lifts! With my band, I schedule around three male musicians; it allows us to keep show days, practice days, and studio days communicated with everyone! Lastly, I put doctors appointments and scheduled time with family and friends in my personal. I never need to keep appointment cards because the appointment is right at my fingertips with this app! That, and I can schedule everything 30 minutes early so I’m on time. What can I say, I know myself. ;) 

Needless to say, if you have a busy schedule like me, have kids who have busy schedules, or just need a place you can put everything down, this is the best app to do it. I love the formatting, the features, and everything in between. Thank you, TimeTree!

Imagine my surprise this morning when I opened the app after the most recent update and was prompted to allow advertisement tracking. Now there’s ads all over the app… disappointed to say the least

title: Small widget size only
body: I’ve used the app for a looonnngg time but now all of the sudden the largest widget is no longer available. Not sure why they would take that away. It was the beast feature. It showed the whole calender. Now it only shows the day and on the only widget an available. Super disappointed

title: Wont load updated events/comments
body:
In order for me to see updated events and new events posted to the calendar I have to delete the app, re install it, and re log in. 

I will get a notification when a new event is added but then it never shows up in the calendar until I do the steps mentioned above. 

I have emailed support and gotten no reply.

title: Amazing app
body: Love the reminders and different pictures that can be added to make certain events stand out.

title: Good, but some flaws
body: I really enjoy this app, I’ve been using it for about 3 years now and it’s great to share between friends from college to know when they’re coming home, etc.. However, recently there’s been an excessive amount of ads. And I’m very upset there’s no version for iPads!!! Like it’s available, but it’s condensed into the middle of the screen the size of an iPhone. I really wish they’d make it available for iPad users FULL SCREEN!!🙏🏼 please the organizer in me is begging for it

title: Sharing is caring
body: I love this app because I can actually share my calendar with people or have multiple calendars which can be joined by friends or family. I got this specifically for our wedding and afterwards my husband and I kept it since it came in handy with our busy schedules.

title: Amazing
body: This is the best calendar apps for busy families!!!!

title: So useful
body: We needed some way to schedule daycare days/appts/etc once having a baby and WOW TimeTree does not disappoint! Very organized, easy to copy and event to multiple dates, love the chat feature between invitees, and nice that there can be multiple people on the same calendar. We have really enjoyed TimeTree, many similar calendars have a cost and this is all free with TimeTree! Thank you!

title: LOVE this app!
body: This is THE best calendar app I’ve used! I love that the developers actually put effort into updating and maintaining the app! Even with the free version the ads are well worth the wait for such a great app. The only thing I could ever think about asking for is more label colors, but that’s just because I like to separate my events by color to see what all I have! Perfect for seeing your schedule alone, or for sharing it with co-workers, kids, or parents! I cannot thank y’all enough for making such a valuable app for everyone!! It helped a TON when juggling multiple jobs and when needing constant reminders for activities! I also LOVE the widget feature, super easy to see what I have planned out for the week!

title: if timetree has no fans then I’m DEAD
body:
I LOVE TIMETREE. LOVE. I spent a long time looking for a calendar that works for me. I needed it to look good, organize well, and be free. I’ve used TimeTree for 4 years straight and I have never thought about using another calendar. I’ve never had to purchase the premium version for me to be satisfied. 

xoxo a girl who live and dies by her calendar

title: Awful

title: Time Tree

title: Really Intuitive and easy to use!!
body: I’ve been using TT for a few months now and it’s been very helpful. It has several features that help it be more efficient and streamlined. Many different ways to customize events and keep everyone and everything organized, even the ability to add pets as their own profile! While there is a paid version it doesn’t feel like I’m missing out on any helpful features by not getting it. My only gripe is I wish they had more customization for the color labels. Don’t get me wrong they have several options but I wish I could make custom colors for labels, I would even go as far as to upgrade to premium for it!

title: Love this Calendar
body:
I would say you don’t hear much about TimeTree, but this app is pretty amazing. It does have a subscription but it’s optional and even without it the app is incredible. You can put in as many events as you want, they can repeat, you can share a calendar with someone, it’ll have notifications to remind you, and it even has different colors if you want to color code. The only thing I would add is that it doesn’t work well with Opera, and the colors are limited. Overall, TimeTree is my go-to calendar. 
Highly recommend!

title: Best calendar ever
body:
I have been using TimeTree for over 2 years now and I have to say it’s my favorite. I can color code everyone so I know who has appointments based on organization alone. I can also set up notifications inside my events so I know what’s coming a couple of weeks out. And those adorable calendar stickers are my favorite way to decorate my calendar. I love that my extended family and babysitters can be invited to my calendar so everyone is on the same page. 
My husband and I are both retired veterans with 2 young children so the color organization and early notifications y’all set up makes my life so easy. Thanks for making something so awesome!

title: 100% loving this app but one thing…
body:
I absolutely love time tree! I can share a calendar with my parents about after school events and set up my class schedule so they know when to call me. I have multiple calendars I have with my friends and my siblings to share our work schedules. It’s amazing and I rave about it to all my friends as the best calendar app I’ve ever used. The only thing that could make this app better is adding a function to merge calendars already made in the app. I was trying to see if I could do that earlier and couldn’t find anything. Please please please add this function if you can because I’d fall even more in love with your app if y’all did🫶 
Thanks for reading have a good one.

title: Impressive
body: This calendar app has so much functionality to it and I’m still finding cool gems to this day! I love the shareability and quick syncing across multiple devices/users. As well as being able to add multiple calendars with different users involved in their respective calendars. So my business calendar is separate from my family calendar, I can view both at once, and family members don’t have to see my business schedule! The free version offers so much of what I need, and honestly other calendars have paled in comparison to what TimeTree can do!!

title: I Love It But …
body: This has been my ride or die calendar app since I discovered it while I was in college. I love that I can have multiple different calendars, I love that I can invite members to view my calendar, and I love the look of the app! This would be a 5 star review if they didn’t take away one of the most important features! I like having a Widget for the app on my home screen so I can see what event is coming next without needing to open the app. Yesterday I noticed that the Widget was not opening the app like normal when I clicked on it and had to manually search for the app itself. I didn’t think anything of it until today when I noticed that the Widget was stuck on yesterday’s date and said “no more upcoming events”. I thought maybe the Widget was glitched so I deleted it from my home screen but when I went to add it back I noticed that there were no options for TimeTree Widget’s at all. I’m not really sure what’s going on, as my app is up to date and there is no other mention of this issue online, but I’d love for this to be resolved so I can add it back onto my home screen and continue enjoying the app like usual :)

title: Really helpful! (a little cluttered)
body: TimeTree is an amazing app for my family, mainly because we have a very stocked up schedule and with this calendar app, we can track everything we do And if someone asks, can you come to____ You can just pull out your phone and look on TimeTree app to see if you’re free or not to give them an answer right away. TimeTree also includes colored events that can be designated to each person in your family! TimeTree does tend to get a bit cluttered though. Maybe this is just for my family, but we have at least five or six things per day and normally it’s for each person in the family. Anyways, our calendar kind of looks ridiculous because of the amount of space given for each day. Along with that, if you turn on notifications, which you probably should do since it’s pretty much a notification app, your notification center will blow up! Sometimes it’s hard to keep up with! Overall, TimeTree is a really helpful app! And I would 100% get it, especially with a family like mine. It’s also very helpful with not forgetting things, and I tend to forget a lot!

title: Awesome! Suggestion!
body:
This app has a very user-friendly layout and is very easy to use! 
I only have one suggestion.
I have three calendars that I have to put my work schedule into separately and to do so I have to individually type in my work days in each calendar.
Is there a way you could add a feature where you could select more than one calendar to add to? Because it’s a little bit of a hassle to have to go in and individually put in the same stuff. 
If there were a way I could put in my event and then have a “select calendar(s)” button and be able to add it to multiple, that would be great!

Thank you!! Awesome app.

title: Overall A Great Family Calendar, few changes would make it perfect
body:
Migrated from Cozi to Timetree. This app works much better because each user has a dedicated login and can maintain and share family calendars and their own.  Cozi used a shared password which was problematic.  
Would love to see one  major improvements that I miss from Cozi.  

1. Export feed to Outlook so I can overlay personal timetree events on my work Outlook calendar. 

Another major improvement would be to support family plans on iOS. Current subscription is only individual and not enabled for family sharing.  For a family of 5, it would cost hundreds per year. Please enable family sharing so I can subscribe. 

Thanks.

Usually at the start of the day it shows all your event. Then you can change your updates to your preferences. I personally get 1 hour before and 10 mins before notifications.

I have a calendar with 20 people on it for an organization within my school; I was hoping to be able to organize the calendar even further with the colored Event Labels but, I ran out of them. The organization does MANY events and though there are already a good amount of them , it’s the only thing I’ve wished to see more of. But all in all, I really do love this app!!

Then you remember, oh yeah you have that doctor’s appointment you scheduled last year. Good thing you put this in the calendar. I am even able to set reminders to tell me a week, a day, or even an hour in advance of an appointment or event. Worth the time to download and explore.

title: Silent Auto-Renewal, No Reminder, No Accountability
body:
Care.com renewed my subscription and charged me $60 without any advance notice or billing alert. I received no reminder that the subscription was about to renew and no real-time notification when the charge occurred.

This is a classic dark-pattern subscription tactic: rely on users forgetting to cancel, then hide behind “non-refundable” policies when they complain. While this may technically comply with terms, it fails basic expectations of transparency and customer care.

When I contacted support, they admitted no notification was sent — yet still refused a refund. That’s not customer service; it’s policy over people.

In today’s app ecosystem, renewal reminders are standard. Care.com’s decision not to provide them feels intentional, not accidental.

I strongly recommend anyone downloading this app to be extremely cautious, monitor their billing closely, or look for alternatives that respect users and communicate clearly.

title: Clunky Poorly Made App

Again, I cannot stand this app.

I encourage everyone who has been wronged by the company to file complaints with the better business bureau, the FTC, and your local attorney general. You can also ask your credit card company for a chargeback since they refuse refunds even when the platform does not function. 

Our collective action can make a difference and stop this company from preying on families.

title: Dangerous nanny!
body:
Had used Care.com in March 2025 to find a nanny for my toddler here in my home. One of the nannies who applied to the job and that we hired for a few weeks until one day. she failed to show up for a scheduled appt. She didn’t answer our texts. Eventually her husband got onto the texts and shared a bombshell! Our nanny was having a very difficult day emotionally because family court had decided “she would not be able to get her own children back from foster care due to a previous SUICIDE attempt!!!” We canceled our membership and went on our way. Months later in Sept 2025, we started a new care.com membership going to find a new nanny. We had hoped that that one  dangerous nanny had been a fluke, a one-off bad seed who got thru care.com’s background checks. 
Immediately after posting a new caregiver job, one of the applicants was the SAME nanny we reported in March! We couldn’t believe it and cancelled our membership. Even after providing documentation (actual SCREENSHOTS of the conversation with our nanny where she confessed to losing custody of her kids due to a recent suicide attempt and hospitalization), care.com still refused to eliminate her profile or offer us a full refund. As a fellow parent, I want everyone to be aware of these extremely dangerous and risky behaviors.

title: Unreliable service

(no call, no text, no notification) at 8:32 AM with a generic message indicating our care was canceled. No explanation, no apology, no follow up.

I’m not sure what’s going on, but my account access has disappeared and I’m paid up till Mar 2026. I need to use the app and can’t get help.

title: App not functional
body: Paid for subscription and tried posting a job, but it “paused” the job and would not let me post it. DO NOT USE! It is a scam. They will not give me a refund either

title: Meh
body: The app used to be great. Now there’s no desktop version, you have to pay more, and it’s harder to navigate.

title: BRING BACK THE DESKTOP VERSION
body: i’m a caregiver and the app is fine for a short look through of job postings but going into depth requires A LOT of patience. the app constantly refreshes and glitches showing the wrong postings from what was clicked over and over. you can no longer delete messages which just adds to the glitching bc the app is overloaded with postings. the desktop version was smoother and so much easier to navigate and clear out. this app is just tedious

title: App needs serious work
body: The app has functions that make sense however it is SUPER buggy. Links don’t work 90% of the time, there are major lags, I am constantly having to reload the app and often times receive errors making me not even want to use it. It’s high cost for such a buggy app

title: The subscription is too expensive!!!
body: This app is too glitchy, too poorly run for it to be as expensive as it is!! Also, no refunds!! Crazy.

title: Green checkmarks
body: Once a family sees an application, there should be green checkmarks. These checkmarks are so difficult to see. There should be a better way to know when your application has been read.

title: Hard to use and very expensive
body: If you’re not paying for premium the app is essentially useless, even then it’s incredibly hard to use, you can’t post for things one day after the other.  Horrible app.

title: You just buy membership
body: You have to pay for a membership to be able to actually find a caregiver. You can post that you need a caregiver but if somebody responds, you cannot message them unless you upgrade and pay. They have different plans and it’s any where from $14-$40 a month.

title: So glitchy
body: I have had the worst time using this app. So many functions just don’t work. It could be so great if the app just worked. Tech support has been awful with the person erasing my profile. I still use it because there’s not really alternatives. But I’m floored that it’s got 4 out of 5 stars on here. Maybe it’s just me?

title: Frustrating app
body: This app is very poor and buggy. For a sleep-deprived and overwhelmed mom, it is quite frustrating.

title: Great Service, Rotten Application
body: I’m glad care puts all the time it does into background checks and safe communication, but the app is quite bad. There is no landscape mode for phone or tablet and, worse yet, if you type a long message or a message with a web address, it simply deletes all you content and says “unable to send message.” It also does not support attachments. How are we supposed to provide detailed instructions to our caregivers?

title: private duty
body: hard to use

title: A fraudulent company!!
body: They closed my account without giving me any reason. I paid for 3 months in advance and only used the service for 12 days. I filed a dispute against the company. I do not recommend you use this application at all.

title: No service provided
body: I tried to get someone to watch my puppies at my house.  No one ever came to my house. No one confirmed they were coming, so I cancel my account, I thought.  Now they are trying to charge me.

title: Paid additional for a report, then denied access to it
body: On top of the membership fee, you have to pay for any type of report you would like to see on the care professionals. I paid additional for a background check and then was denied access to seeing it after being charged. I reached out to the company more than a dozen times requesting for my issue to be escalated only to receive email saying my case was resolved and closed. My case was never resolved. TERRIBLE customer service

title: Money maker App
body: Very unpleasant experience. Expensive and lazy babysitters. No security guarantee. They charge \$20 more than the price of App subscriptions . The App don't show the contacts right after subscription was paid , but they hold the payment on the credit card before showing available nanny . The App don't refund the money even if the subscription is canceled just a minute later.

title: Unfortunate customer service
body: When you’re already dealing with an ailing senior, their lack of good customer is concerning. You get generic copy and pasted emails if it is not AI. They really don’t care if you find anyone, even if you’ve exhausted your search on their service or not as long as you give them your money. Disappointing for a service called “Care”. You tell them about very low rated caregivers and they tell you to just filter the search, so they don’t care about their quality control  either. Cool.

title: Policy
body: I enjoy this app because it has been helpful finding babysitter/tutor. As a mother I’m concerned with the policy of posting a pic of myself. I’ve noticed once women see that I’m attractive they become insecure and no longer interested in helping. I have more luck finding people when I don’t post a pic of myself and it’s no coincidence..it could be racism but I think it’s also insecurity.

title: Fake leads
body: It’s so obvious that there are LOTS of fake leads on here, and no actual luck finding anyone. A fake profile will reach out, set up a time to meet with you the following week, and then cancel the day-of. Save your money!!!!!

title: Definitely a scam
body:
Do not waste your money on care.com
They will take your money to cancel your account. 
You have to be a member to read messages you receive once you become a member they cancel your membership and give no explanation and they do not refund your money.
Modern way of conning shame!

title: Lots of people but their rates are asinine
body: First off, you have to pay almost $40 for a “premium” subscription just to message anybody. Don’t know why they call it premium when it’s the basic minimum requirement to use the service. And most everyone charges by the hour and 2, 2.5x minimum wage so unless you’re rich and looking to hire a full time nanny or a one-off date night babysitter the services offered are completely unaffordable for the vast majority of people. The only reason I give it 2 stars instead of 1 is that there are actually a huge amount of people working on here compared to Urbansitter which I get a discount on but didn’t have a single person in my area. I ended up finding an in home daycare off platform because the prices on this app were too expensive and I had to waste $35 just to find that out. And they have the nerve to charge a hidden service fee as well which other platforms like Taskrabbit do not, it’s built in to the rate.

title: Horrible
body: Unable to login after each update... will attempt to login with Apple, Google, I get your emails yet. It tells me the app does not recognize my email???

title: App Update/ Developer Response
body:
Went on the app this morning and a message pops up say, “it’s that time again, update app”. Once I hit update it takes me to the App Store. I then select open and it’s just taking me on an endless ride to update. It will not update anything and I can not get into the app to respond to applicants.

The above was my previous issue and I deleted the app and reinstalled and no longer had the issue. I will say, I like the developer’s response time to issues brought forth. Thank you for that. So to all that may be having any app issues, delete then install once again.  I will also say, I have found great sitters with this app. It has been useful in my life. Give a little grace, everything is a work in progress.

title: Defective App
body: The app does not install or update. It is like”Ground Hog Day” continuously asking you to hit “update”. Somebody better get on this ASSp and fix the defective code.

title: Payed for it to not work
body: Overall its a decent app & serves it purpose. But functionality isn’t working as needed for $100+ per year. Direct contact messages wont send. Can’t send a message and include existing posting. Cant even send a message with a new posting. On top of all - I can’t use the app now because its forcing me to update the app, but there is not an update available! Within 24 hours of paying I’ve run into all these problems. Needs fixing asap!

title: Update app
body: I open the app and it says “update app” so it takes me to the App Store with nothing obvious to click and update. Click “open” for the app and then rinse and repeat. I’m going in circles and there’s no update. Make updating simple. I’ve reached out and am awaiting a response.

title: Forced to update now app won’t open
body: I was instructed I needed to update app, but now every time I try to open the app it still says I need to update but it already is so now I am locked out of the app at a time when I am setting up a new babysitter.

title: Issue with App!!!
body: App says I need to update to use but there is no updated version available on IoS store. Will update review of corrected.

title: App doesn’t update
body:
Application is constantly being updated which means it won’t allow you to download for use. Happens for a few days at 
a time. Makes it hard to stay in contact with Nannys

title: Update?
body: The app claims it needs an update but is unable to update.

title: No update!!
body: Every time I open app it says to update the app. But there’s no update!!! More I can’t open the app at ALL!!! PLEASE HELP!! I’m paying for a subscription and can’t access it!!

title: Bad software & careless product
body: Always for other issues with app and easy for scammers to create profile and fool people. You want to hire one person you found but you cannot reach them directly for child care. Only for housekeeping etc. better to find an agency.

title: Soooo many bugs
body: It’s crazy that we have no choice but to use the app and the app is soooo bad. There’s a bug every day and today it’s insisting I update the app… I already did! There’s no update available but yet I can’t use the app because it thinks I need to update. So I guess I can’t talk to the woman who is supposed to watch my son tomorrow!! Yay. Would be nice to have the desktop version as an OPTION. Especially when the app is so bad.

title: Job rejection
body: I keep posting a nanny position and it is continually rejected with no indication as to why. Very annoying.

title: Scam
body: Hello my name is Jayona, I was looking for some caregivers to care for my 1 year old son! You have to pay for service ( which is fine ) but I accidentally clicked the yearly plan which was $155. I haven’t connected or used a caregiver on this app. I then contact there customer service and they were giving me the runarounds. Then after 1 and an half of week waiting I still haven’t received my refund then then two weeks came by , they told me that my account is not eligible for a refund this is bull crack. You have me waiting this long just to tell me that! I WILL BE DELETING THIS APP this is a scam!

title: fake
body: I paid the 19.99 and then they wouldn't let me in anymore.

title: Sketchy Profiles / Terrible Booking System
body:
I have used care.com in the past and done all of the legwork to interview and connect individually with tons of people are varying skill and ability when I was hiring a longer-term nanny position. I had to wave through tons of what seems like fake profiles and sketchy people. It was overpriced, but it worked.

Recently, I needed a babysitter and took a chance with the app and their booking system is totally broken. I got two terrible confirmed babysitters. They both canceled using a canned message within 48 hours of accepting. I highly doubt that these were even real people with experience. The last one canceled 30 minutes before I was supposed to leave the house so it was too late for me to find someone else. This was a super important event to me and I’m so furious with the bad service from care.com.

title: Don't waste your money
body: Quite possibly the biggest scam in the app Store. Roughly 90% of the profiles are for caretakers that don't exist.  They are taking advantage of the people that need help the most. Was a member for just a few hours…tried to get a refund and refused to help me.  System will not let me delete account… customer service refused help

title: Fees fees fees
body: Users must pay $23+ for their own background check, and care.com takes a portion of the earnings from actual service providers. Care.come effectively charges the customer and the provider.

title: Love it
body: Care.com had been a lifesaver for me and my family for the past 10 years . We really love it . Have found a lot of great people using this app .

title: App is broken
body: App will not allow you to shut the app down on an iPad. Simply reopens each time.

title: Why Only Portrait Mode???
body: I’ve been waiting for years for this app to be updated so I can use it on my iPad in landscape mode.  Out of hundreds of apps, it’s the only one I have that won’t shift.  How difficult can that be for you?

title: App bugs
body: I am not able to read messages and when I go to upgrade nothing happens. Very frustrating!

title: Buggy app, poor customer support
body: I kept getting charged despite canceling my subscription within a month. I never got payment confirmation emails or cancellation confirmation emails and when I reached out to customer support, they were just repeating talking points from their handbook.

title: Help needed and found
body: I find their people to be quality people and we have had great luck finding good people here.

title: The free option is basically useless
body:
You’ll need to pay to view the message from the caregivers. Basically this means the free option of this app is useless.

I understand the company needs to make money but the user experience is not that good. Feel like tricked

title: Can’t get into my messages.
body: Slow app and no access to messages once upgraded

title: Missing critical features
body: New messages don’t show up and can’t select a date for care

title: Very glitchy
body: I paid for the membership and they blocked the only nanny I reached out to. I can’t send message, and I can’t even read her message that she sent me. Tried everything and reached out to customer care many many times, and they told me that they on working on an update and marked my issue as resolved. Still can’t message her :)

title: Another Review
body: Just dropping in again to reiterate how awful of an experience I’ve had with this app. Spent another 30 minutes trying to set things up and just get blocked at every turn. Not user friendly by any stretch. Just go to FB marketplace and do your due diligence. Much better than this hassle.

title: Glitches out every time
body: Half the time hitting the hire button does nothing. User interface is not intuitive, 2-3 ways to do everything. Silver lining is that it does actually connect you with local care givers.

title: Never again
body: Honestly it’s just dumb and I’m not gonna explain I tried contacting them but they are not professional

title: No longer usable
body: This service has become unusable. You can no longer contact sitters without paying for a booking first. I have found that the sitters with many good reviews do not respond to job requests and find myself flooded by messages from sitters with no reviews whom I am not interested in hiring (even though I never contacted them). I will not return to Care.com again.

title: Can’t log in
body: As title say, can’t log in. I tried entering password and login through verification code (passwordless), both times it just shows blank page, no further action.

title: Very unstable, basically unusable
body: App constantly freezes and crashes. Unfortunately it’s not isolated, as the same thing happens on my partner’s iPhone 16. Forced to use the website only.

title: I do not recommend this platform
body:
The only way to use this platform is by upgrading to their premium membership. Once there, you’re inundated by automated messages from sitters who don’t respond back to you because they’re likely inundated by automated messages and listing as well.

You will have better luck searching for help on Facebook.

title: Still waiting for the Enhanced background check, one month later
body: Purchased the Premium Membership and the Enhanced Background Check so that I could hire a nanny for my child. Requested the Enhanced Background Check on June 16, a month ago. Called Customer Service and they “elevated” the case, two weeks ago. Still nothing. No case number, no email communication. It seems as though they have no interest in delivering what they sold us. I should have read the other negative reviews before subscribing.

title: Have to pay $39.99 to read messages
body: They need to be more upfront about this. When you create a profile and post a job, you can open your first few messages from caregivers, but you have to “upgrade to pro” for $39.99 per month, auto-renewing suscription, to view or respond to any messages after that.

title: Banned without Reason
body: Care.com’s cancellation policy is really challenging. I was a top sitter and tutor in my area for years with hardly any difficulties with parents outside of minor miscommunication with scheduling. I’m an SLP with lots of experience and this platform was instrumental in keeping up in the slow times during the summer. One day, my profile was banned for life without reason and their policy is that they can do that to anyone at anytime without reason. I can’t use the platform any more and have no rationale provided on what happened. All of my connections and connections were lost. It’s very disappointing

title: PAYMENT ISSUE
body: I hired a girl from their site she canceled an HOUR before expected to be there. I request my payment to be returned to me (140.00) and they cant refund me until the sitter accepts the payment?? *Use at your own risk*

title: Why can’t I make an account to baby sit
