body: I have zero kids I just want to branch out as a babysitter and I can’t because it’s telling me to make an account for my “kids”

title: app incredibly glitchy, customer service is useless
body: My app keeps glitching and not allowing me to message my booked caregiver. I’ve reached out to the “contact us” link, but it only works some of the time and when I finally connected with someone she did not actually fix my issue.

title: Messaging Issues
body: super frustrating experience with posting requests from my cell (full screen did not load, continued error message) and unable to contact/message care givers directly.

title: Great concept, poor execution
body: This app offer is so many amazing opportunities for people and families but whoever designed it doesn’t know anything about development they are terrible because this app is so hard to use, but it could be better. It’s almost like if they just tried a little harder it would work better lol I don’t know.

title: Creepy
body: There’s not really a way to know if the sitters applying for your job are real or not. We had one get all our info and then ghost us and now I can’t stop thinking what if that was a ploy to get my address!? I reached out to Care and no one ever responded so I canceled my subscription and am making sure to set the security alarm!

title: Not working as they advertised
body:
I paid for the premium membership, but the app and website don’t work as they advertised. The benefit of the premium membership includes message any caregiver. But I can’t message anyone. Even after someone accepted my request, I still can’t send message. After I tried a few times, the contact button disappeared. I want to send message before I hire a caregiver, but I just can’t message them. The only option for me is to pay first. So disappointed. They didn’t provide the service they promised. They break the contract, but they just don’t give refund. That’s how they do business.

Update: finally I got refund after reaching out to them again. Change to 4 stars because of the customer support.

title: Great site
body: Have met such wonderful individuals who have been amazing caregivers

title: Money hungry
body: On top of paying for the subscription you have to book a person which involves putting a hold on your card in order to even message a babysitter. Parents should be able to contact a person before having to book them.

title: Buggy
body: I click childcare then nanny and it leads me to dog sitters.

title: Terrible App
body: This app is terrible. The service is great, but that is completely un functional on multiple levels. Literally can’t even use it when I need it. And it’s happened multiple times for various reasons. This time I can’t create a job, last time the job disappeared when I already hired someone, the time before that the messages wouldn’t update..

title: Same slow app
body: Nothing changed

title: Frustrating
body:
I used an older version of the app. I spent a lot of work trying to navigate.  I had a few people responding and the history from last time I needed to hire. I had a paid subscription. Now I am forced to start over with a new email?!?.
All the work I have already done is gone because the app crashed.
It worked this morning.

title: Used to be good
body: Now it wants to verify old accounts (15 years old), so I paid the $42 and THEN it said to send it read messages (the Reason to pay!) you had to submit them your verification info, INCLUDING the barcode (huge amounts of personal info is encoded there - WAY more than the front of the card!!), AND wants your microphone enabled for some reason… so if an image of your DL isn’t bad enough.. yeah, identify theft here… everything you need to open accounts in my name!!   It costs me $60 to sue them for my $42 back!! I’ve used this in the past 10-15 times to hire people, now it is cr** deleting it!!

title: Amazing App for caregivers!
body: I love how easy it is to find caregivers for almost any project you need help with. It’s always reliable and they take the necessary precautions and measures to ensure you are receiving the best care and service possible.

title: Quality Applicants
body: This app helped us find our dream nanny and it’s helping us find her replacement (she’s moving). We feel a peace of mind going through this app vs. finding people through other means. Totally worth the $75.

title: DO NOT PURCHASE
body: This company’s app is horrible. The system lags like crazy, when you purchase any form of subscription you have to be verified before you can use the app (which they spot tell you). It’s not a good mobile application. Go online and find another company.

title: Critical System Flaws in the Booking Confirmation Process Must Be Addressed
body:
For example, even when a booking is marked as “Confirmed,” it doesn’t actually function properly.
It’s very easy for sitters to mistakenly press the “Confirm” button, and often, they themselves don’t even realize that the booking has been confirmed.

Moreover, once a sitter presses “Confirm,” the requester is blocked from ever messaging or booking any of the other sitters they previously contacted.
This is a critical flaw in the system that must be addressed immediately.

Since Care.com charges users for the service, this should absolutely be fixable — but perhaps due to its position as a market leader, the company has grown complacent.

title: Payment not reflected
body: Payment not reflected on my account after being charged. This app is such a waste of time.

title: You have to pay.
body: After entering your information you’ll be prompted to purchase a subscription so this is not as advertised as an easy tool to find caregivers.

title: Too many problems
body: Too many times I will apply for a job, and after hearing back from the family, I am unable to even view their profile or posted job. This has been the case for a while but before I could atleast just log in on my computer and check. The fact that that is no longer and option to check these things online is ridiculous and infuriating.

title: Not Fair
body: Care Givers must pay to search and apply to jobs, but the care giver families that are looking for help don’t have to pay first. So what happens they start the process, set up an account, at the end is where the payment comes in, if they are not that interested they don’t pay, but the job still gets posted.  They cannot answer the post that all these care givers that paid and looking for work apply to and waiting to hear. If you’re a premium member, it tells you that they are not actively accepting applications at the moment. So tonight I applied for 10 jobs and 9 are not actively accepting applications.  Just not fair to use care givers that pay to look for work.  They should make the families pay before they post.

title: Contact
body: I pay the background and now don’t let me login if I don’t pay monthly , I paid and ask me pay again and again 😡

title: DO NOT BUY - NO SUPPORT AND WOULDNT RESTORE PURCHASE
body: I purchased the app for a year and I cannot use it. I contacted support - radio silence. Asked Apple for refund - denied. Appealed right now. Next step is contact credit card. Super frustrating to lose 150.

title: need to pay subscription, very  unfortunate
body: This app used to be free, but it now needs subscription in order to post babysitting requests.

title: Aggravating experience
body:
The app requires a premium subscription to do anything, even to message potential caregivers. I caved and subscribed for $40/mo and still can’t do anything helpful. 

I tried to list a new job but it wouldn’t let me without a credit card on file. I tried to enter my credit cart and hit some kind of glitch that wouldn’t accept my card. So stonewalled there. 

People are DMing me about nannying for my baby (a job I was able to post previously), but I still can’t reply until I verify my account, requiring my phone number even though it didn’t require it before. 

I’d like to reach out to babysitters to get to know them and maybe schedule a meet and greet of some kind,  but I can’t do that without listing a job with a date and time that multiple people can apply for: arduous.

So even paying for premium, I’ve yet to make any progress on my nanny search which is very frustrating to me. 

I work in product development, and I do not recommend this product. I find it sneaky (not straightforward about what it requires or offers) and frustrating. I’m turning to a local facebook group in hopes of finding a good sitter on a free platform.

title: Good place to find an independent caregiver for home health
body: It was easy finding someone to take care of my dad using this platform. It takes all the stress and time away.

title: Not working
body: This app is so frustrating I immediately got the fee deducted from my account but I’m unable to message people and day on I couldn’t hire anyone the payment won’t go through! Waste of time and money. I still don’t have a sitter

title: Not reliable.
body: Paying monthly for a service that barely works. When its time to hire someone now i can’t sign in or message nor receive messages.

title: Worst app ever
body: No customer service, waited several mins to get match with a rep, it never happened. The UX is really bad as well. I would recommend urban sitter, it’s a much better service.

title: Unable to send messages
body: You’re able to author a message to a caregiver from the app, but there are no buttons or anything to send it. The keyboard can’t be dimished to see the full screen either.

title: No support
body:
I am literally trying to pay them their yearly membership and because of the glitching app, payment errors, and robot-only support I can’t. 

I’m stressed enough that I’m looking for respite care, using an app shouldn’t be additional stress on me.

Oh well, I guess I’ll try one of the other guys.

title: App is very glitchy, impacts ability to use a great service
body: We have hired several care givers through this platform and have no complaints about the service at all but the app is super glitchy and has made communication with potential hires difficult. On this most recent update, my job posting disappeared along with all of my archived conversations. Customer service is difficult to get ahold of.

title: I wish I kept the older version
body: There is a glitch with this new version that I can’t leave a message for anyone. I can type it out, but there’s no way to actually send it.

title: Vulture - app
body: I do not mind having to pay for the services to connect with people. But once that hefty fee is paid, it should be up to me to write and pay people directly, without exorbitant vulture-fees. Some jobs add 20-30% fees, that is just stupid. Thankfully there are alternatives such as SitterCity which I HIGHLY recommend.

title: DON’T HIRE!
body: I hired Adysun J. To care for my two dogs for a day and a half, Thursday evening and all day Friday. She did everything she was supposed to Thursday, but Friday she never checked on my dogs never fed my dogs. I have cameras inside with live view and I saw my husky German get up on my sink to try to get water because she never came and took them out nor fed them. I had to call my aunt and she checked everything and said they were never cared for!!! I paid for a subscription just to have a conversation with her and she was paid for both days even though she didn’t do her job!! She neglected my dogs!! I sent an email and got no response back but canceled my subscription!! Do not use this app. Do not trust these people!!!!

title: Impossible to decline jobs
body: Used this app a few years ago to find my current family and it worked great. Now I can’t even decline jobs without them popping back up immediately. I continue to get the same several job postings that don’t even match my availability.

title: Fake Reviews Allowed
body: I will be leaving indefinitely after a parent was able to lie in their review and Care will do nothing to remove it or give a chance for me to respond to it. Don’t spend your money here!

title: Lacking Rating on Cancellation Rate
body: The app generally does what it is intended to do and have some good sitters. My only complaint is that there is no indication of last-minute cancellation rate of sitters or any repercussion on that behavior, where sittter just unexpectedly cancel one hour before the scheduled time due to some ‘personal emergency’, which has disrupted my schedule a few times.

title: Horrible service
body: I renewed my subscription which showed in my bank and subscription list in my account, even on their app but did not go through on their end. The upgrade was not working. I then spoke to 3 different customer service reps and they all tried to put the blame on me for it not working. One even tried lying and said it was due to a typo in my email (I even sent a screenshot showing her everything was correct). The app itself isn’t the worst to find someone to hire, but I will never be returning to this platform again after multiple horrible experiences. None of the customer service reps I spoke to wanted to do their job.

title: Can't do a broader search
body: How stupid is this app!  I am looking for someone that speaks Mandarin Chinese but it will only let me search by local zip code.  I can't do a national search for a unique talent.  Completely idiotic!

title: Eh
body:
The app itself has issues. Showing notifications when there are not any, glitching, not able to cross our caregivers you have no interest in, no clear way to see if you’ve messaged someone before etc. 
let alone the people on there- you could message 50 people and most are not available or don’t get back to you. Not the best when people don’t update info.

title: Frustrating User Experience
body: I attempted to use Care.com to find a house cleaner but was met with a convoluted and frustrating user experience. The sign-up process was unnecessarily complicated, requiring the creation of separate accounts for different services without any integration between them. Each login prompted an intrusive “Are you a human?” check, adding unnecessary friction. The lack of a unified account system and constant verification hurdles reflect a disregard for user convenience. If the initial user experience is this cumbersome, it raises serious concerns about the quality and reliability of the services offered beyond the login screen.

title: WATCH OUT!!
body: I had made an acc and while trying to process to be a sitter they flagged me after taking my money and putting my social I contacted customer service and they “put in a ticket” to review my acc and they just emailed me saying it still wasn’t able to go thru so they banned me for no reason

title: Both sides of their service is a scam
body: They will take money from you and those in your community. It’s not worth it to use them. None of their services are refundable. Even if you happen to misclick or click on something to find out more about the many options they offer while checking out.

title: Not easy to use at all!
body: I have been trying to find a helper for two weeks with no success. The categories are not clear and the candidates are interested in narrow jobs. My money so far has had no return!

title: Scam
body: I cancelled my subscription was charged another 120 dollars and when I contacted them they told me “subscriptions are non refundable” in reply to every time I’d say there was no subscription… scam artists

title: It’s a scam.
body: Honestly what is even the point of this app. You are forced to purchase the plan in order to use it effectively to hire your nanny or babysitter. Otherwise you are completely handicapped. I can’t even message people who applied to my listing if I don’t upgrade are you kidding me. Parenting is hard enough as it is without this frustratingly dumb app. Skip it and save yourself the frustration. FYI I did have the membership for a year too. But guess what, most of the “sitters” are either non responsive or never show. I hav a feeling the company just populates this app with bots.

title: Great App
body: Hired a great housekeeper

title: Dishonest company
body: I subscribed to find a nanny for my family in April of 2024. I had many caregivers apply then never respond or show up for interviews. After many no responses and three no-shows I cancelled the subscription using the app. On my app it said my subscription would be cancelled September 6th. I did not receive an email but this was not a weird experience because I NEVER received an email regarding billing. Payment confirmation or that a bill was due. I had every reason to believe my subscription had ended as I received no emails for over ten months. Now I find out they have been continuing to charge me $40 a month. When I contacted customer support they hid behind their “no refund policy.” I stated I had cancelled and they asked me for a cancellation email copy. Of course I don’t have one. I didn’t even know I was supposed to get one. Why would I? I didn’t get any other email regarding my subscription after the initial one. After requesting to speak to a supervisor to file a formal complaint the customer representative stopped responding. The company has taken over $500 from me. I have been inactive on the app and website since August 2024. The only reason they do not send billing emails or list their subscription on Apple subscription account is to take advantage of families. The company is hoping to do what they did to me. Quietly continue to collect $40 a month and provide no service. This is extremely dishonest and when you try to connect to discuss your issue they ask for evidence they know you won’t have because they don’t send it. I wish I had reviewed the company before subscribing. I will be consulting with a lawyer.

title: Do not recommend.
body: As a caregiver it’s unfortunate to see where this app has gone over the years. You do have to pay for a subscription, if left on basic (free) you are basically prohibited to use any feature possible, let alone share your profile. Also do not go through them for background check, go to your local metro for 1/4 of the cost. It’ll be done in minutes. This app took over 2 weeks for me to even have an update on where the process was. Luckily, it was cancelled. Bad experience, have now chosen to deactivate my profile.

title: Worst Experience Ever
body: Care.com billing is awful! I mistakenly purchased a year-long membership instead of the month-long membership I was seeking (long story involving a keyboard loving toddler). I called IMMEDIATELY to try and correct this expensive mistake and they would not downgrade me or refund me. Most infuriating and unhelpful experience ever. Come to find out, they settled a FTC lawsuit for unethical practices. No surprise!

title: They don’t care about cleaners
body: They should care about cleaners but they don’t, I guess they only want our membership subscription. I paid twice + the anual background check to get jobs in this app, I’m a premium cleaner but I don’t get ANY job, they don’t even let me see the jobs in the area I set up to work, ( because there is an option to set up the area you’d like to work) they only show me a few jobs from 25-30 miles and they are not even in  area I want they  are in the opposite area. They are making tons of money with us the cleaners too, so they should respect us. They have their favorite cleaners so they appears on any search the people do, even though they are not good enough or very experienced they only show them. So sad because I paid a subscription to get jobs

title: Buggy, no support, expensive
body: I’m updating my previous one-star review because they resolved my issue promptly afterward. However, it’s disappointing that I had to leave a public review to get the problem addressed in the first place. Additionally, the inability to edit reviews remains a concern-somewhat ironic since I’m able to edit this one now.

title: They have an AI text problem
body: Overall good service - but Care needs to implement an AI-generated text filter for replies. Part of what I need to know about an applicant is how they communicate, and almost ALL of my care replies from potential caregivers were AI generated (I assume to seem more professional). Communication skills (and language skills) are important and that’s part of a caregiver’s skill set. There are so many AI generated responses now, the platform was functionally unusable for my recent childcare search. A real bummer.

title: Buggy
body: So buggy, doesn’t load all messages, loses messages, just overall poorly built app

title: Milking a dead cow
body: I’ve been using this site and app for many many years and all I can say is the developers are insistent on putting as little effort into revamping the site as possible. It’s slow and clunky and needs a complete overhaul. STOP trying to milk every penny from your customers and actually update the site please.

title: Latest update full of issues
body: Not of fan of the 4/2025 update. Experiencing more issues with communication on the app. Duplicate notifications and sending of messages. Tried to get help and submitted a video of the problem (click on notification or e-mail and instead of opening app, it crashes). I feel the app has worsened over the years. Not worth the subscription price.

title: Great App! But cost, not so great
body: They are great! So far they have connected me with some great Nannie’s. I just think the cost is very expensive.

title: Buggy app and bad user experience - but good caregivers
body: Such a missed opportunity here. Lots of experienced caregivers on the platform but app and website are always buggy. I wish that they made it easier to run a proper hiring funnel using the app by allowing you to create a pipeline and tag applicants. Also create a way better filter so you can search for what you need (I.e. multiple, infant experience etc)

title: Response time is too long
body: Caregivers listed either respond days later or dont respond at all. Can you make sure notifications come through for everyone and maybe mark those that dont respond quickly, they dont want a job i guess.

title: NO
body: They need to make another service other than this one.

title: What is going on?????
body: Can’t even access app! I get nothing!!! I actually pay yearly.  Terrible

title: Nobody answers
body: Nobody applies to my ad.  If they do they are from 100 miles away.  People don’t answer messages I send.  People don’t really want to work.  Housekeepers want to clean homes that are already clean.  Last time I used care.com it was a waste of $ and looks like it will be again.

title: Not a Secure Platform for Families
body: There's no privacy protection for families on this app. I left a review on some caregiver and that person got mad and harrassed me. It's not safe for families to use if we can't even write reviews to share experience without any concerns

title: Overpriced
body: You can’t really use the features without paying a subscription, and they don’t remind you when it’s about to renew. As a person juggling childcare, this is especially unkind. It can be hard to track quarterly payments. I’ve used care.com 2-3 times now and I’ve always been dissatisfied with what you get for the price. The price is too high, I couldn’t get human customer service and the auto renewal is easy to sneak by you. I’ll be looking for other ways to find care.

title: Horrible customer service:(
body: I’ve never actually been able to use their services:/ After paying for their service my husband and I were very excited to book a babysitter for our son, but the system did not register our payment and I was not able to use their site. By the time they fixed the issue I had booked a sitter elsewhere. No apologies or refunds, and all I could get were bots. SO frustrating!

title: App Keeps Crashing
body: When trying to buy a plan, I go to fill out the verification screen to pay, it took Apple Pay, then crashed multiple times. After paying, now it just crashes when opening the app.

title: No jobs
body: You will never get a job on this app.

title: Scam
body: This app is a scam because u have to pay just to chat with caregivers I don’t like it yall need to fix this problem ASAP

title: Great experience!
body: Very timely and reliable, plus my kids loved her! Definitely would recommend.

title: SCAMMED ME
body: They took my money for a subscription &’ background check and didn’t give me access to the app!!

title: App is not usable
body: Tried to set up a quarterly subscription and couldn’t remember my Apple ID password so I created a new one. It took payment as I can see from my statements and I have a receipt but I’m not able to use the app at all. I would like a refund if the app isn’t going to help me after all. And I already deleted and downloaded the app many times. Very frustrated

title: Terrible App & Customer Sevice
body: I received a promo code to come back and use their services which was great timing because I was in need of a nanny. When I went to restore my subscription in the app I wasn’t asked to provide the promo code before authorization of payment but thought it could be inputted down the line. It was not and on top of that I was charged an additional $25 for a service I never agreed to that did background checks. When I reached out to customer service they could not help me because all charges on the app need to be discussed with Apple. Also they said promo codes can only be used on the desktop website not the app. I’m never using this app again but if I do I would just use the web version over this app

title: Great idea, price is a scam
body: The price for the service is a scam, they also act like you need to add background checks when they do them anyway. Absolutely scammed into paying $200 more dollars and I couldn’t even get a refund same day

title: Thankful
body: I’m thankful for this app and how secure I feel using it. I hate that I had to pay to see the responses on the job, but it was worth the money!

title: TERRIBLE COMPANY charging my credit card yearly even though account has been closed since 2023!
body: Impossible to cancel the account!! I am in a continuing dispute with my credit card for the last 18 months. I closed my Apple Card because of this company. They are the worst and continue to charge my closed account! If you sign up with them be warned, it is impossible to cancel even though you complete all the steps required. Stay away!!!

title: They steal your money! Background checks don’t work.
body: I paid for their premium membership bundle which included a $60 up charge for background checks. To date, I haven’t been able to get the background check to work after multiple contacts with customer service. I had to pay out of pocket to a different service to run the background check on time to hire my nanny. Customer service hides behind terms & service while denying refunds for services that don’t work and can’t be addressed in a timely manner. What a scam!

title: Care.com Misleads Users – Your Personal Data Is Never Deleted!
body:
The app provides an option to delete your account, but when you select it, you receive an email stating: “We are unable to complete your deletion request because all personal information that we maintain about you is subject to a legal exception to deletion in accordance with California law.”

Essentially, the delete option appears to exist only for regulatory compliance, but in reality, it does nothing to remove your data. I would think twice before using Care.com.

title: Would not recommend! They stole money from me!
body: I paid the monthly subscription and it came out of my bank account and when I open the app it wants me to restore my purchase when I purchased it yesterday so now I have paid $51 and have not been able to access anything I will be contacting the correct people when I am able to.

title: Cannot have a specific username
body: I got Care to get a nanny for my children, after I created the account on 3/9/2025 and paid $40, I tried to change the screen name to show my wife and I. It said it would not do that so I canceled but it has prompted me to verify my account which has all the same info as when I started. I was able to find a nanny and I just cancelled because I can’t log in and I have only had it 2 weeks.

title: Very buggy
body: Get ready for lots of screens saying “error: something went wrong”

title: From mediocre to surprisingly bad
body: I signed up for the premium membership and after a lot of searching, I did find some legitimate care givers. The problem is the app itself and the business model. You can’t contact care givers without creating a post, which then opens a floodgate of people contacting you.  On top of that, it seems that now I’m being asked to pay up front for the requested time - even though I have a premium membership.

title: Limited functions
body:
Just skip the app and use the website. If someone messages you about a job, you can’t decline them from the app. You can’t favorite their profile from the response, either.

The signup process was shady, too. I canceled immediately to be sure I wouldn’t be charged again (twice as much as I expected) in 3 months.

title: Not usable
body: I’m sure this app works for some users. I unfortunately paid $40 to find a sitter for my baby and quickly got kicked out of the app because it needed to verify my account information? It’s been a few days now and it keeps requesting the same info from me but no solution. I would like to use their services if someone from the app could please help.

title: Ripoff - required premium payment
body: I was trying to explore the app but it required a premium membership just to look at it. There was no free basic option, only monthly or annual premium “family” subscription. I cancelled it within 15 minutes and they refused to credit me back the $40 for the one month which I paid.

title: Not worth if unless you constantly need a new sitter
body: I need a sitter for one night while I’m out of town and have to pay $40 to just message someone back. Total crock.

title: Slow app
body: The service is good, but the app is so slow and buggy. Half the time I can’t access emails on my phone

title: This app needs a COMPLETE redesign.
body:
Here’s the deal. Will it help you find a caregiver for your child? Sure. Is it a complete pain in the ace to do? Well…lets talk about that. 

Let me preface this by saying that I architect and design mobile apps for a living. Whoever designed this app needs to be demoted and I don’t say that lightly. The basic things like messaging people is a complete mess, almost as id there’s two separate inboxes depending on how or what screen the message was sent. Also, some of the profiles of the care givers are so old that they aren’t even checking the app. There’s also no way to know if you have messaged someone to inquire about availability without offering them the job. I have also talked to caregivers that we interview or hire, and they say that they don’t even get notified when we send them messages half the time. I could go i to a lot more detail…but they need a good QA team to dive into the issues.

title: Care seeker
body: I have found care.com to be very helpful in finding, and maintaining care for my loved ones. The filters are very nice once you understand how to use them, otherwise that can be a bit of a stumbling block to having access to possible workers…my advice is to chat with the business office if this happens and they can help. I have used this service over the years and recommend it. What would be nice is if there were an easier path to find the contact information for the business office or to “chat” with them, That is not easy to navigate to, but is a good place to go to broaden your search capabilities/access to more caregivers.

title: Profile glitch
body: Just started using the app.  As I was updating my profile, when I would click on the pets section it would take me to the home section.  Makes me wonder if I should dump the app after all the negative reviews I just saw.  Need to make sure EVERYTHING works as advertised before developers push the app out.

title: Disappointing
body: Not a single available person. Have to click on each profile then says not available. Huge waste of time.

title: Beware
body:
I have given Care.com several tries, wanted this to work, but it didn’t and has cost me - A LOT.
Beware - if you sign up, they will trap you in a subscription. You cannot cancel from the app. Difficult to cancel and even when I have chatted to cancel, it doesn’t work. Meanwhile, charged for $50/no for several months. They will hide behind the Terms of Use as to the reasons why they can’t refund the money. Such poor and disappointing experience and sub-par customer service.
I have escalated and am told a supervisor will review. I asked for an email to write too. Nope, “don’t call us, we’ll call you”. Zero confidence my concern will be addressed. They have no problem taking your money while they make it difficult to cancel and set you free. 
Most of us are busy care givers in search of help. Guess what we don’t have? Time to play games, and money to pay for a useless service

title: Completely Crashing
body: This app is not working AT ALL. It’s crashing my phone every time.

title: Very buggy
body: I downloaded the app to find a nanny. But writing up a post, saving/editing favorites and updating my profile were a nightmare. My photo (which meets the guidelines) was denied but I can’t change it. I put my child’s DOB in and the app displays a very incorrect age group. And lastly, my new job posting has old data when I view it that isn’t there when I go into edit. Do you guys even test??

title: Beware of auto renewal
body: They get you to sign up for monthly or annual services that are difficult to cancel. No refunds even if you do not use the services. Buyer beware.

title: Horrible app
body: Crashes constantly, takes forever to load a page when it’s not crashing. It’s the bane of my existence when I need to spend time on this app to hire a nanny. Literally nothing about this app works smoothly or correctly all the time. I generally avoid using it as much as possible. Cannot believe it’s still in business, it’s so bad.

title: Crashed constantly
body: I’m happy with the selection of available help but the app crashes ALL THE TIME! Just trying to message potential hires it crashed out 4 times in 10 minutes, please make this app more functional because it would be very useful!! It’s a bummer to spend $40 and barely be able to send a message.

title: App doesn’t recognize my apple purchase
body: I purchased the 3-month subscription through Apple, but the app doesn’t recognize the subscription. Contacted care.com’s support 3 times and received no help. Apple refuses to refund my money.

title: Horrible
body: Charged me $106 instead of the $14.99 option I selected, and the bank won’t let me dispute it.

title: AI GENERATED MESSAGES
body: I use to love this app but I have noticed they use AI generated messages for people to respond. In a babysitter add I had 3 different people say word to word same exact paragraphs in their introduction. Just doesn’t seem a great way to actually see who is qualified when AI is generating their words and have no idea what is real in people’s abilities.

title: Terrible app
body: The app is terrible. Pages take forever to load, notification badges don’t show up appropriately, I can’t update an active job posting or reply to inbox replies without receiving an error. This must be one of the worst apps I’ve used a in a long time. Please fix these bugs, I’ve cancelled my subscription in the meantime

title: Search function is terrible
body: This is about the only gig in town in terms of finding local childcare, but if you are looking for something specific it is very difficult. You are fed the same people no matter whether they are a good fit or not, and there is no way to filter by availability. Because most caregivers are looking for part-time gigs, that means that to find full-time care you have to sift through 10-20 concise candidates to find 1 that is even interested in full-time work.

title: Maybe not the best place to look for caretakers sadly
body:
I’m sure this platform is great for some but for me personally I have already gone through 5 different people just to find a caretaker for my mom.  

Tbh most of them are either unavailable, or don’t respond clearly that indicates that they’re not able to or not interested in taking the job. 

I’ve only gone on this platform to help me find some options to arrange some care for my mom, and I like that I can overlook people’s profiles to find specifically what im looking for 

Rather solely relying on an agency to send someone without knowing much about the person, and having to go through the process of going through multiple candidates

title: I pay for the app
body:
I have had the app for almost four years. In the past two years I have noticed that the system blots out my number when I exchange contact information with potential clients. My understanding is that because I pay premium membership, I can at least give out my contact details to potential clients. Well, not so with Care.com

This month alone, my contact information that I exchanged with two potential clients was blocked after we had exchanged messages back and forth with the clients. Because of this continuing blocking of my phone number I have decided not to renew my membership. What is the point in paying for an appt that wants you to only stay on the platform as a means of communication. Most clients are busy and want to communicate via a regular cell phone.

title: Care.com: Overpriced and Unregulated
body:
I needed childcare fast, so I remembered Care.com. Not sure why, because the app isn’t user-friendly at all. I got the monthly subscription for about $45 but canceled right away—way too expensive just to use the service. The extra fees to Care.com weren’t too bad, but the real issue was the nannies all charging $22 an hour, even with zero experience and bad communication.

Honestly, I’d have no problem paying that if they had an associate degree in childcare or some certifications, but most of them don’t. A lot of profiles start with “I enforce rules” or “I don’t mind setting the tone,” which is weird because their job isn’t to be a parent—it’s to provide a safe, nurturing environment for kids.

I feel like Care.com should have some kind of pricing guideline based on experience and references. Letting random people set their rates based on what others are charging in the area makes no sense. They could even offer training or certifications, and once a caregiver completes them, they unlock a higher pay tier. That would actually make the pricing fair. Right now, it just feels like people are making up numbers, and Care.com is letting it happen.

title: Suddenly closes
body: This app needs an update.  It suddenly closes every 1-2 min, while im trying to respond to possible candidates. It constantly blocks my phone number making interviews difficult. Also the premium app makes it sounds like you have unlimited background checks but you have to pay for each one and they are expensive.

title: HORRIBLE COMPANY
body: I would HIGHLY REFRAIN FROM USING THIS COMPANY AND ITS SERVICE! The absolute WORST customer service and caregivers. Had a horrible experience and would never recommend to anyone. USE URBANSITTERS!!! URBANSITTERS IS BETTER THAN CARE.COM

title: Deceptive
body:
Deceptive billing, fake profiles, difficult customer support.

EDIT: Care.com’s reply is just damage control. Instead of addressing the real issue—misleading billing practices and poor customer support—they post the same generic response to every complaint.
They claim they want to help, yet when I contacted them directly, they refused to issue a full refund for a charge I never authorized. I also requested a supervisor call, which never happened.
Care.com can pretend to be helpful, but their actions speak louder than their scripted replies. Beware of their deceptive billing and unresponsive customer service.

title: This app is not good.
body: The prices are outrageous, next to know customer support, it’s also extremely buggy and doesn’t work half the time.

title: Nice
body: I’m comfortable for now thanks

title: You have to pay to just to have an account
body: I wanted to come on care.com to see if any families would match the services I am looking to provide. Turns out, you need to pay a fee to have an account and you need to have a paid subscription to message any potentials. It’s just frustrating. I’m not going to pay for something that isn’t going to guarantee a match for me. It’s a waste of money!

title: Horrible app
body: Chats don’t load, terrible customer service, background check is trash

title: Rip off
body: I really feel bad for the people that use this app, paying so much money for an app just to be able to use it.

title: Bots everywhere
body: Im convinced I didnt speak to a single real person when I was on here trying to find a nanny. I spent two non-refundable 70+ dollar membership fee and background fee. I would have “bots” contact me interested. Then I’d do follow up with a message and the “person” would reply that they arent available. Yet their profile would say they are available and ready to work. Waste of money and time. How can you charge 70 dollars to even use the app and then there be nothing but Bots!!? Buyers beware!

title: Expensive and a scam
body: I spent the past two weeks contacting people in my price range. Turns out that is not real as they insist on giving a price to do the job. I wanted vacuum and mopping for 2 hours. How hard is that? Secondly, most of them listed don’t reply. Third, if you are trying to do this from out of state- DONT! I had someone lined up and then she found out I was in another state and she blocked me. So bizarre. This is not worth the time or effort - if you need cleaning, go to Merry Maids.

title: App is HORRIBLE !!! And customer service is even WORSE👎🏼👎🏼👎🏼
body: My account was shut down MULTIPLE times. And the app glitches soo much it’s so frustrating. Never using the app again. Not calling customer service which told me they couldn’t tell me why my account was closed and that they don’t have any answers.

title: Not posting jobs correctly
body: Care has been awful.  I can’t find reliable sitters.  Several of them don’t respond.  At times, it doesn’t push the notifications through. Most recently, I posted a job with the hourly rate I was willing to pay only for applicants to see a rate 1.5times higher than what I had posted.  Awful app considering the amount of money we pay for the annual subscription.

title: Annoyed
body: It easy to u subscribe. Also the app makes it hard to right bad reviews . It forces more that one star

title: Unreliable
body: Very disappointed with the applicants in this app as more than 4 of the people identified didn’t show up and provided late notifications. So I wasn’t able to hire anyone from the app. Terrible experience.

title: Wasted money
body: This app returned zero results. Needed housecleaning. Sent multiple inquiries to people. 95% said they didn’t houseclean. And the one that did respond was nowhere near her advertised rate. App needs work for sure.

title: A very helpful site
body: I have used Care.com for about 4 years now.  They have helped me find several reliable caregivers.  I highly recommend them.

title: App is Terrible!!
body: While the service is valuable, the app interface is awful.  Messages do not load, even after force quitting, updating the app, etc.  there is something fundamentally wrong with the messaging function.  I have started to use the web interface instead because it is so awful.  Also, the process for doing background checks is unnecessarily confusing and not seamless at all.

title: Worst App on my phone
body: The service is fine but this is the laggiest, buggiest, bad UX app I think I use. Most days I have to resort to messaging from my desktop bc I can’t get the messages to even load in a normal timeframe. Navigating multiple open job applicants is confusing and there’s no way to archive messages or somehow sort the inbox. Even the way you’re required to set a schedule when the job is flexible is confusing and dumb. I hope they overhaul the app because it’s really awful.

title: Do Not Subscribe
body: Pay for a subscription but cannot message any one or reply to candidates and they keep telling me they will fix. Been 3 weeks of my subscription and they cant fix and wont refund. This is fraud.

title: App crashes
body: This is a great platform to find caregivers and other services however the app is glitchy and often crashes. Very frustrating.

title: No show!
body: Setup an appointment with a house cleaner and she didn’t show up and wouldn’t respond to further communication.

title: Overpriced
body: Only the standard background check is included while criminal and vehicle history are available for additional purchase. It already costs a whole lot and applicant quality hasn’t been great. Also missing guidance on what to ask applicants and how to pick. Disappointing experience for us.

title: So happy with Care
body: This service is wonderful

title: Slow
body: The app spends so much time, buffering, reloading and timing out every time I go to the chat/messages option. It’s quite frustrating and I may as well go on the desktop.

title: Lousy app, lousy content
body: The app is slow, unresponsive, and difficult to use. Beyond that we paid for a subscription to find a nanny. All of the babysitter’s on the site tag themselves as nannies so the search isn’t useful. Support didn’t help either

title: Constantly freezes
body: Navigation and features are nice, however I am unable to use it without having to close and reopen it every time I want to go to a new message or review applicants for jobs posted as it constantly freezes.

title: Glitchy app
body: This app is not easy to navigate, you’re constantly having to close it out and reopen in order to move from one application to another. When reviewing profiles it takes a long time to load and freezes up. Really bad app for something that has to have a paid subscription for.

title: Frustrating app
body: The website for care.com is horrible and this app isn’t much better. It freezes up all the time. So frustrating to use. It seems to be impossible to message a babysitter on the app without creating a job and agreeing to pay for the job through the app.

title: So Slow
body: It’s so, so, slow. It’s so slow that every time I try to load a message I have to close out the app and reopen. I’m paying $40 a month to use this app and it’s been quite painful.

title: So slow
body: This app is so incredibly slow that it becomes unusable. Opening messages take so long that I give up and just go to the website on my mobile. Absolutely worthless.

title: Charging card just to contact a potential baby sitter is absolutely Insane
body: After paying $40 just to use the freakin app. They are requesting a $200+ hold on my card JUST to contact a potential sitter. I’m getting my money back and never using this app again.

title: Predatory Charge practices - use caution
body: I signed up for the premium subscription in summer 2023, every three months. It auto renewed without sending an email until January 2025 when my bank finally flagged it as fraud. Keep in mind, I never found any care help on this site, never hired a single person,  and have not logged in since fall 2023. Again, never any notifications about my subscription still being active. This is clearly a scam on care.com’s part. I recommend that you do not sign up for the premium membership. Be careful out there, parents.

title: This app is trash
body:
This is by far the worst app in the App Store. 
It is not user friendly, every page takes forever to load, or it crashes.

title: Terrible app experience
body: This is probably the worst app experience I’ve ever had! It’s incredibly slow, messages take forever to load, notifications are out of whack, and it times out constantly. If I wasn’t desperate to find a nanny (and recognizing there aren’t many platforms) I would have deleted this app within days of downloading it. It’s honestly almost unusable.

title: Premium Subscription
body: Do not, I repeat DO NOT let your account be idle. I created my account a year ago, looking for care for my two month old son and didn’t use the sit to book help because a family member looks after him. I haven’t logged into the site since until I noticed a charge on my account for a premium subscription that I never opted for. I reached out to care customer service about the matter and was informed that the matter would be resolved but days later and still no resolution. Beware!

title: App constantly crashes
body: Worst app ever made. So hard to go through profiles and messages. It crashes every 5 min or so. Always been a pain to use.

title: $40/mo for a glitchy app
body: I hate opening and using care.com and wished I could have tried it first via a free trial because the bugs are insane!!! You can hardly open it and view messages without it glitching and freezing. I expected a much better experience after paying $40 for one month. I haven’t found a nanny yet either after 2 weeks - it doesn’t seem worth it and I will not be renewing.

title: Very unhappy with new updates
body:
I’ve been using care.com for several years and I this time I paid for the subscription there has been significant changes. Now the app it’s very controlling about who you talk to you can’t just message sitters you’re interested in. It automatically messages people you’re not interested in working with and then you have to fend off weirdos who are not a good fit for you at all.. disappointined wish they would change it back to how it was before.

Update: still disappointing and now experiencing glitches. This is absurd considering how expensive this app is!

title: This application has helped me so much
body: I really appreciate this app my daughter recommended it to me because she has used it and was happy with it.

title: Awful search functionality
body: You would think that as a platform where the whole intent is try to help connect people, it would allow you to filter your searches based on AVAILABILITY!  This platform allows you to do no such thing, so you have to open and read through every single profile.  It’s like trying to find a needle in a haystack.

title: Glitchy app web version works fine
body:
The app is frustrating as heck. I like that I get notifications, but I can’t take any action on the messages I get because the app never loads them. 

I’ve tried deleting the app and re-installing, etc. but nothing has worked so far.

title: App timeout
body: The app glitches and gives me the three dots of death. It doesn’t sync well and times out often. Frustrating because the service you provide is great.

title: Freezes a lot
body: The aesthetics are clean and simple. However, the app has a tendency to freeze up often. Due to this I will probably stick to the desktop version.

title: SO Glitchy
body: This app freezes more than it works

title: App does not work
body: After creating a job for a specific day and confirming with the care giver and them accepting the job. The date changed on me 2 different times then charged me for “canceling” before the 24hour time period. Worst app and company ever.

title: Successful, but app is frustrating
body: We have used this app multiple times to find caregivers with success. However the app itself is incredibly frustrating. It has so many issues loading messages and refreshing the inbox. I am often force-quitting the app just to get new messages to load. This has been an ongoing problem. It is frustrating to pay so much for a single month to find a caregiver and struggle through what should be basic features.

title: No Way to Find Anyone without Subscription
body:
I can’t seem to find a way to message people without paying the app. There is no way to “X-out” of this pop-up when I am trying to message somebody. 

Better to use something like Facebook to find people in your area. This app is basically useless/scammy.

title: Loading times
body: The loading times on this app are excessive. I am trying to message people about a position and every time I do it takes a very long time to get to the chat. Sometimes it just stops loading all together and I have to restart. It’s a great service which is why I’m still giving 3 stars, but I’ve just been using the website which actually loads things without freezing.

title: Slowest buggiest app in the App Store
body: This app should win an award for the absolute worst garbage app of all time. It’s so painfully slow. Crashes all the time. It’s the worst app ever created.  Everyone that has anything even remotely close to working on this app should be fired immediately.

title: Waste
body: As soon as I paid for my subscription for an immediate sitter need now all of a sudden my “account can’t be verified”. I’ve had this account for two years with no issues. I want a refund

title: Cool but way too glitchy
body: Freezes constantly

title: Slow & won’t update with new messages
body: This version of the app needs to be worked on. To slow to download messages… when they even load.

title: Waste of time
body: You fill out everything that takes several minutes and you ultimately have to pay a large fee at the end to use the services.

title: FRAUD!!!
body: I checked out the site and with a  doctorate and 4 degree and very astute and technologically savvy was duped into a simple one payment for a simple one time trial I got zero and they kept charging me!! CRAZY IM GOING TO  APPEAL THE CHARGES AS FRAUDULENT

title: Very buggy
body: Crashes often. Inbox nearly unusable.

title: Slow
body: This app is so slow. Just use the website.

title: Fraudulent report
body:
The app provides misleading information about its paid membership benefits, such as access to nannies and criminal background checks. It hides additional fees—requiring an extra 30% charge per purchase for nanny access and another charge for background checks.

The app also suffers from functionality issues, including frequent crashes and user-unfriendly features. It misrepresents its services and quality to consumers while tying users to a “no refund policy,” avoiding accountability and refusing to address quality concerns.

This is outright fraudulent behavior. Furthermore, the Apple App Store appears to prioritize displaying favorable reviews, which inadvertently supports this app’s deceptive practices.

title: Beware
body:
Recently renewed subscription via Apple.  Successful.  
Care got the money, but won’t recognize me anymore.  Wants to be paid again.
Frequent connectivity problems.
10 years ago, it was worth the time and money.

Can no longer recommend.

title: Slow
body: The app messaging system is very slow and glitchy. The app won’t let you exchange phone numbers for a smoother experience. You need to give your address to meet if you want to talk to the person, which I think is weird if you’re trying to screen people. The hiring system is also strange. Cleaners set an hourly rate, but then charge a per cleaning fee rather than an hourly rate. The way cleaners charge is not very direct and they come look at your house before giving a price. All of this just extends the time it takes to get care that suits your needs. Lots of time wasters.

title: Horrifically slow
body: This app works so slow that it's almost impossible to use.

title: Latency issues
body: Care.com is a great service however about 50% of my time on the website and app is spent waiting for things to load. For an app that’s just loading pages and messages, this should be much much faster.

title: Serves its purpose but does not work well
body:
I have never written a review but this app is so janky that I had to say something. This is purely on how the app works not on the service it provides. From a service perspective, it allows you to connect with potential caregivers and provides in app messaging.

Load time is awful. It seems like it has gotten slightly better with the recent update. But my lord, nearly every time I want to review a profile or read a message I have to wait 5-10 seconds for it to load and half the time it fails. You will get frustrated. I find it easier to open a browser and navigate through messages and respond there

title: App is full of bugs
body: App is full of bugs and constantly crashes. Half the time I can’t open the messages from sitters, the app just gets stuck in a processing state. In general the pages take a really long time to load. You need to invest some time in debugging and QA.

title: Terrible app from technical side
body: It is loading and crushing constantly. I can’t delete 20 conversations in my inbox from babysitters who are not available, it is so user unfriendly. It is very frustrating

title: Service is great, app is terrible
body: I have had this app for less than 24 hours and it’s already crashed about the same amount of times. Impossible to do more than one thing at a time — anytime I try to open a chat after I’ve read another one, immediate crash. For the amount of users and the importance of this service, you would think they’d have this thing working well. Very annoying and disappointing.

title: Freezing up
body: This app is frustrating; it takes a long time to load and more often than not, the app freezes up, and at times, just closes.  I am constantly having to get out of it and go back in.

title: Buyer beware
body: Requires a subscription to message caregivers. I spent hours reading profiles and messaged four different caregivers and only one got back to me and indicated that their schedule is full. Care.com has a no refund policy. It was a complete waste of time and money.

title: Super buggy and slow
body: This app needs a lot of work. It’s really slow and super buggy. Many times there won’t be any message notifications but then I’ll open the app up and, voila!, I’ve got 10+ messages. For the cost of this app service I would expect more. Do better!

title: Useful service, terrible app
body: The marketplace of caregivers is truly useful. The quality of the app is dreadful. Especially the chat function. So slow, so glitchy

title: Slow slow slow
body: Great app but it is extremely slow to load messages and profiles

title: Great idea, slow app
body: I posted a job and had 11 applicants within 24 hours!  Great except that I am constantly closing the app and re-opening for the chat function to work.   Half of the time, it still doesn’t work. Any improvement in this function would be amazing.

title: Slowest App Ever
body: This is the worst app I've ever used. Every page takes forever to load. It takes way to long to assess caregivers on the app, forcing me to go to the web version or to use my laptop. It's not like the website is any better. Would love to provide some real time feedback on how people use the app, how to navigate between jobs, how messages can be better managed, archived , deleted.

title: Super laggy app. Website is wonderful
body: Just wish the app was as smooth  as the website. The app just freezes nonstop. Even when I’m on WiFi. Do not have this issue when I use the website. website is super smooth.

title: Use the website instead
body:
The app consistently (literally every time) freezes, then crashes after about 6 or 7 clicks. Even when the app is “functioning” it is still extremely slow.

They obviously have placed their focus on the website and not the mobile app. Disappointing

title: Serves its purpose
body: The goal of care.com serves its purpose but the app is not the best. It is very slow in loading from page to page, most of the time I have to open and close the app multiple times just to read messages from potential care takes. Sometimes messages are even missed.

title: Service is ok, the app is terrible
body: This app is fine for the service it provided. But the app itself is slow, glitchy, and freezes way too much. It's borderline unusable.

title: Great in theory, weak app
body: Very glitchy, half the time I can’t access the responses by potential sitters.

title: Deceptive add-ons and Bots?
body:
After subscribing they immediately try to upsell their additional background checks and make it look like your total subscription will be the cost of  the upsell. It’s on top of it and very expensive. 

All the responses we get are the similar templates “I would describe my personality as likable…”, “One thing you should know about me is I am an honest person.”) and no matter how we respond no one responds back. This seems like poorly programmed bots designed to trick users into thinking there are services in their area.

title: Useless
body: I didn’t receive a single reply from any potential dog walkers I contacted. this was a total waste of money.

title: Great service; terrible app
body:
Care.com has been very helpful for me to find local babysitters. I have no complaints about the service, but the app almost makes me not want to use the service. It’s always glitching and buffering. ALWAYS. After every update, I’m hopeful things will be better but they never are. 

Posting a job and finding sitters has been pretty easy for me but actually hiring them is way more difficult than it should be and not at all user friendly. 

Seems like people have complaining about the software design of this app for years and yet the same problems still exist.

title: Increases price and no support
body: @developer subscribers can’t update their profile information and it’s impossible to know what information is current and accurate. Sometimes the app freezes my phone and I have to exit. I can’t read messages in my inbox because all I see are 3 dancing dots. The messages don’t load and I have to log onto my laptop to see them. Fix the app, especially for the price subscribers have to pay.

title: Terrible
body: Tried finding a tutor, spent 40$ just to see the options. More than half didn’t respond and the others tried pushing me to spend more than the range was set to. Very unprofessional.

title: Huge charge.
body: Don’t pay for this. They charge the full amount immediately, and it’s very expensive. I’m disputing the charge either my credit card company. I think this app takes advantage of people needing help

title: Super squirrely
body: Takes for ever for messages to load up. The app lags and works very slow. Deleted and reinstalled and it still lagged. The interfacing and ability to organize prospects is not good. You cant delete applicants unless you found an applicant. This app needs work.

title: Worst experience
body: I don’t know how they have a 4.3 rating - I’m sure it’s paid for. They have the worst customer service - stay away!!!

title: Helpful
body: I found this app, very helpful and connecting with sitters, especially when out of town

title: Can never log in
body: This is one buggy app. It used to work and then about six months ago is impossible to login. It is still impossible. Makes no sense. The website is fine. I don’t understand why it won’t work. But it’s been a constant problem, so no stars on the usability of the app.

title: App not stable
body: The messaged page has serious performance issues. Half the time I try to open a message thread, it takes longer than 20 seconds to load and I end up closing the app and re-opening it to try again.

title: Long load times, glitches and freezes
body: For a large corporation whose core product is the marketplace, the app to view the marketplace is trash. Long/indefinite load times, constant glitches requiring killing the app. Horrific performance.

title: Good service, terrible app
body: I’ve successfully used care.com to find my last Nannie’s, but the latency in this app makes me feel like I’m living in the year 2000. Every time I click on anything it takes literally minutes to load. This can’t be a connection issue because I experience the same thing on high speed WiFi and high speed data. It’s so bad, the app is nearly unusable.

title: Bugs
body: While creating my profile I can’t get past the section to share schedule and care information. It says “something went wrong”.

title: Unusable
body: The service is ok… I wouldn’t say it’s great, but maybe 3 stars for what it offers (caregiver match-making via the website). The app is a total hot mess. Pages don’t load, notification badge won’t clear, design not at all like a native app. Clearly built on a junky cross-platform framework, rather than native Apple technology. Save yourself much time and frustration by not installing this app, and using the website instead. The engineering team needs better leadership and a new direction… or to remove the app from the App Store and stick with web only.

title: Keeps crashing and losing messages
body: I pay for a service I can’t use. Useless app

title: Easy to use but glitchy
body: The messages keep taking forever to load. I’m a busy mom and don’t have time for each message to take several minutes to load to be able to read and then another several minutes to load to reply.

title: Overpriced for the value and quality
body: Not worth the high price tag. The app is constantly glitching, which makes it frustrating to use. Trying to open up the inbox to read messages should not talk as long as it does every time. Babysitters on the platform often ask for exorbitant hourly rates that don’t match their qualifications or experience. Overall, the service feels overpriced and unreliable.

title: NEVER WORKS
body: The app is a mess- I have to log in from a browser in order to see my jobs and messages and actually use the app. It also seems it sends messages to people you didn’t select. Very annoying to use

title: useless app unless paid
body: nothing works in the app till you pay minimum 40$ monthly. app should tell to sign up as paid users first instead of asking in last step of registration. waste of time

title: This app crashes
body: You have to restart the app every few minutes. Really bad app.

title: Awful App
body: For such an expensive service, I can’t believe how much I need to wait for the app to open. I look at the care logo for about 2 minutes sometimes before the app shows up. Messages never refresh. I also lost a potential nanny because she was automatically marked as not interested. As good as the idea of this service is, I’m disappointed by the quality of the app and further surprised by the ridiculous 4.3 stars - which makes me think it may not be all humans.

title: Way too slow loading times
body:
Long time customer. New app updates are terrible and now is very slow. Please fix this . 

Signed,
Test engineer

title: New customer
body: I have paid for the yearly subscription and I am cancelling it. You don’t get live support to chat to someone I paid $99+ with the intent to get the best service yet there are many caregivers who caught my attention I messaged and they are no longer in my city or are working full time and no longer sitting. There are various people who applied to my job although the rate is way more than I agreed to pay because I am not in desperate need of sitting since I’ll be home helping. In addition when u book someone u pay additional fees so the subscription is only so u use the app. Me and a caregiver had agreed on her being hired and she has no longer responded to me so I have to message or reopen my application overall unhappy with this app it’s a waste of money go to your local establishments like church, mosque, and ask for caregivers if u go to the courthouse u can ask for background checks it will be less than all these hidden fees

title: Furious with their service
body: They charge me 11-20dollar service fee for a 4h nanny job on top of the annual membership. But when i told support that im not comfortable with the way nanny told me she only has 10 dollar on her and needs me to call her a uber right before job starts. They told me they are a self service which means they wont do anything. I wanted them to cancel the job for me so im not charged with cancellation fee because i wasnt sure how i can trust that nanny. In the end i just canceled it myself. If you dont do anything when suspicious activity is going on while charge a hefty fee?

title: Buggy/Auth issues
body: For $30 something a month, the app is constantly giving me issues. I constantly get logged out, my chats won’t load, the time stamps are completely messed up. I have to close the app frequently just to review new messages. Very frustrating.

title: Unbelievably slow
body: I’ve been using this app for one day and I’m already super frustrated by the slowness of it. Messages and profiles take forever to load, and it is not my WiFi connection. I may stop using it just because it wastes so much of my time buffering, which is unfortunate because I know there is a good network of caregivers here.

title: App is broken
body: Messages always delayed. Slow to open up. Such a pain

title: Great for finding help!!
body: Fantastic site for finding the caregiver you need for your special situation

title: Terrible Messaging Platform
body: Considering you have to pay to use this app, the bugs with messaging applicants is atrocious. Although you reply to applicants, when you check your messages the following day, 50% of your replies have disappeared in spite of previously confirming they were sent. This is a huge flaw in the app and I can only imagine even more frustrating for job seekers. The initial phase of finding someone is based off messaging via the app then you can switch to other forms of communication (phone, email). But if that initial contact can't be made because messages are disappearing, the app is essentially useless. Waste of time for applicants  and waste of money for people seeking help.

title: Wrongful Charge!
body: Absolutely terrible company with terrible customer service! Incompetent customer service rep that do not understand or articulate in English. They Do Not Respect their own terms and cancellation policy! We were forced to pay a cancellation fee even we canceled a month before the scheduled backup care. The terms clearly specify CANCELLATION FEE ONLY APPLIES TO CANCEL WITHIN 48 HOURS! HRs, please do your due diligence for the company benefits to your employees. I cannot believe this company was selected!

title: Messaging feature freezing
body: The message feature continues to freeze. Unable to speak with potential care givers. Kinda pointless app at this point.

title: Slow service
body: I can’t get anyone to respond.

title: App needs work
body: For what it costs to be a member and find care, which is great service, the app is awful. The messaging feature doesn’t load more than once. It takes far too long to toggle from one tab to another. And I always have to go completely out and go back in. I don’t experience these glitches with any other app and I’ve used this one two years ago too and this issue was the same. Fix it!

title: Horrible UI
body: Always need to start from the beginning each time. Poor performance and clunky usability.

title: Why did I pay money for such a crappy app
body: I signed up for premium but app is so buggy it makes me want to ask for a refund! There are glitches everywhere, messages don’t send and theres even a messaging limit. I do not recommend this app at all. Devs this is an embarrassment, fix your app.

title: Unfriendly user
body: The app lacks lot of user friendly features. For example, every time I look a profile, it shuts down automatically. It freezes a lot. .

title: Deceptive
body: They claim you can use the basic “Guest” account for free for occasional use; however, there is literally no way to use the app for less than $109 (60 day access). Any attempt to do anything without paying throws you violently into the paywall.

title: Filtering needs work
body: Please add “availability” as a filter option. It has become increasingly frustrating to find a match just to see they are not available during the times we need.

title: App is horrible!
body: The app is super glitchy and constantly crashing and getting “error” messages. Horrible especially when you PAY for a subscription

title: Good service, terrible app
body: No complaints about the actual service the website provides, but the app is terrible. Constantly crashing (and yes, I’ve tried un-installing and reinstalling), being required to log back in multiple times per day, badge notifications don’t match up with the number of actual notifications in the app, and constant errors when switching between the “chat” and “profile” tabs. Save yourself the trouble and just use their website

title: Outstanding Job!!
body: I loved having Jimena clean my house. Not only is she prompt, pleasant, and kind but she does an excellent job. She’s extremely detail oriented, meticulous and thorough. She’s very well organized too.  She showed up and went right to work! She provided order and clarity to my subtle mess. I loved having her to help me. I am delighted that she will be returning.

title: Message & Push Messages work horribly
body: I can’t believe it’s 2024 and such an expensive app doesn’t have more quality development assistance. The amount of times I have to force close the app just to be able to respond to the messages inside, is so unbelievably frustrating. It’s not a network issue, either. The push notifications don’t open the app, it stays stagnant on the orange care screen. Y’all need a new CTO and a new head developer. Based on the reviews this should have been addressed years ago by the Care company.

title: Helpful app, pathetic app
body: Helpful app, pathetic app

title: App Needs Work
body: App freezes and keeps reverting to old information. Redo criterion several times. Finally gave up with a frozen screen.

title: App doesnt work
body: This app needs a lot of work - i paid the membership but it is not worth it i have to open it multiple times to be able to see one message

title: Care.com is a handy site
body: But the app itself is slow. Crashes all the time.

title: Great app
body: I’ve had an incredible experience with care.com and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for reliable caregiving services. The platform is user friendly making it easy to connect with trustworthy qualified caregivers. I would absolutely recommend it to anyone in need of support at home!

title: Not worth it
body: It’s not worth it. They charge additional fees to view background checks and it’s the slowest app on earth! Takes forever to load and force quits a lot! 🙄

title: Unauthorized charges impossible to cancel
body: Imposible to cancel

title: Great idea, horrible app
body: One of the most buggy, glitchy, and poorly built apps I’ve seen. Also, the “add ons” and “special offers” make it feel more like a Walmart than something for serious caretaking

title: Great Resource, Slow App
body: I love using care.com to post jobs and find sitters, and there are always plenty of sitters available, but the app is very slow and clunky for me. Loading messages takes a long time.

title: Hired nanny & baby sitter
body:
Nanny was great! We loved her. The process was helpful .. we are using app a third time for a replacement I even helped others by telling them about the app and found them helpers as well 

Wish there was a way to archive or delete conversations cuz after a while it gets a bit cluttered and hard to keep up w the old messages in the way

title: Payment method
body: Before I subscribe it showed me the price for housekeeper per hour so I got yearly membership but after I started looking for someone to clean my house all housekeeper charge per house not hourly it really disappointed

title: Don’t upgrade through app
body: I upgraded my membership through the app via Apple Billing.  It popped up with an error but the charge still went through.  My account however was not upgraded and after 45 minute chat with customer service they couldn’t help at all.  Had to call Apple for refund, not a fun experience.

title: You are paying for background check  to be done on yourself
body: And the subscription is non refundable

title: NOT CONVENIENT
body:
Aside from paying for the subscription- there will be an additional hold to actually contact someone for information on whether they can babysit or not. 

If they agree during the period of the hold- great! BUT if they cancel day OF , they still get paid. 

I seen a review on a babysitter who canceled on someone day OF. That’s very unfortunate and not convenient for us struggling parents. 

Please remove that hold for us parents looking for a stress free app to find someone to care for out little one!

title: Do not use !!!!!
body: I went on here to look for a one time cleaning but it was an absolute nightmare. I got over 50+ messages for the job i had posted but it was very overwhelming. People were sending messages about their personalities, hobbies, and a “they would be happy to assist your needs” but i don’t care about any of that stuff. Some people don’t respond back which makes no sense why bother texting me your interested in the job,  in the first place ??? Some of them would go ghost and not show up for an estimate. It took me a month to find a person and then just for her to now show up the day of cleaning 😐. Also, paying for a $40 membership just for people to not respond, no shows, or charge so much is outrageous!!!!

title: Frustrating app
body:
The service is fine but using the app is an exercise in frustration:

- Opening a notification often results in a splash screen that doesn’t load
- Messages view has an animation bug that interrupts the ability to actually navigate messages
- Loading time for various pages feels like the app is hanging

title: The UI of this app makes me want to cry
body: The UI of this app masks me want to throw my phone across the room. It is so laggy and infuriating to use.

title: Opportunity for improvement
body:
There’s weird little quirks with the app

I put in a start/stop time that were 10 days apart and got the error message “must be at least 2 hours” and changed the time on the end date.   Uh, 10 days is already greater than 2 hours. 

You cannot change your password unless you’re on a computer even after tapping on request desktop site.  The app or mobile browser cannot do it. 

When you forget your password you can’t simply reset your password the best you can do is get a link to login without password. 

I also got some API errors but that’s probably not common maybe a server was down 

Business practice. 
They wanted to charge my card about 900 bucks before I even talked to a caregiver.  Based on the highest possible daily rate across the 10 days plus their booking fee. 

Well it was 10 calendar days but it was planning to negotiate an every other day drop in because that’s sufficient for cats. And if the person wanted more than 5 days * their daily rate because of the long duration (but hopefully less than a full 10 days) that would be a reasonable conversation. But Care just wanted all the $ up front.

title: Great app
body: Great app! Easy to use and navigate.

title: Easy to use, and I have had luck using them
body: This is a great app, and it has been a great resource for me to find senior care

title: Slow to Load
body:
This app has a weird issue when the inbox loads that it constantly is refreshing and it slow to load on all the other pages of the application. Also delay in getting messages. I checked last night at 10 pm and no messages were received but then this morning I had 3 messages sent at 7-8 pm.
