These are basic things that should’ve been handled much earlier than the version they’re on

title: Needs improvement
body: This app is not user friendly. You can’t sort your results so you’re not scrolling past the same providers over and over. You can’t search a name. And providers who are not available still show up in a search. And the price is absurd.

title: Disappointing
body: For the cost, expected the app to be better.  For starters the sitters should have a requirement to reply to messaging or have rating decrease.  Before I paid for premium I had someone “apply” for my job.  Only thing is I can’t communicate with them unless I upgrade to a premium account.  I pay and then guess what?  This person who was very interested suddenly can’t reply to my message.  Also, I keep having sitters and nanny’s who show they are available for the times I am looking say they are not available.  The app needs a way for them to be clear on what they are looking for.  Also, I will have someone apply for my job, then tell me no and we haven’t even discussed rates yet.  My advice is to reach out to a local university and advertise on a job board-it’s free with better results.

title: Account creation issues
body:
Care has 2 apps - one for caregiving, and one for receiving services. Why they need two apps - not sure. 

Accidentally downloaded and created an account on the app for caregiving. Realized my mistake and downloaded the second app. 

Cant use the same email for both apps. I’m not using the platform now. Their loss.

title: Paying doesn’t ensure you will find someone
body: We paid 90$ and posted an regular baby sitting job, and did not find.

title: Multiple No-Shows - Horrible Communication and Experience
body: My wife and I both need to work. We use backup care as it’s a benefit provided by one of our employers. The problem is Care.com keeps scheduling people who just don’t show up and we have no way of contacting this sitter. Only care.com can contact this person. She hasn’t showed up multiple times and I’m now missing days of work. Incredibly frustrating

title: The worst costumer service
body: They charged me after I had already cancel my subscription then I spent an hour  talking with costumer service. They were slow and not very responsive. This is so disappointing and frustrating. I’m never using this app again.

title: Terrible customer service, Blocked for negative reviews, Bad UI
body:
Was a paying user of Care for ~6 months seeking elderly care help for my mother. One day after giving two separate one star, professionally worded reviews to aides we had booked through the platform who failed to show up for appointments, my account was closed and I was banned from using the app. After attempting to contact the company, was given no information on why my account was blocked nor any ways to resolve it. Was also told they can ban you for whatever reason without disclosing why. This is the equivalent of being told I’m never allowed in any Walmart store ever again “just because they felt like it”. What’s the point of having a review-based platform when providing fair, but negative reviews on poor quality caregivers gets someone kicked off the app. 

Separately, the app is awful, buggy, glitchy. Very disappointing, especially for a paid service.

title: BAD APP
body:
TOO MUCH WRONG WITH THIS APP !!! 
First of all the poor quality of people and their Lack of respect in replying or many cases even showing up! 
In addition the app has problems all the time ! 
One I don’t get notifications and secondly it just has soooooo many glitches ! 
It seems like all this App is concerned about is getting paid. 
NOT on improving the overall app and the quality of people.

title: Clunky
body:
This app addresses an important need, but it’s clunky and slow to load.  

Hey dev team, why can’t you delete messages from an active job?

title: Beyond glitchy
body:
This app makes it virtually impossible to communicate with people. 

It is slow, the chat doesn’t load properly…

I dread having to use it

title: DO NOT TRUST THIS SITE
body: They allowed a family that I have NEVER WORKED for and have no idea who they are to leave a review on my profile. I tried to ask for help and for them to prove that I have worked for that family and I never got any help. I was slandered by a family that I have never worked for and the website did not help at all, this is CRIMINAL. Do not check on this app. On top of that, the platform is terrible and poorly developed and continues to only offer families about 20 miles away. HORRIBLE, DISORGANIZED AND DANGEROUS! I am considering suing them for slander.

title: App functionality
body: Checking one’s inbox via the app is terrible.  Everyone agrees.

title: App crashes often, poor support
body: The app crashes often. Customer support requires a long wait. Accounts are costly.

title: New user experience
body: I recently began to seek childcare for my toddler & infant and I started with care.com online & created my job posting on my laptop , today I check my email to see I have 4 applicants , so I downloaded the app to check out my applicants & literally I cannot even see their messages or profiles anymore unless I “subscribe” to the premium version ??? Not cool at all . How am I supposed to connect with anyone or consider anyone for my children with out being able to see anything ??? Why do yall make us pay for that ?? Why do I need a monthly subscription just to find a babysitter ? Makes no sense not to mention the app just glitches and force closes everytime I click on anything , horrible app , horrible company, horrible everything .

title: Issues with posting job on app and poor customer service
body: Issues with posting job on app and poor customer service

title: Almost impossible to login
body: The login loop is so annoying. Sign in with Google leads to sign in with email leads to “link your accounts!” Leads back to sign in with Google. So. So. Annoying.

title: Improve messaging
body:
App is slow and laggy. Which is an annoyance to live with for the platform. However, the worst part is the slowness with the messaging...please make that more fluid. It literally reduces the efficiency of communicating with prospective care providers. 

After you get the basics in, add video conferencing. From there Care should think about helping people manage the hiring process through multiple steps, contracts etc. There's more to do here and I'm sure people would be willing to pay a small fee.

title: Zero support for account holders
body:
There is no path to help customers -no 800 #, no live person, no working chat BOT it disconnects and , no email options . You can be sure they process auto payments tho. 

All the employees have the label of “Chief” this or that and no workers. As a primary member I am supposed to have acess to background checks . l I use them prior to having a candidate come into our home for phase 2 ,”face to face meeting”. But, it has NOT worked in weeks, and there is NO one to help. They want you to read their “ articles “ to self help thru thier technology deficiencies. 

I have a great candidate pool, and I cannot access my prepaid benefits. Spending top dollar to have when needed to proceed thru the employment process, but now I have to create another path to do something I already pay for . Ridiculous!

title: Disgusting Paywall, Terrible performance
body: Paying 40 monthly to be able to message people on the app is heinous, especially for an app that is slow and crashes often.

title: Overpriced and underwhelming
body: To do anything is at least $40, then you get nickled and dimed after. They need to clean out their profiles too. 90% of caregivers on here are not active and won’t be writing you back. Of course, that’s how they get you to spend $40… by seeing so many potential caregivers! The website has better search features, the app is useless

title: Worst app!
body: I can’t speak for the service, but I installed the care giver app instead of the care seeking one. I can’t cancel my account and can’t use the same email for the other account. Terrible design and very frustrating

title: Pay To Play
body: I downloaded this app to find someone to watch my cat while I’m out of country and came across this app. I submitted the job I needed done and received a lot of applications but I cannot view any of them or reply to the messages without paying a hefty $30/month subscription. This is ridiculous for a freelancing app and shame on the developers for this. I would rather they add a tax to every job rather than charge the user that is supplying work for their clients. I can guarantee that this lowers the amount of users that are willing to use their app due to the subscription of using the app. I suggest you change this feature, I will not be using this app. Uninstalled

title: Great help
body: This is a great tool for even the more remote areas in the nation. I was pleased to even get one hit for our under resourced community.

title: Service totally terrible
body: I had a technical issue I was hung up on twice. And everybody else said they would get back to me. I never heard from anyone but yet they took my $160 without any problem and told me it is nonrefundable.

title: App design
body:
Much liked with the previous design green and blue colors.
Please bring that icon colors back.
Thank you.

title: Good enough
body: Wish the app was faster especially in the chat, but have found great people on here.

title: Works, but could be much better.
body: I always find help with Care.com, but their messaging does not include notifications to their app, so communication with prospective workers is clunky and we miss out on opportunities to meet people that might have been a good fit.

title: This app is awful.
body: You are required to pay for a membership, there is no free options. It’s one of the least user-friendly platforms I’ve ever seen. I cannot login through the app, I can only access my account through the website on a laptop. I had a minor issue to edit something I posted and it took over 72 hours to resolve. Then I got a notification that my membership was being shut down. No explanation. No refund. It’s an absolute joke. No parent should ever use this exploiting, dishonest, awful company.

title: Please use caution!
body: This “free” app allows you to post a job and book someone for a service but you have to upgrade to a premium subscription to be able to send messages, which is kind of a necessity when booking someone. I hired someone to dog-sit over a holiday weekend because no one was available on Rover and this seemed like a comparable service. Well, how do we proceed if we can’t communicate? So I bit the bullet. They did not respond to my communication over a 3 week period leading up to the date, presumably because they did not pay for the ability to message. It was getting to the cutoff period for me to pay a cancellation fee so I had no choice but to cancel days ahead of the scheduled service. Because of the timing, I didn’t have the ability to try to find anyone else and was forced to stay home with my dog while my wife and children traveled to visit family out of state without me. This is a horrible service and app, very misleading, and a complete waste of time and money. You fooled me once, and I will not be taking any more chances. If you use this app, please beware and know that you are taking a big risk!

title: Very Helpful Service. App Regularly Breaks
body: Care.com is a great platform but I routinely have issues with the app. I will have messages not show up, the app just won’t open, basic functions result in an error popup. Really frustrating to use.

title: Unreliable
body: We hired April full time and were completely honest about her hours, pay and responsibilities (nothing outside of keeping an infant content while I have remote meetings from home). April showed up for her first day on a Thursday. She said she had prior commitments on Friday, which we understood. The following week she messaged us 8pm the night before her next shift that she’s decided to stay at current employer. We were left scrambling to find care for the next few days and had to miss important meetings.

title: Very useful and all in one place!
body: I get tons of applications!

title: Service/concept is good. App needs tons of work.
body: It’s surprising that so many people have reviewed and commented on the poor user experience of the app and nothing has been done to remedy it. The app crashes consistently. The chat is really difficult to use. Add to that it’s really slow to load and sometimes doesn’t load at all. If you could just resolve your app bugs, this could be an amazing service offering.

title: App has some problems. Decent network of caregivers
body:
We have found several excellent caregivers through Care.com; the app, however, has some problems, mostly in the arena of chats/communications between caregivers and parents.

It seems like many caregivers never see chat messages; this is completely op as to why; are they not checking their app? The notifications not work? Is there a delay? The answer is probably all the above. Anytime we have been able to chat with the caregiver about why they haven't seen our messages or why we haven't seen their messages it's always been either the app did not send notification, or simply did not display the chat messages for many hours or even a day after message was sent. After over a year of on and off experiences using the app to find caregivers for different positions and seasons, I do think it's a pretty consistent repeated error where a caregiver can send a message or parent can send a message through the chat/messaging feature, and often it can take the better part of an entire day to appear for the person that was sent to. This is obviously unacceptable when trying to communicate in real time and get something scheduled. There also needs to be some kind of transparency feature where parent can tell if they have sent a message and it is being ignored and they need to move on, as in: "the caregiver has seen the message and is choosing to ignore it" vs. "the caregiver has not yet seen the message".  

There's also a huge number of caregivers who seem to be absolute throwaway profiles on here, I don't doubt that they are real people; however, probably 70% of caregivers just don't even respond after they apply for a job and you the parent get back to them to say "yes, we would like to interview you!"... maybe this is not a reflection of the app, but rather a reflection of the throwaway/ghosting society that we live in today.

title: Not a fan
body: I wasted my money for months, paying for a premium subscription and got nothing in return. Canceling the membership is also annoying. And they offer no refunds.

title: Login malfunction
body: The app will not accept my password.  I created the account initially online. When attempting to login on the app, it asks for the password to connect accounts, the password is not recognized.  Would love to use the app.  Please help.  This is my first time using Care.  In my position, convince is key. So please help. Thank you.

title: People don’t respond
body: It is hard to tell who are still actively looking for a job. I get no response to most of the messages I send out. Some of the descriptions I read from profiles sounds old.

title: Consistently awful software experience
body:
If you wanted to know what it’s like to use software made by an engineering team that hates you, use ANY of the Care.com products (web, iOS or Android)

Every action requires a brand new page load of 4 seconds.

Clicking Back in any capacity resets the view (and another 4 second page load)

Actions you can take on one platform don’t exist on the other.

“Decline” candidates just sends them broken empty texts so they ALL respond to you in your messages (which all take 4 seconds to load, 1 by 1)

All 3 platforms are so consistently unusable I have to imagine Care.com is just giving the product team guidance like: “whatever the UX design for hate is, go with that.”

title: Candidates
body: I have had excellent success in getting caregivers for my husband with Parkinsons and a tutor and life skills person for my autistic daughter. This has been an excellent site for me. Lisa Schreiner

title: Care
body:
It’s a good service I really had to search for a while!
But have so far hired to really good people from here in 5 years who work with my Disabilities and age! They also allowed me to work with them! When I could and smart enough to know when
I needed to stop! So thanks for your service!

title: No refund after 2 days
body: Not great having no calling costumer service. Not great costumer relations. I asked for refund after 2 days, no refund!

title: App doesn’t work consistently
body: When paying for a service I expect it to work more than half the time

title: Today, current issue
body: It looks like the app crashed and I am not able to access the app or any messages

title: One time babysitter
body: Needed a one time sitter. Got the run around from help/support. The bottom line is you have to pay a monthly fee to access any information or even try to hire one.

title: Needs updates. Many issues.
body: Big delays in opening messages. Complete time outs. Can’t attach a current job, can’t edit jobs, can’t delete jobs, there is no system for checking off people that you have already sent messages to. The search feature issketchy. It is just a really poor app.

title: Account access problems
body: For $160 a year I should be able to use my account at all times. I constantly have trouble accessing by my account. Furthermore, like 10 years ago I tried to be a caregiver and it caused so many issues with my account when I signed back up to try and find babysitters. I ended up having to use a different email but somehow it still causes issues. Will not renew, waste of money.

title: Fast responses
body: Super fast responses in the timeframe I would need.

title: This App is Awful- what a scam!
body: This app is awful- takes forever to load, obstructs communication between families and care givers, and will mysteriously malfunction if you try to edit your jobs or contact Nannies. If you post a job, it is very difficult to communicate to the care givers-between the bugs and the terrible ux, the latency between messages, not to mention the abysmal help bot- do yourself a favor and spend the money and your time somewhere else.

title: Care.com for your Caregiving needs
body:
Excellent site. Love how as a paying member, you can correspond with those seriously interested in caregiving. 

So far, all interactions have been positive. I recommend.

title: Inconsistent information
body: I found it quite confusing that the rate and description of caregiver changed. There’s one description and price while I’m browsing for help and another one when I started messaging them. Don’t understand why.

title: Very Poor Customer Service
body: I made a mistake when setting up my account causing it to close less than a week after I paid for it. Customer service told me that I would have to open a new account and pay again. Care.com would not provide a refund to be used to open a new account. As if I need more stress when seeking palliative care for my mother I also list over $300 in the process: the core fee of $265.44, but also the additional fee of $53.09 required to ensure the caregivers are vetted and safe.

title: App takes forever to load
body: I wish I could love this all because the basic functionality is great. But this app takes minutes to load, sometimes doesn’t at all. It’s terrible.

title: Never received any response or care
body: I ve had this app for two years and have yet to find help or care. It’s like a ghost app.

title: Buggy, clunky, Unusable
body:
Unable to submit a request for a caregiver. “Something went wrong” window pops up everything time with no links to help or assistance. Just says “Try again”. Zero faith in this app…also why have two separate apps for a caregiver and someone looking to receive care. You should have one profile and be able to toggle between the two. 
Poorly developed, and lacking in customer care.

title: Rating is for the app not the service
body: This app is glitchy at best. For charging member so many fees, you would expect top quality IT and software design. It is not it is one of the slowest messaging platforms. I have on my phone, sometimes it doesn’t open at all. I get stuck buffering and loading in all different aspects of the app when I’m opening messages or looking at profiles iPhone 14 pro user who uses plenty of regular apps like Facebook messenger Snapchat Rover an email with absolutely no issue. It’s so frustrating that I have all these issues when I just want to contact a different sitters and actually try to use this service for the service itself, you should be able to delete a message out of the inbox once you have eliminated a prospect candidate that you’re not interested in using after you posted a babysitting job but you can’t makes it more difficult to sift through and eliminate potential candidates and find the right care for your child I had like 40 applicants to the last job I posted was trying to figure out which ones I had new responses to. Theres no Arsenal of caregivers you have Fed or clicked or connected with that easily accessible because everything is project based. at this point, I don’t feel like the app needs updates. I think it needs a complete overhaul and new design.

title: Tech issues make the mobile app unusable
body:
The app is sending messages to people on my behalf, incorrectly reporting statuses of my bookings, and showing messages out of order. They want $40 a month for this janky system?

Edit after developer response: I wrote my initial review before I discovered that care.com tries to charge a service fee for each booking ON TOP of the subscription price! Absurd! I’ve followed their instructions and deleted the app but I won’t be reinstalling.

title: All about the money
body: Have to pay to view someone’s reply… thought it was a free app but all apps have a catch… wish I knew before wasting my time posting a job listing…

title: App won’t open/load
body:
The app hasn’t been opening/loading for a few days now. I tried uninstalling/re-installing and clearing my cache. 

It’s a mediocre app to begin with. Everything is cumbersome, including deleting applicants to a job in the app. There’s also no ability to take notes on applicants.

title: Crapp
body:
If you want a caregiver and a house cleaner, when you go to post the job for the house cleaner it still posts as a baby sitter, or vice versa. 

This is not a well thought out app.  The idea is great, poor execution.

title: Challenging to use the app
body: This app makes it difficult to sort and evaluate applicants - it’s clear that the developer has not actually used this app. I have over 72 applicants to my job, and many are simply not a good fit. There is no way to sort or filter inquiries, and there is no way to efficiently remove inquiries from my inbox.

title: Illegal and immoral
body: The fact that Care.com allows parents to post jobs that pay below minimum wage is disgusting. No one should be allowed to create a job posting below minimum wage. Care.com doesn’t care about nannies/sitters, they only care about getting membership fees.

title: Taking advantage of families
body: It is hard enough as it is to find affordable child care. In order to respond to anyone’s messages, you have to upgrade to the paid version of the app. Talk about not caring about families. Just a money grab.

title: Total ripoff. No refunds, no support, horrible app
body: I got this app, and I set up a job offer looking for somebody to help me with my mom. Then it made it look like I had to sign up for a subscription so that people could apply. I’m not sure if this is correct or not, but that’s what it looked like. $160 later, I get a few applicants, most that don’t seem appropriate, With a button that says marked hired rather than hire this professional or something like that. I accidentally hit this button and there’s no going back. It’s not clear that I’ve hired a person as other applicants are still applying. So I’m not sure if I have hired somebody it looks like I can hit the same button for other applicants so it looks like there’s a potential to hire multiple people for one position, and I have been trying to reach out to Help all day with no luck. There’s lots of articles about random things but nothing about how this app works or how to get in touch with somebody there’s no refunds so I’ve just spent 160 bucks for a very frustrating afternoon, I want my money back and I want help on using this app.

title: Really difficult website to use with lots of glitches
body: They need to make a serious investment in UI. The site and app are really buggy and not intuitive/too many steps to accomplish what you need to, information is not displayed in an intuitive way so you need to look around a lot. Would like to be able to block people I’ve already interacted with and would not want to hire. It would save me a lot of time.

title: Price gouge much?
body: The amount you’re charging for x,y and z is asinine. It’s 40 for the app itself, 25 for a background check then THREE HUNDRED MORE DOLLARS…. For extended background check (or whatever the F it is). Honestly I get your hustle “oH dOnT u WaNt YoUr ChiLD iN gOoD hAnDs??is tHAT nOt wOrTh iT??” But for how few options there are in my small town this is complete rip off. If I lived in a Bigger area and there were 50 applicants - totally worth it I’d say. Otherwise you can F right the F off.

title: Logging in
body: I don’t care for the app because I constantly have to keep logging in. It’s the only app on my iPhone that keeps logging me out. Also, I tried to delete my account from my phone and it ended up charging me $112.39. I was on my phone and they bait click. I have sent an email and called for a refund and have not received any help. I have to dispute with my credit card company.

title: Inconsistent issues
body: App often takes some time to open. Also phone numbers display as “x’s” often.  Not being able to search by someone’s availability and distinguishing between nannies versus babysitters is frustrating.

title: Crashes and dumb fees
body: App will constantly crash. Also you cannot message or open messages on the basic plan. All it really allows you to do is list a job but you can’t get contact info or reach out to the people who reply. You basically have to pay care.com to reach out to those interested and then pay the people you hire.. seems a little whack

title: Scammy
body: This app will charge you from 3 separate payment platforms. Good luck ever canceling your subscription.

title: Can’t cancel subscription
body:
Got the app for a one time event when I needed care in another state. 
After the event I cancelled my subscription and deleted the app. Now, a year later, after almost a year of not using the app and the subscription being canceled, I’ve been charged again for the subscription. Apparently there is no way to cancel.

title: Not User Friendly!
body: As a Nanny, I’ve used this site for over 10 years. It’s nothing but a headache and sometimes just doesn’t work. Very poorly designed and frustrating.

title: Do not buy. STAY AWAY
body: Like most new parents we bought this app in hopes to find a great sitter. We paid for the upgraded version and had no luck finding anyone reliable. Paid more for background checks that were not complete, dealt with people who said they would work for X amount and asked for 40% higher after talking to them. So we stopped using the app, and went word of mouth. Fast forward many months and they auto renewed the subscription at nearly $400.00. Then when you try and cancel a week later they say there is nothing they can do, and that they can help cancel for the following year.  STAY AWAY PEOPLE, there are way better options out there.

title: Customer Service is Trash
body: I have been a user for 3 years and when I went to renew my subscription through ICloud on my iPhone the app did not show the reflected renewal.  The charge was taken from my bank, Apple noted that this was an App development problem and when Care.com was called they said it wasn’t there fault and essentially “tough break”.  They refused on multiple occasions to honor the payment and renewal and continually insisted I call Apple again or insinuated I make another payment.  The app is glitchy, and the customer service is trash.

title: Make the app work properly
body: This app should be easy and helpful at almost $50 a month for premium, which you need in order to talk with potential candidates. It’s so slow, it says error on me at least twice a day, and sometimes it gives me the option to say “Not Interested” in someone that’s applied for a job and sometimes it doesn’t. Not worth the money for a broken app.

title: Terrible app
body: Badly coded, buggy, slow, crashes all the time… I end up just using the website mostly

title: SLOW and missing integral functions, categorizations
body:
Overall, the app is usable. The app is SO SLOW, I have 1 TB internet speed, so this issue isn’t on my end. When I switch between screens I see the dancing 3 dots, which seems the case for every screen transition. It lags terribly. 

There are integral functions missing. I can’t delete messages/candidates, or favorite others. There’s no category for a house helper/house manager or a hybridized position that doesn’t perfectly fit into one category. With pet care there’s not always an option to communicate with the prospective carer, I suppose Care.com expects them to be hired with no communication first. I’m sure this is largely done so the business isn’t taken off the platform due to Care’s business model for that particular category, but I don’t entrust my pets to anyone I haven’t communicated with and met with first. 

In other categories there’s not an option to exchange phone numbers, which is ridiculous. Communication is necessary but it seems Care.com is trying to double dip (maybe triple +) to increase revenue well beyond subscription fees. 

In my household we own a company that designs websites/apps for large companies. As much as Care.com charges and as much revenue as they bring in, this app is mediocre at best. C’mon Care.com, do better, please. This app is what I’d consider a strong starting point, but nothing more.

title: I can count on Care.com
body: Whenever I needed an extra hand, I have been able to find someone all of the time. This is a great resource to have.

title: Expensive but amazing for finding sitters
body: It’s amazing to find awesome sitters, it’s just super expensive, $40 a month? Plus the sitters have to pay a premium as well, pay for background checks etc.. which I think is awesome but it can be a lot of money especially when your trying to pay someone to watch your kid while you work for example. Your paying away your paycheck for someone to watch your kid while your working.

title: Liars and horrible caregivers found here
body: They are very sneaker about their fees. Do not sign up. They will trick you into thinking they have quality caregivers, but they don’t. Before you know it you have unknowingly signed up for a month/year and they refuse to give you any money back ever. I wouldn’t be writing this is the babysitting was of high quality, but it isn’t.

title: Bad UI and Crashes Often
body: The UI is really poor and I thought I was posting an up to amount for the job not a fixed rate. There is no tutorial walkthrough. The app constantly crashes.

title: Terrible usability
body: The scrolling function for selecting hours is completely broken. There's so many usability issues with the app it renders it useless.

title: It’s great
body: I just think it’s expensive. Also I keep getting applications form people way out of our posted price range who have not read the job description.

title: App keeps crashing
body: New to care.com, as a mobile engineer myself, I’m not impressed with this app. Keeps crashing, reloading the same page after single clicking to nav to another, and overall just bad UX.

title: Too many bots
body: I signed up for 3 months hoping to find a babysitter for my two kids. Only got replies from bots. When I asked for my money back for my subscription after two days of useless searches, they said NO REFUNDS

title: App Lacks any form of Change Control
body:
This company clearly needs to implement a change control policy for any/all changes to the application. 
I have a job listed, but I am in NO WAY confident anyone can respond or see messages that I send.  
For some applicants I have the ability to decline the person and I have a “declined applicants” tab, while there is no option to decline for other applicants (all within the same posting). 
The care.com idea is great, however the app is woefully buggy. 
Change management is lacking and desperately needed. 
Instead of spinning your wheels:
1. Test, test, test
2. Implement a single fix package
3. ONLY fix the new bugs (if any) you just created with your fix package
4. Then and only then, moving on to the cutesy new things someone wants to add

title: Don’t use
body: The worst app. $39.99 for a month to find a sitter? That’s crazy.

title: Issues with app
body:
The app is very slow, messaging in particular is very frustrating, it reloads the entire list of conversations very slowly every time you go back and forth - parents looking for child care don’t have time for this.

Background checking is also not smooth.

I also think the app could do more to discourage unqualified applicants from applying to jobs. Only about 4/30 applicants for my posted job actually met the criteria. Felt like a waste of time.

Had I not used the app successfully in the past, I would have rated this experience lower.

title: Inbox and messaging is so slow and laggy
body: This app is helpful to find caregivers in your area. I submitted a job posting and I got a lot of applications, which was helpful. However, trying to message your potential Caregivers is very slow and clunky. Every time you try to open a new message it loads for like 10 seconds. there’s also important features such as the age of the caregivers that are only available on desktop and not mobile. This is not very helpful for new busy parents who would prefer to use the at mostly on mobile while they’re busy caring for their kids.

title: Waste of $40
body: I’ve wasted over $40 for a service that does not do what it tells you it will. I have had no help from care.com in finding anyone to help petsit

title: Unreasonably high prices
body: Enough said

title: They will trap u in recurrent payment scam like others have noticed
body:
Did subscribe the service in July of last year, but was not able to find any good help. Was only going to pay for three months subscription, but to my surprise, they have it on the recurrent payment every three months. Most of the time you won’t be able to find anyone anyhow, but they keep charging you every three months. There is no option to take your credit card information out so that you can stop this recurrent payment. Even after spending a lot of time with customer care via chat, they cannot figure out how to take your credit card information. Very careful and do not put your credit card on file because they will keep charging you . I hope there’s a way to hold companies like this accountable. THERE IS NO WAY TO TAKE UR CREDIT CARD INFORMATION OUT FROM THEIR SYSTEM

Update 
- even after spending an hour with the customer care, they cannot figure out how to take off
. They keep sending generic messag that go to the desktop app but again there is no way to delete. Only you can update the information.

Again, do not put your credit card otherwise they’ll keep charging you.

title: Many bugs
body: App could be great but there are many bugs. Messages in chat say delivered and not read when they’ve been read, the time stamps are 10+ hours off, notifications aren’t working anymore so I’m not alerted when I have a new applicant or message.

title: Not user friendly
body: I found it hard to navigate around this app. I couldn’t send messages to potential Nannie’s unless I created a job. It’s hard to share information (which I get) but how can you move forward with hiring if you don’t talk to them? What’s up with all of the upselling of background checks?

title: Non-Refundable
body: “Subscriptions are non refundable” meanwhile I can’t even get the app/webpage to function properly. What a complete ripoff.

title: Intuitive but could use some updates
body:
The app is very intuitive to use, although there are a few things that would be very helpful as someone looking for a caregiver.
1- currently you can’t remove/delete/archive a chat if you are not interested in a candidate until the job is closed.  This creates an enormous  amount of chats clogging the inbox.  Suggestion to archive chats even if job is still open.
2- some candidates I cannot select as “not interested”, and they remain in my active applicant pool. Suggestion to be able to remove candidate from applicant pool, or remove them if the candidates selects they are no longer interested. 
3- the ability to star/heart a candidate is only visible in the job applicants section.  Suggestion to add or carry over star/heart to chat section for better visibility.

title: Great place to find help!
body: I have utilized Care.com for senior care and the caregivers have been great!!

title: Good idea but horrible execution
body: My biggest issue with this app - when you post a sitting job, applicants can take the job, even if they don’t intend to carry through with the job and even if you didn’t book them for the job just so they can send you a message. We should be able to message each other in advance to see if it’s a good fit. Once they take the job, they block all other applicants from it. And if they can’t or won’t do it, then you have to cancel the job you never booked them for in the first place. This cancels the entire job and you have to post it all over again and re-send all the messages you previously sent to other applicants.

title: Expensive, slow and glitchy
body: This app is ridiculously expensive for what they offer. I have found the app to be very slow and glitchy as well which again for the amount you pay it should work much more smoothly.

title: Interface needs major improvements
body: This site was launched before Urbansitter yet Urbansitter as a more user friendly app. Only thing deterring me from using Care.com. UX Designers need to do better

title: CHARGED FOR DELETED ACCOUNT
body: I have had a nanny account looking for a job for years. I made a family account (following care.com directions) to find a nanny for my preemie baby. They charged me the $65 then promptly deleted my family account. Then they deleted my nanny account. Then they sent an email to everyone I’ve ever messaged, telling them I broke community guidelines and not to talk to me. Customer service is a joke. They only provide email for non premium members and have ignored several emails even though they claim to reply within 48 hours. DO NOT USE!!!

title: Worst experience
body: Don’t waste time and money those housekeepers the read you message and never respond

title: housecleaning
body: was able to find good housekeeping persons.

title: Waste of Time and Money
body: Title says it all, yielded zero results in finding a housekeeper for me.

title: Poor message loading
body: Messages do no load properly or quickly. I usually have to quit the app and open it back up for a new message to show up. Also, when writing a message it is difficult to navigate up and down through the message as it gets stuck and does not move at all.

title: Glitchy and Absolutely Frustrating
body:
The app and website are glitchy. Signing up was an ordeal—Care.com happily took my money (I paid for a monthly subscription and paid for a premium subscription to have piece of mind in terms of background checks for contractors) but after that I had the hardest time setting up my request (signing up for a dog walker to come to my brother’s house twice a day, morning and afternoon). Every time I signed in to Care.com, a message comes up, saying “Someone already has this account associated with this email address”. Yeah. It’s me! It is ME who is trying to sign in after I just created an account. I had to resubmit my family information and a photo over and over and it STILL got rejected, even though I didn’t violate any of their guidelines. Then when I tried to re-book the dog walker that has ALREADY done the task I had to resubmit the information for the task over and over for the search engine to say, “There is no one available”. 

The dog walker herself was awesome. This is no shade on the actual employee/contractor; just the dang website and the app. I bypassed all of the nonsense and had the dog walker call me and we stayed in contact off the app. I know it is not the “correct” thing to do, but I couldn’t figure out how to do the app. I consider myself a smart and patient person but this experience made me feel dumb and impatient. 

Absolutely the worst app experience I have ever had. As soon as the dog walking services were no longer needed (brother returned home) I cancelled my subscription immediately. There was something that said that I cancelled only the Premium subscription. You have to go through additional steps to COMPLETELY cancel the subscription—otherwise you will be charged for the basic subscription at whatever frequency of payment you selected. Read the fine print so you don’t get screwed.

title: Oof!
body:
Wow, I hate to be this woman. However, as a marketing leader in the family space, let me say my disappointment in the app, service model, and, most importantly, customer service didn't meet any need or minimum amount of aid. We paid for the year membership with a significant discount. We got our account running and thought we were good to go. We got to booking our first babysitter, and everything was going great. 

The sitter ended up a no call no show, and then the messaging portion kept giving us an error message to “try again later.”  when we reached support, we were passed around and told we didn't know why we couldn't get or message your sitter and told
To cancel the booking with no other options for advice or help. When we discussed why and how we could fix the issue, I was told I'd already been given an answer and was then disconnected from support. 

We couldn't send messages, but the app could send a message to let the sitter know we were canceling. Also, we are unable to find the sitter profile. Did the app let someone book and confirm a job and then delete her account in less than 24 hours? Without notifying us? We’ve been denied a refund for the app and premium membership. Everything about our experience with care.com has been bad, off, or outright wrong. Working in the pediatric and family space, this was frustrating, worrying, and disappointing. Please consider not using this service.

title: I hate this app
body:
I’m paying for the 3 month subscription. If I dare scroll through some profiles and hit “contact” to chat with candidates I like suddenly I’m getting random “so and so” has accepted your job. I never even saw this person’s profile or reached out to them. So why does this app keep doing that. It’s embarrassing and frustrating. I can only respond that’s it’s a technical error. I hate paying for something that’s barely working properly.

Update from Care customer service 

Thank you for letting us know you are experiencing an issue. Our Technical team is aware of the issue and is working diligently to resolve it. We greatly appreciate your patience and assistance in this matter!
?I would like to tell you that as a tip you can write the numbers with an asterisk, while we find a solution.

Like I said this is a rubbish app. You can’t charge for a product that can only work with “tricks”. There’s no trick in taking $80 from my bank account.

title: App needs work.
body: I have lost sitters and more bc of the app crashing.  No refund or help.

title: Crashes over and over
body: I'm using this app as a caregiver. I have yet to get my profile completed as the app repeatedly crashes with each step I the set up process. This is extra frustrating as I've already purchased a subscription.

title: Very slow, keeps crashing
body: Takes forever to load. Keeps crashing. Terrible app.

title: Scam Company
body:
This company will close you account and not refund you - and even worse won’t even tell you why but will tell you that they refuse to pay you your money back.

A nanny I hired was crazy, crystals, moon current, Jupiter ascending theory type of crazy. I was super nice to her but it didn’t work out when she had to keep the kids directed to their tasks which she thought kids should lead themselves. 

Anyways this girl reports me and care.com closes your account without any notice. They didn’t even tell me, they just kept the app saying it wasn’t working. 

I had to waste an hour waiting for customer service (also shows how bad this company is). Just for them to refuse to tell me why the account was closed, then finally telling me. It’s crazy to think someone can report you and they close your account without any form of defending yourself off the word of someone random online.

They refused to refund me saying that I never had a paid account nor did I ever pay. I said it was ridiculous to say this when they are looking at my account details. I finally take the time to show them the charge and then they say take it up with the credit card company to get a refund… WHAT!!!

So they scam me, steal money, will close your account based on any ticked off caregiver, will not refund your account and will not even tell you that they closed it but will make it seem like the app is not working.

Besides them being a complete scam and I have also read the internet and heard from people after this incident that they do this all the time. 

But besides that their app is slow, it takes forever to respond and sittercity is way better. 

Go to sitter city, they are a way better app.

title: Optimistic, using this tool for summer plans.
body:
The Care.com app is a simple enough and easy to use.
   I gave it four stars because it is a little bit slow while loading up or searching.
   And something that would make this service better is if we could search keywords, when we’re searching for tutors or sitters, which is not available on the app or the website. and even if this doesn’t change for we, the people looking to hire someone.
I should hope that the people looking for work are able to use keywords at least on their end.
But I do like that there’s less of an AI vibe, care.com doesn’t show me how well and personal they know me like my other apps and browsers do and that’s comforting. 😂

title: Useful!
body: We paid $280 for everything because we wanted the more in depth background check for our nanny. The app is very slow moving which is suprising and expensive but other than that great!

title: Find Caregivers Elsewhere
body: This app was terrible and a horrible experience for finding child care. 0/100 would not recommend. The customer service side when needing help was unhelpful and rude.

title: An excellent resource to find senior caregivers.
body: I have had very good experience on this website in my search for senior caregivers. What I like about it is that if I have to replace one later on, I have names that I can contact.

title: App regularly crashes. Applicants lacked basic qualifications.
body: Very disappointed so far. Both in the quality of the app (it’s poor) and in the service too.

title: Horrible customer service
body: The company I work for gives us a $1000 childcare benefit and this year has contracted Care to administer it. They make it extremely difficult to use our benefit and look for any reason to deny the claims First of all they can never find childcare providers to meet the need and they don’t tell you until the night before you need coverage for work. So you scramble at the last minute to find someone yourself last minute. If for whatever reason you receipt doesn’t match the times you requested (like say your sitter that u had to find last minute was available until 7and you normally start at 6 but because you had no one to watch your kids because Care didn’t let you know until the night before so you had to go in late) they will deny your claim for reimbursement. Not only do they look for any reason to deny your claim it also takes them a whole month to tell you they reviewed and denied your claim and that you need to resubmit because you’re is about to expire. And it will take another month for them to review the claim again. When you call, everyone has such a thick accent you can’t always tell what they are say and it’s hard to tell if they even understand you because their responses are often strange. The company we used before understood how to be more flexible and as long as the childcare hours lined up with our work schedule for that day it wasn’t a big deal and they were much faster. I’d give negative stars if I could. Hope we can rid of these guys next year

title: Fix

title: No refund after auto renew.

title: Great product, slow app

title: Seems fraudulent, and refused to issue a refund
body: From “caregivers” responding to my ad to them say their not available. To “caregivers” ask for more than what they have advertised. Unprofessional headshots, spelling errors, and canceling once I mentioned meeting in person. This app is a fraud BEWARE!!!

title: Unreliable babysitters/last minute cancellations
body: I used care.com to find a babysitter when I was going back to work after having a baby. Through the app, I was able to find one great babysitter, but I had multiple babysitters accept jobs and then cancel at the last minute. Having to let your young baby scream in it’s crib while you take a meeting because your babysitter didn’t show is wildly stressful, particularly for a new mother. There’s no penalty for the caregiver to cancel last minute, but I would have had to pay a penalty fee if I would have cancelled last minute. Also, I’m not willing to leave a negative review for someone that has my home address. If possible, I’d suggest finding reliable childcare outside of care.com.

title: No availability in my area
body:
Tricky way of presenting pricing to make you think it’s $25 but they really charge you $78 for all 3 months at once with no refunds. 

No availability in my area. I wrote to every single option in my area and no one was interested (some didn’t even reapond) in an ongoing gig for my infant. I live in a touristy area so the caregivers are interested in 1-2 weeks gigs at a resort. Not a reoccurring gig at my house with my infant. 

I wrote them to tell them of my issues and they offered to close my account and not give me a refund. Super annoyed I wasted money on this.

Firstly, features appear and disappear. 

Secondly, for any given feature, one day it works directly, another day it takes you out of the app and sends you to the website.

Can it just work the same for six months? 

On the plus side, adding appointments to your phone calendar via the app works more often and is more flexible than adding them via the website. Adding appointments via the website often fails and when it does work, they cant be edited.

body: The places where this app works are, indeed, useful, but there are too many hiccups. My biggest frustration is menu options drop on and off. Some days I can click in to see my lab results. Other days I can’t. There are also too many core functions that push you out to a browser, where you have to log in again. Why have an app when so many of the key tasks you want to do have to be done on the website.

title: Not Available In The U.S.
body: I can’t even download it, but I need to so I can upload my insurance card

title: E-visit does not work
body: Just cycles thru and won’t let you proceed.

title: Going down hill
body: This app used to be great, but it has really gone down hill in the last year. Nothing loads, and there is no way to see test results anymore. I expect way more from such a big company that profits billions of dollars a year.

title: Why was test results removed?
body: So upset that the test result feature was removed, please add it back so we can have visibility to our reports. Not sure the point of this app without that.

title: Terrible Implementation
body: This is a highly stripped down proprietary version of MyChart with very limited functionality. The Evisits don’t work, the test results icon was removed.

title: Medication refills
body: There needs to be a way to request medication refills. Pharmacies cant submit refill requests for controlled medication, so the only option a patient has is to call the office and wait on hold to have the receptionist send a message to the doctor.

title: Where are alerts?
body: I get that HP wants to brand the MyChart experience but I am not getting alerts for preventative care like I do for my daughter in her MHealth MyChart. Sure the colors and splash screens are great in the HP “app”, but they need to make sure basic health care functionality is still part of the experience.

title: HP app creator hates older patients
body: Whomever got the bright idea to “make the new app EASIER to navigate” needs to be fired! I can no longer find my way around this platform! I blame upper management for this. Someone has to approve this new format. These CLOWNS need to find employment elsewhere!

title: Decent app
body: App was easy to use when submitting a refill request but the new update has changed it and made trying to send a message or refill request difficult and frustrating..

title: Crash
body: App crashes constantly.

title: App not opening
body: Uninstall and reinstall 5 times, the app just won’t open

title: Crashes when opening
body: I installed the latest version (4.1.20) and now when I try to open the app, it crashes and does not open.

title: App won’t open
body: After bux fix 13 hr ago, app won’t open

title: Can’t see anything
body: Since yesterday I can’t see any of my information or appointments or test results.

title: Doesn’t work since last update
body: I cannot access any of my care information. Just shows my plan info. So frustrating! I sent a message asking for help and no response yet. Can’t make appointments. What is going on?!

title: App is useless
body: I loaded the app in a vain attempt to message my care team.  Yesterday I discovered there is no link in the website to actually create a message.  In using the app, if I want to send a message, the only choice provided is for scheduling an appointment.  When I clicked on that, the resulting pop up said I’m locked out of doing that.  Considering I waited on hold for an hour and 15 minutes yesterday to schedule an appointment, and can’t message anyone, I’m stuck.  Do I make the one-hour drive to the clinic just for communication?

title: Really like it, but I have some suggestions
body:
Suggestion 1: incorporate messaging directly into the app instead of needing to open the website to manage & send messages - confusing experience. Maybe there are special privacy concerns or integration issues, not sure but it is confusing.

Suggestion 2: for appointment scheduling, have a “first available” search function within the specialty area, then refine by location. It is very time consuming to pull up schedules of individual providers only to find out each one is booked out a month or more. The option to search by clinic is the same, just narrows down which providers’ schedules you can view. This always necessitates a call.

It does seem that the video visit scheduling has improved in the past few months - you can select same day times and see the provider you’re selecting - excellent!

title: Can’t upload from Files
body: Debit card documentation does not allow you to upload from Files, only Photos or take a picture. It’s also absurd that EOBs aren’t automatically attached in the first place.

title: Locations
body: Tried to schedule an appointment and it claims the closest hospital is 55 miles away. I can walk to two different hospitals in less than 5 minutes.

title: ID CARDS
body: Ever since the last update I cannot view my ID cards within the app. That was one of the main reasons I loved this app. That is gone now. Please put them back!

title: App keeps closing on its own
body: Trying to submit a claim but the app keeps closing and restarting on its own

title: Terrible
body: I have had to submit the same claim documentation several times and it is not being applied and I continue to get messages stating I still need to submit documentation. Customer service is a joke.

title: Can’t even sign in
body: Something is going on with this app. I can’t even sign in. This started a week or two ago. Uninstalled and reinstalled multiple times and still can’t sign in on my iPhone. My husband has an android and I can sign in just fine on his phone, be neither he nor I can sign in on my iPhone. Please fix this!

title: Can’t see all the information I need
body:
My doctor communicates important medical info through letters. This app does not allow me to view that whatsoever. I have to use the web version. 

It’s much better off just rendering the web UI at this point. I don’t understand why there’s this separate effort that abstracts so much away. 

It’s also confusing because my doctors will say it’s on MyChart for my viewing when it’s not that easily accessible. Why build an interface on top of MyChart? It seems like extra work that’s just frustrating for users.

title: Not friendly to use, broken functions
body:
This has been better in the past. Been with HP on and off for years.

Currently can access historical information which is convenient.

Unable to message care team, unable to schedule. 

Used to be able to message and schedule. Would have given a 3 or 4 in the past as it’s not easy to navigate. It’s written without patient vernacular in mind like most health care apps.

title: Welldyne link doesn’t work
body: I am unable to access my pharmacy information from the mobile application.

title: Scheduling appointments
body: Unable to schedule appointments with this app so incredibly frustrating! I used to be able to I’m not sure what happen I can’t even see any of my information anymore! Horrible please fix this.

title: Horrible to navigate
body: I came from using Mychart to MyHP and struggle constantly with this app.  Doesn’t matter if I’m using on my mobile or on my computer.  If you try to respond to your doctors comments regarding your test results just forget it and pick up the phone.  In the old system it was possible but this.  No - I don’t want an e-visit - it was within the last couple of days and for the life of me I can’t figure out a way to respond.  It makes me want to find somewhere that doesn’t use this horrid app!  It’s only a one way street for the provider and NOT patient friendly.

title: Clunky
body: The app has some good uses. Now here’s the annoying issues. when you want to submit a claim, the app crashes more than 50 percent of the time after selecting that option. There is not an option for Face ID with iPhone so you have to manually enter your password each time. And when you want to submit an eob to the hra account you have to take a photo of it instead of the app allowing you to just transfer the file within the app itself.

title: Garbage app
body: This thing is a mess!  Every time you check a test result for the first time, it gets hung up for a few seconds and then takes you back to the Home Screen. You have to go to test results again and select it again. Then it works. There is no indication of whether a test level is within normal range either. The list of appointments is incomplete. Get it together HealthPartners!

title: Poor app
body: The app closes out randomly nonstop.

title: Bad app
body: Pretty bad. A lot of things I still need to go to the website to use, why? What’s the purpose of this app? Nothing is centralized, can’t check in on the app, etc.

title: Selecting Test Results Bounce you back to home - please fix
body: See above

title: Submitting claims not working
body: For over the past week I’ve not been able to upload claims. Once I try, it shuts down the app. Very frustrating as I put money away every check to get reimbursed from my FSA!! Please fix Fast!

title: Can’t contact doctor
body: The app is great to see your test results but it lacks in the communication aspect and can’t contact your doctor or staff directly like MyChart can.

title: Doesn’t work often times
body: Most of the time I am unable to see my appointments. It does not allow me to open my care messages. I just received an e-mail notice that my test results were in but when I go to view the most recent results are form 2 months ago.

title: Horrible app!!
body: Very difficult to use , when you tap on send message to my care team you get a big long message about ways to make appointments , no link to message your doctor. Cannot find notes from last visit with my doctor. Very frustrating to use when you’re not feeling well and need to contact your care team. Also, the app randomly decided to invalidate my password and made me change it 3 times in a row. Will be switching to a better health care system.

title: Page Bounce
body: Every time when I go and check labs the first time I sign in, I get bounced back to the Home Screen.

title: Getting booted out of test results all the time.
body: I love the app but every time I click a test result it boots me back to the very first main menu and I have to click back into test results and click on the test result to view it. Please fix!

title: A lot left to desire
body: The app is ok-looking, however, it could be so much more. An example: for a couple of years now, app offers viewing your card(s). Although on iOS, it doesn't support rotating the cards automatically, as you rotate your iPhone. Whatever Healthpartners, did you get your UI designer from a checkout lane at Target?

title: Overall, a very solid app
body: Clean and intuitive design. Pages load quickly even without a strong signal. I think it could be easier to contact a provider(‘s nurse) for questions, to request Rx refill or order regular tests.

title: Bill pay
body: I should not have to re-enter account info, guarantor, credit card etc. after the prompt of “Make Another Payment”!!! The first time yes, but not after that

title: How to pay a bill?
body: Incredibly confusing about how to pay a bill- both on the app and online- it shows the claim but there exists no link from there to pay… wildly unintuitive.

title: Making or changing

title: Not as good as MyChart was
body: This app has all the bare bones info, but MyChart had your prescriptions and allergies and your care team you created. Why the need for the switch to this app, which lacks what made me start using the app in the first place.

title: Constantly asking for location
body: I declined the app using my location yet it just keeps popping up and forcing me to settings and then I come back to the app. I don’t need to share my location with this app to use it. Design flawed. Creates crap user journey.

I think the problem is the health partners app is not connected to epic ?! 
Can’t respond or write to my specialists or my doctors.  Difficult to connect with them. 
Email then directly or their care team through the site without trouble. Also in the app need to get out of site to web browser to complete some tasks.  I’m sure your aware of that too.

title: Terrible App
body: App is completely worthless. App crashes does not allow you to make appointments, pay bills, or really any functionality. This app is good for you to waste your time attempting to schedule an appointment before you give up on frustration and sit on hold to call and make an appointment.

title: Not enough SETTINGS in the App to correct or update
body:
Part of my last name is now showing as my first name and no settings to correct this! I went to the website and corrected there but App is still wrong! 
My name was correct in the system for years, why is this happening now?!

title: Med refill request
body: Really would be nice to not have to call in for med refill requests, much more efficient if this was something you could do.

title: Erratic, Mostly Non-Functioning Lately

title: Good, But Needs Work
body: Easy enough to navigate. Unable to get access to non-pharmaceutical prescriptions such as for glasses. It’s a little tedious to contact a healthcare provider. Also, my second COVID dose is not showing up on my vaccination card.

title: Messaging

title: So many problems
body:
Can someone from Health Partners PLEASE contact me regarding the horrible functionality of this app?

If not - please alphabetize health concerns wherever they listed. This is kindergarten logic.

title: NO ability to contact a physician?

I had high hopes that this would be like MyChart. 

Through MyChart, I can send a message or a question to any of my providers. 
I can also send photos, which would be a BIG help with something like Dermatology! 

You’re way behind Fairview in providing healthcare that is more readily accessible to patients. 

I truly hope Park Nicollet changes. Otherwise you’re going to lose a lot of patients.

title: Scheduling
body: It did not give me other options for appointments. It only offered one option with provider of my choice and confirm. If that date and time did not work for me, there was no other option to choose from.

title: Need more links
body: I would like links to the “call one near you” comment when an online appointment cannot be scheduled. For example, I want to speak to, possibly set a schedule with, a therapist/ psychologist. Can’t do online. It said to call but no indication where or #

title: Underwhelming

title: Screen doesn’t load

title: Learn from others

Coming from Allina the my chart experience at Health Partners has been disappointing. I’m unable to graph my lab results from previous. Billing does not automatically link to my login, causing want to look for account numbers which is confusing.

Looking for something as simple as billing information and a phone number is not available. Likewise try to understand what I’ve been charged, is also unavailable. Such a large company I hope this can be utilized to improve.

title: Works well most of the time. Buggy at times

While scheduling an appointment, I chose a Doctor from a list of Doctors displayed. Since the available time for the chosen Doctor did not work for me, I pushed the back arrow to try a different Doctor. It was frustrating to see the app take me to the start of the appointment scheduler, thus forcing me to restart the entire scheduling process again. 
Minor bug, but very handy and works well most of the time.

title: Needs improvement
body: It needs a quicker way to be able to email my doctor, instead of jumping through so many unnecessary hoops.

title: Slow website
body: This website is so slow and inconvenient. It’s not just my phone beciase it’s the same in my computer

title: Looks nice. Not User Friendly
body:
Many functions are not intuitive to find. Have to go through multiple menu screens and make sure to select the exact right steps. 

Must not want patients communicating with their providers. There are far too many steps to go through to actually send the message. I also use Fairveiw MyChart App and you can reply to messages right from the same screen you get a message from your provider and writing a new message is right on the main Home Screen.

Finding where to submit supporting documents for existing claims is also way too many steps. 

The design is very stylish and pleasing to the eye just wish common functions were more user friendly.

title: Earliest available option

title: The app is great to have

title: Messaging could be clearer
body: Coming from Centracare epic the MyChart messaging is much less patient friendly. It looks like you’ll have to pay for it no matter what much of the time. Although there is the possibility of a free message it’s clearly not intuitive. Would suggest the payment screen appearis after Nurse has triaged message and said this won’t be free but requires an E visit or in person visit. Currently, as you start to write a message the only screen you see is the payment screen and it’s not clear you can click past it and get a free message if appropriate. I think that will discourage or confuse many patients who just have a quick appropriate message for the nurse/MD. And that’s what happened with our patients coming from Centracare my chart. (They have no E message option)

title: Appointment Scheduling

Great app for making my appointments with 
my favorite physician at Park Nicollet Tria

title: HP
body: Somewhat hard to find what you are looking for. Have trouble finding the app as it doesn’t have health in it have to remember to search for “HP”. On my Apple device anyway.

title: Review

title: Very user friendly.

title: Health app

body: I was able to schedule my appointment using this great application. Super simple.

title: HP app
body: The app is more difficult to use than the last app.

title: Incredibly poor developed app with no regard for user experience.
body:
Nothing works as it should.  It’s clunky.  I’m starting to book my health services through other providers that use MyChart out of sheer frustration with the amount of time wasted trying to get anything booked.  The booking system is the worst I’ve ever seen in an app.  You can’t add vaccine records (received by outside providers), you can’t easily view test results, can’t really accomplish anything on this app.  
It’s sad, Park Nicollet used to be my go to for all healthcare related needs, but now the whole system is so inefficient.  Making an appointment shouldn’t be such difficult task.  Don’t get me started about the billing system - I’ve had to call insurance on every single claim sent by HP/Park Nicolett this year due to incorrect insurance coding resulting in incorrect coverage and higher medical bills.

title: Easy to navigate
body: I love having my records available quickly and the ability to make appointments without being on hold forever!

title: customer service Not knowledgeable
body: App doesn’t show latest claims.  Called customer service and they claimed there’s something wrong with my cell phone not their app!   Very disappointing and dissatisfied with the rude customer service representative.

title: Whoever wrote this app clearly doesn’t use it!
body: Scheduling an appointment is an incredibly difficult and non-intuitive experience! Why do I have to choose a doctor if I just want the next available appointment!? Or choose a location for that matter? And don’t dare tough the <Back button at the top if you want to back out of a selection - THAT STARTS YOU ALL OVER! You have to non-intuitively tap the doctor or location you just selected to select another one! Then it e-mails you frigging PDFs of notices that are too small to see in portrait and it won’t re-orient them to view in landscape when you re-orient the phone! Half the things you can do on the app you can’t ACTUALLY do in the app - you get redirected to sign in through your mobile browser! Bruh…. Do you even AppKit?

title: Where are my results????
body: My app does not look like the version shown on the App Store. There is NO WAY to view my results or to make an appointment which in effect makes this app useless.

title: App Rendered Pointless After New Update
body: When I first got the app a couple months ago I was able to access my test results, insurance information and see and schedule my upcoming appointments. Then the app updated and I only get insurance information and appointment scheduling. I get an error message any time I try to use the living well section, I cannot access upcoming appointments and the most frustrating thing is that I cannot see any of my test results online or on the app. And it seems I’m not the only one making this complaint. I tried mychart, but it says to use the myHP app unfortunately. I don’t know if it’s a system glitch or what, but I’ve been trying to get answers to a medical problem and I can’t get access my lab results on an app that is supposed to provide easy access to them. I honestly hate using this app because it only makes me frustrated.

title: No Messaging Capability
body: There’s no way to send a quick note or question to Doctors or Nurses. It’s time consuming to call and wait for messages to be returned; it’s not a comfortable experience to tell the front desk staff personal information.

title: Appointment scheduler not intuitive.
body: Had difficulty going back and forth between the options

title: Scheduling a doctors appointment
body:
I planned to schedule an appointment with Dr. Forrester using the appointment line but was told my wait time was 78 minutes so I went to the online appointment process. This method went fast except I have to wait until November 8th for my appointment to occur.

Health Partners needs to do something to speed up its appointment making process and for patents being able to be seen sooner.

title: Contact or lack thereof
body: I don’t like not being able to email my doctor or clinic directly. Spending a looong time on hold is not my idea of a good time when I have a relatively simple question.

title: Nice new website
body: There’s so many nice improvements to  the website I enjoy using it.

title: The best
body: The best

title: No open choices
body: You need a category, other, this was useless.  Don’t know if it went through.

title: Missing pages
body: Most times when I need to get to a page; I get an error message telling me that the page is not available.

title: Disappointed I can’t do check-in
body: I have Blue Cross Blue Shield and when I go to the check-in pages it doesn’t allow me to check in unless I can do HealthPartners insurance ID and I don’t have Healthpartners I have your class. Thanks

title: Always something wrong
body:
There is never a smooth experience with the HP web site. It took eight tries to make an appointment with my foot doctor. Still
Have not been able to send an e-mail to my orthopedic PA. This happens every single time I use this web site. It’s greatly annoying.

title: System doesn’t work so well
body: I tried to send an email to one of my position PAs and all I get our messages saying that the system doesn’t work

title: Frustrated
body:
This was easier to use when the access was actually through the MyChart app specifically. I don’t know why a logo specific front end was created that is clunkier/less user friendly. I’ve been using MyChart for years because we started in Texas with Texas Children’s, so it doesn’t have anything to do with not being used to MyChart. 

Switching between my kids and my profile always takes extra steps somewhere. I’m not able to access all the records and documents from the site or app that I could in MyChart. For instance, I need to provide my child’s asthma actioyplan to the school before they will administer his medication there. I can’t access the document in any section that I can find, whether it’s after visit summaries/notes or health records. Yet if I log into the old Tx Children’s account I can actually get to all of the old asthma action plans in a couple seconds. I find that ridiculous that I cannot get to a current and valid plan on your app or system, but can easily access records just as a history on a different MyChart. 

Growth charts on the other system are more varied and complete. If it follows WHO data, I can find it. 

Messages are easy to send from within the MyChart app. Messages in MyHP are painful to deal with. I’ve given up and use the website because it at least isn’t as clunky. 

If you’re only viewing your own profile on this and only accessing the bare minimum, it’s not the end of the world. But MyChart’s specific app is far superior than the bare minimum that was sunk i to this. If you’re not going to make this at least functional, at the very least you should let us still log in through MyChart itself since everything is still through the MyChart service. This is ridiculous.

title: Covid test
body: I am concerned that I won’t get my results in time before I travel.  I need a negative Covid test not more than 3 days before I travel.  This site says I will get results in  2-3 days.  I am getting a test 56 hours before I travel. How can I be assured I will have my results in time?   Frustrating.

title: Worst web support
body: I have two accounts that HealthPartners are aware of. It has been a year. If I use the same credentials I see some appointments on the web, and different ones on the app. So, my data is in two places. I have asked them to merge my accounts and data for the past year. They are aware of it but are unable to do anything about it because they don’t know how. If you want to speak to anyone via the phone be prepared to wait for over an hour, then have your call dropped.

title: MyChart is 10x better
body:
In my view, a medical app needs to provide a great deal of medical information in an easy-to-access and intuitive way. The MyHP does not do this. HealthPartners used to use MyChart as their app of choice, which is much better than the app they are offering now. 

Want a list of your prescriptions? Not on the MyHP app.

Want to leave a message for your care team? Can’t do it on the app.

Were you previously enrolled with a different health system/insurance company and want to view those records? Can’t do that with this app. You could/can with MyChart.

I recently returned to HealthPartners after living out of state. The replacement app is insufficient and the company that now handles their billing (Homelink) is insufferable. 

Side rant about homelink:
If you have a pretax benefits account with your employer, you cannot use the benefit card to pay homelink by mail or pay bills with it online through their web portal. You can only use your HSA/Pretax account to pay by phone. The problem is that they rarely send you a receipt using this method and wait times are occasionally excessive. The fact that homelink often sends bills for medical equipment that was ordered over a year prior is a separate rant for another time (recurring monthly equipment charge).

HealthPartners used to have the best service with the best tools to manage your health needs. They do not anymore. I can’t recommend this app and I almost can’t recommend HealthPartners.

title: Incredibly useful but 1 request!
body:
I'd love to give HealthPartners ★★★★★ but I can't until the app is renamed to “HealthPartners”. 

I have an onslaught of apps on my phone so I usually use the iPhone’s search feature to pull up apps by name. Every time I go to search for “Health” or “HealthPartners” I have to delete what I wrote and rewrite "MyHP." This is incredibly annoying and on top of it just being an annoyance the branding doesn't even make sense. HP® is the company name for former Hewlett-Packard, a tech company. The MyHP branding doesn’t fit for HealthPartners, and quite frankly it’s borderline copyright abuse of the HP® registered trademark.

Please update the app name and you’ll receive the five stars you deserve.
