title: Unbelievable!
body:
Pharmacy says call provider. Provider says call pharmacy. Web app does not list my clinic. It lists a different clinic. My doctor is not listed at that clinic. Submit email for help, and explain the situation. They respond by telling me to make an appt. using the steps I just said do not work. After more than a week of this nonsense, I call any number I can find and only get automated messages telling me to contact my pharmacy directly. Press any button that sounds like I will get to talk to a human, as that is no longer the last option on the list. They are cordial, but tell me my doctor now works at the location that doesn’t show him. They also say they can no longer leave a message for this department directly. So he transfers me to that clinic. I stupidly try their phone options only to get in the trap and speak to no one. And now I do not know what number to call because I didn’t dial it, I was merely transferred. Go back onto the app, and check this location my doctor is supposed to now be at. The clinic is not listed. Go on the internet and google the clinic. It is now called something else, and show a different city. Go back on the app and select THAT clinic and my doctor comes right up, and I make an appt. in 3.5 seconds. By the way, the clinic that he now shows up at, was the very same clinic that he did not show up at , for the last two weeks! UUUUUUHG!
And one more thing, I STILL HAVE SPOKEN  TO NO ONE ABOUT MY PRESCRIPTION REFILL!!!

I would also love it if they would turn on account linking like almost every other organization has. It’s clear they’re either trying to pigeonhole their users or they aren’t keeping up with Epic’s updates which is problematic. I give this app an outstanding “MEH!”

title: Features removed - scheduling, test results
body:
Looks like in one of the recent updates, they removed the ability to see one’s scheduled future or past appointments, and it seems they removed the ability to even make appointments. This feature existed just a few months ago.

You also cannot view test results anymore. This also existed a few months ago.

So, this app is borderline useless. You can’t make or see appointments, you can’t see tests, you can’t message your doctor or even see most of the messages they send you. Half of the options that do exist just take you to the website, so this begs the question: why does this app exist, if you would always be better served by just logging onto the website from your smartphone?

This app somehow just keeps getting worse and worse. We can’t even use MyChart anymore, HP disabled the ability to access anything using the MyChart App.

title: Terrible, horrible, no-good interface
body: I don’t understand the point of this app. I’m trying to make a virtual appointment with my allergist. The first time he can see me is in the middle of March and I can’t wait that long. So instead of offering suggestions as to who might be able to see me sooner, I have to go back and do the search over again and pick a random dr from his office. Turns out they’re scheduled out, so I have to start over. All I really wanted was the number for their office so that I could call and schedule an appointment. Instead I get an unnecessarily complicated and inefficient waste of my time.

I want advice on whether I can get a COVID test to rule it out but have to wait 5 days to discuss with primary physician. Perhaps the delay is because I didn’t use primary physician at the start, which was on a Sunday.

title: I miss the MyChart App!!!!!
body:
I am unable to access most of the healthcare things I used to in the MyChart for Park Nicollet. The only thing I can do is see upcoming appointments, read messages, and see test results as single results “not together with results from the past to see trends”. You can’t see any information about your last vitals from your last appointment. 

You can’t see your prescriptions. I went to the dentist they asked for an updated list. I usually just brought it up on the app, as it had dosage and the proper name for the drug. Some drugs are so close in name, you could easily make a pronunciation mistake. I just gave up. Trying to log in to the website on your phone in the COVID stress of healthcare wasn’t worth it. 

I thought, “I still had the MyChart app, maybe I can get the functionality back by using that. Nope, they took away access to the MyChart app as well.

 HealthPartners is the worst thing to happen to the Park Nicollet system since COVID.

body: I am blind and use VoiceOver, iOS' built-in screen reader. I am unable to read my explanations of benefits PDFs, as I can only read the template messaging and not the actual amounts/charges. I am also unable to review the claims information other than to see what my provider charged and what I need to pay. In the claims detail screens,￼￼ the text is smashed together at the bottom of the screen (likely because we use larger text, which is also a built-in iOS accessibility setting) and I cannot use VoiceOver to read this information either. This is unacceptable for any app, but especially unacceptable for an app that displays personal health information. ￼

Reply to developer: Nope. There is nothing about claims in the My Plan tab. I could prove it with a screenshot if there were a way to send it to you.

title: HP Pharmacy
body: Sorry that HP is bailing on the pharmacy biz.  Thanks for the years of cranking out the chemicals to keep us all alive.  Best of luck to the fine people who made it a great customer experience!

title: Easy
body: Very user friendly app.

title: HP app
body: Wow, so easy to file my receipts and then sit down and upload photos and information.  Check comes in the mail!!!

title: Ease of use
body: This application is very easy to navigate and use.

title: Searching for next available appointment
body: Nhaving to select a doctor and a location before a date and time took me much longer to find an appt for this morning. Also, the text in this review is not showing up so I can’t see what I’m typing so that’s not cool.

title: Closing ?
body: It is u fortunate that the HP administration could not see past their own pocket books and chose to close the HP pharmacies instead of lowering their own useless salaries .  HP pharmacies were the best and their mail service rocks !

title: Hp app
body:
I filled out an online questionnaire with virtuwell. Spent over a hour.
It should be stated upfront Medicare does not cover online diagnosis.
Making an appointment was easy.

title: Ok but frustrating
body: I adore the camping scene on the splash screen, my favorite part of this app. BUT Can’t view plan documents/benefits from the app. Or access explanations for labs. Other features seem limited. It’s ok for on the go, checking a time, sending appts to calendar, ...but prefer using the website via browser on my laptop for more information. Would be helful if screen rotation was supported also.

title: Thank You!!!
body: Best medical app ever!  Love the ease & convenience of submitting HSA receipts online.  Thank you for spending time making this app so user-friendly.

title: Some improvements could be made
body:
Under the prescription info can you add the date the prescription expires. 

It would be nice to have the optical prescription in the app.

title: Navigation
body: A little difficult to navigate from one family member to the next while on the mobile app.

title: Scheduling!
body: Pretty slick !!!

title: FSA Reimbursement
body: Almost every time that I have submitted a claim and each time it’s a different reason. There is always 4 or 5 reasons why my claim might be denied leaving me trying to guess which one it is. Most times I have to call health partners and wait on hold before I can get the actual reason why my claim was denied.

title: Just Okay
body: I’ve had the app crash on me a few times while searching for a doctorándonos then searching for right day for an appointment.

title: Needs a little more work
body:
I like a lot about this system particularly access to the list of upcoming appointments, ability to cancel an appointment on line, scheduling and test results.
I liked it more when I could easily contact my provider by email if I had a follow up question. Now I have to call. 
The dental services are not well integrated into this system. If you try to make a dental appointment with your preferred times you just get a call and are asked when you want to schedule an appointment. The billing is also not integrated into the regular medical billing.

title: Love HealthPartners
body: I have belonged to HealthPartners as far back as when it was Group Health. I’ve always felt like all HP doctors I’ve seen are excellent. I don’t have to carry records from one provider to another. My medical history is available to all providers scheduled to see me. I use the mail order pharmacy so l receive my refills in my mailbox. All the HP sites I’ve been to are comfortable and attractive; the staff are friendly and professional.

title: Excellent service
body: Heqlthpartners pharmacy, mail order and at the clinic, is great! They're fast and efficient.

title: Easy to use
body: Easy access to lab work so I can share the numbers on the fly to my health provider.

title: Mail order pharmacy
body:
Hello. Just want to let you know that we really like the pharmacy refills that you provide. Best service ever. Thanks. 
Ed Charbonneau

title: Do not go through
body: Every receipt I send in I get 1 or 2 letters saying I need to re-submit a receipt

title: Rx order buggy
body:
Attempted to order rx.  Logged in, saw my name, pressed rx button. 
Your app then wizzed around for a few seconds, then asked me to log in a second time and decided to log me into your mobile app which I didn’t want.  I am using an iPad and the app doesn’t display as a full page.  Why was I forced out of the Internet app into the mobile app?

title: Pharmacy refills via phone app
body: Easiest way to renew prescriptions.

title: Ok
body:
Great for EOB’s; meh, for prescriptions.

It would be great to be able to see how many refills are left, who the prescribing doctor is, and receive some sort of notification the order has been received/shipped.

title: Future labs
body: It would be great to know what lab orders are needed in the future.

title: Mail order pharmavu
body: The service is extremely convenient. I really like this service: it works, its efficient.

title: Best pharmacy!
body: Health Partners’ mail order was pharmacy has done a 100% fabulous job since I discovered it. Excellent service!

title: Extremely Convenient!
body: Love the app. Far more convenient than calling and waiting on hold!

title: Amazing
body: Easy fast thank god for NON-single payer.   I can see who I want when I want.

title: Please offer dates first
body: I really would like to pick a date before picking the doctor. This is a great service. But please let me choose the date first.

title: Phone App!
body: 2 swipes to reorder prescriptions is just amazing!  Thank you!

title: Prescription
body: Had problem getting to my lower prescription..

title: Booooo
body: Never wants to load I can’t even use the app

title: Terrible app
body:
Hard to navigate appointments.   Constant “oops” messages where you have to click the same command 2x or 3x for it to advance.  Recommended scheduling does not take in to account any plan limits, so I would have had to pay for a free wellness visit if I didn’t pay attention.  Can’t advance to next month from recommended date have to click on each day to advance the calendar.

Simply awful.  Glad I didn’t have to navigate while I was ill....

title: Easy!
body: Everything is so easy!  Thank you!

title: Dr. Defor
body: Doctor Defor is a very knowledgeable and he really Cares about his patients. I had a wonderful experience when I saw him. Receptionist and nurse were very friendly and professional. I highly recommend a visit to Inver Grove Heights health partners and doctor Defor.

title: Game Changer
body: Everything in one place. My only suggestion would be to ensure that you can always get to an address and a phone number to the facility or pharmacy from wherever you are in the app.

title: No other choice thru my employer
body: The app is ok the company is not.  Have had many issues with RX since we started with this company.  They have tried but the communication is not very good considering they have an “app”.  They should be able to text you or email you if there are problems with a refill.  I have gone weeks without BP meds at start of our policy because my med was tier 2.  They stated they called but I have zero proof on my phone it from them them that this happened.  2 weeks and then had to switch to new med which is kicking my butt.  I can barely stay awake for a 15 minute drive to work but let the pharma and health insurance companies make a bazillion dollars.  Don’t mind me

title: I don’t seem to be able to login
body: It used to be very simple but now seems impossible

title: Very convenient & fast!
body: saves a lot of time when scheduling appointments, or viewing test results.

title: Great health app
body: Great integration with my health care and insurance. Easy to use and provides relevant information.

title: Very nice but would love Apple Wallet support
body: This app is great.  I downloaded it to have digital access to my ID cards in an effort to reduce the cards I carry around.  I would love to see the ability to add my ID cards to Apple Wallet.  The App Store says it’s compatible with Wallet but I see no way to add the cards.  I can access them quite easily in this app with Touch ID but it would be nicer to have them in Wallet to eliminate the step of finding the app and logging in.

title: Not so useful
body: Why can’t appointments for behavioral health be scheduled on this app?

title: No Test Results
body: I got an email saying my test results were ready to be viewed. I was super excited to use the app! Since I didn’t immediately see a “test results” tab, I spent a lot of time looking through each tab to try to locate my test results. There wasn’t a tab for test results at all- only explanation of benefits. This app is not helpful- it would have been better to have gotten a phone call or voicemail from a nurse. Very annoying!

title: Good but lacking some essentials
body: This app has many great features but lacks some essential features, like email my doctor. That would be infinitely helpful for busy parents who can’t always get on the wed sight.

title: Love this app!
body: Gives me everything I need to manage my plan and care.

title: Overall great app
body: It is a great app. I would have given it 5 stars if it worked better for dependents. For example, I’d like to get messages for my child in the app inbox. I apparently still have to log in online to see them, which takes away a bit of the convenience of the app.

title: Kids accounts
body: It is hard to find the kids’ accounts on the app and then it isn’t possible to refill their prescriptions using the app (or online) which is very frustrating.

title: Health app
body: Easy to use

title: Review
body: I couldn’t add a new credit card it happens all the time

title: My HP
body: I love the app. I’m able to make appts, order proscriptions, keep up with my labs, talk with my favorite doc(Dr Sebas), etc. I don’t like Health Partners very much but I do like the app.

title: New update
body: I have an iPhone 6. I just opened the app to find I don't have my test results anymore. I have relied on this through my cancer treatment this last year. I also see that my regular messages from my doctors are gone.

title: Find care
body: The app won’t let me search for cars by a physician. It only gives me the option of finding a dentist.

title: Excellent App
body: Scheduling appointments is very easy and the ability to see test results right away is great.  This app has helped me take care of my health in a quick and easy way.

title: There’s this one thing missing.
body: The app is generally quite useful, and mostly minimizes any issues to call which is burdensome for me due to the nature of my impaired hearing. But, it is frustrating in paying the monthly plan premium since there’s no way to access that function except on a notebook based browser outside tha app. (which is peculiar since meassages about paying that premium are accessible via the app!) It would be great if that function could be connected to the app!

title: Missing capability
body: When will this app have the capability to schedule laboratory appointments?

title: Appointment list and test results missing
body:
I have lost the display of up coming appointments.  I can schedule them.  Rather frustrating to see a blank page.  Tried reinstalling twice now.  More stars once it works again correctly.

Oh great you update the app and now the test results are missing too!

Hopefully they will all be on the web page.  This is a pain.

title: Useful appointment feature is gone
body: I liked the app when it broke down my past and future scheduled appointments. That feature is gone, and it’s harder to track my appointments now. I’ve ended up calling in to confirm times.

title: HP
body:
Edited 5/21: app no longer works - repeatedly says my session is no longer valid. No way to contact app support. 

Previous:
Love the app. Would love to receive notifications when my prescriptions are ready at a HP pharmacy.

title: Grateful for convenience and thoroughness
body: I use this app all the time to check weekly tests, set appointments, and renew prescriptions. The convenience for a person undergoing chemo cannot be overstated. Thank you for a wonderful tool.

title: Where’s the “Accept” button?
body: I’m rating it a one star because I can’t get get past the sign in stage 😖  Says to “accept” but there’s no button to push so app is useless. Would love to rate it better so please fix this 😊

title: Appointment phone number
body:
I can’t find it online, or on the app. Your automated app appointment scheduler just assigned me a time I can’t use

This is extremely frustrating

title: Limited functionality on iPhone
body: Cannot make appointments with an iPhone. Not all features are available on an iPhone. Works well on a computer.

title: No support info for app
body: There is no option to chat or call the HP technical help line for issues with the app. The new appointment option does not work in the app for past week and the appointments does show up only when logging into their web site (not using app)

title: Clinic doesn’t show as option
body: Tried to schedule an appointment online through the MyHP app and my clinic is not listed. It is a Park Nicollet clinic.  Update:  The app has been updated and I can now see my clinic.

title: Quick Appt & Quick add receipt
body:
Still the quickest way to schedule an appointment with my regular doctor.

Easy to add a receipt for my FSA.

title: Good but room for improvement
body: The HealthPartners app makes it easy to check on test results, reorder prescriptions, and find care. The downside is you can't make an appointment other than with your primary doctor or VirtuWell. It would be helpful to have the ability to make a follow-up appointment other than just with your primary physician. Overall, it's straightforward and easy to navigate.

title: Copay
body:
How the heck can you justify a 100% increase in copay fee?
$20 to $40

title: Overdue flag is not accurate
body:
I had my colonoscopy done at a non HP site and had my records sent to my primary Dr. The date was entered into my chart and I see the date in the app. The overdue message is listed and it is not accurate. I called web support and was told that it only changed the overdue glad if the procedure was done at a HP site. 

The code should be changed to use the most recent date of a procedure irrelevant if it was at a HO site or not. The current logic could cause someone to get a procedure that would not be covered y insurance. 

Really bad logic as it is now.

title: Mail order option
body: Great app but would like to see a mail order option on the app

title: Great easy to use app
body: Well designed app, easy to access information and schedule appointments.

title: Prescription help
body: Thanks for quick response to my request to get a new prescription, hopefully Dr. Nick Anderson in Arden Hills will agree to fill a slight medication alternative without needing a new exam!

title: Making appt not intuitive
body: Only option to get there is “next appointment” and then hit the plus button on that page. Way too buried if your goal is to simple scheduling experience.

title: Best App Ever

title: Great app but one issue

My older iphone 7 shows an icon for the ID Card as well as the MyPlan icon at the bottom. She has the iphone 8+ and an ipad mini and both are running 3.1.1(254). Those don’t appear on hers.

title: So Simple

It takes me less than one minute to renew my prescription. My 90 day supply arrives within a few days via mail. No trips to pharmacy, no waiting. So simple. 
I can check all my upcoming appointments as well. So Simple. Love it!

title: Can’t open PDFs in inbox

title: Good appp

title: They no longer have ID card or #

They no longer have this access and it is truly missed. If it is still there it is NOT easy to find. I just spent 10minutes under every button possible to locate my daughters ID number for paperwork for school activity as I did not have paper cards with and was unable to locate number.

title: Can’t access my children’s records
body: I have access to my children’s records, tests, medications, etc on the full site version of My HealthPartners, but not on the app. It would be much more convenient for me if I could access those things in the app like I can do for myself. If my intention were to use the app just for me, then I think it’s a very convenient and easy to use app. Like a previous reviewer had said, I can order refills of medication in about 15 seconds.

title: Good but not great

I can see my appointments and my daughters appointments but as far as receiving messages in our Inboxes, I can only see mine and have to log into the HP website to see my daughters. It’s annoying. If I’m missing where I can see it in the app, please tell me. 
Also, it’s frustrating that I have to log into HP website to send a message to my doctor. If we can read Inbox messages in the app, why can’t we write them too?

title: Cannot Schedule an Appointment

title: Such an improvement!

title: Super convenient!

title: Prescription refill
body: Using the app to refill my Rx was probably the easiest thing I’ll do today- it took me less than 15 seconds. Thx for making my life better!

body: When I try to make an appointment the app won’t let me click on anything. I have to do it so many times until it finally shows up but then it disappears again so I just give up on making appointments on here.

title: Great, but how come you can’t pay your premium on the app?
body: I use the Touch-ID log on the app and don’t remember what password I typed in so it’s convenient. If I can only pay my premium using HealthPartners.com site it requires a typed in password, which does no good since I don’t remember it and the fact I can’t change it because I can’t remember my security questions. Just want you to know what I’m having to go through, the above, all because you don’t put a pay my premium option on the app??? Makes no sense. If I can’t pay my premium my insurance will be dropped and therefore the app will become dead to me, which will be horrible PR for you considering you’re a health insurance company😜

title: Mixed experience

That’s frustrating, since the merger between these two companies is at least a couple of years old.

Thanks HealthPartners

wish i could email doc from app

body: I really wish we could email doctors from this app.

title: So convenient

title: Great app
body: Great app

title: Good App. Would have been great if they added the monthly bill payment
body: I find it easy and user friendly. Just want to pay my monthly insurance bill without logging through broweser.

title: More services
body: Works great for most things, just wish I could also schedule chiropractic appointments from the app as well

title: Very useful
body: Love that I can see test results and all upcoming appointments.

title: Unnecessarily restrictive
body:
The program locks a person out after too few tries snd then requires a NEW password.  We have too many passwords.  Requiring them to be changed when we mistype with fat fingers in cell phones is unreasonable.  

I have a new super secure password system, but forgot that I had already changed HP over to it.  Now I can’t use it.  So I am out if HP.  I cannot change all these passwords all over again.   The doctor can go back to calling or mailing as I cannot get in to see my data.  

Overly complex and restrictive.

title: Not sure what happened???
body: This app worked great for years and now I can’t get passed selecting a clinic! Very frustrated because I loved the way way it worked before. Can’t make an appt. on here so only good for checking test results.......I think, unless that changed too.

title: Love the app, but where’s my inbox?
body: I love the app, but one thing that’s frustrating is that there doesn’t appear to be an inbox to view/reply to messages that you receive from your doctor. Kind of a hassle to need to go to the website to reply to these each time when there’s an app on your phone that should allow you to do the same, albeit even with giving you alerts on said messages.

title: Not very useful
body:
Cost estimator: doesn’t work. 
Plan information: doesn’t exist at all, except remaining deductibles. 
I can’t even see my plan information, copays and coinsurances with this app.

title: What's the point?
body: So I tried this app and don't understand why it exists. I was told the My Health Partners apps and online account would allow me to view test results, contact doctors and see prescriptions. I navigated all the functions and it's basically another search engine. As a topper, most of the doctor searches result in an error message. Give me something that's useful to improve the patients experience with health care!!!!!

title: Needs Wallet Support
body: Really convenient to have this app. Wish I could save my card to the wallet app.

title: Should not call it myHP
body:
I almost deleted it because I thought it was to do with the HP printer I threw out. 
Would have had to reinstall it and start all over.

title: Most Recent Update Leaves a Lot Missing
body: I noticed during the most recent major update the app no longer has as many functions as before. Particularly the ability to refill prescriptions which I used it a lot for. That feature alone made it worthwhile and without it there’s not much use of it now.

title: Useful app but 1 request
body:
This is one of the most useful apps on my phone.  I always have access to my card, my in network choices, and my account status.  I travel for business and this helps me pick providers when I'm in a new place.  

I do have one request.  I am able to see all medical cards on my family account but I can't see all medical appointments.  Specifically, I want the option to see my minor child's appointments as well.  I understand keeping my wife's appointments private.

title: Needs Apple wallet for ID card
body: The only good reason to use this app appears to be to not have to carry your ID card in your wallet.  This app achieves that purpose but you actually need to launch the app to get it.  Would be much easier if there was Apple wallet ID cards.  It's there for other providers.  Why not HP?

title: Eyeglass prescription?
body: Why can't access eyeglass prescription on the app?!?

title: Missing messages
body: It looks awesome. Would be nice to be able to access the messages between me and my doctor.

title: Great Functionality, UI, services and more
body: I am actually super impressed with this app. It has mobile mail order pharmacy, medical records, and even a digital copy of my insurance cards so that I don't have to carry copies of them in my wallet anymore. The UI is really great: smooth, responsive, and iOS 10 ready. I get notifications with my explanations of benefits, immunization records, and it has touchID support (and even a touchID prompt button for when touchID fails to load automatically!). I can't imagine all the security headaches dealing with both insurance and health records across providers, pharmacies, and corporate what have you but I imagine they are immense. This app would never let on how difficult it is to put all of this in one place. And this is from a nonprofit regional healthcare and insurance provider. It is light years ahead of any national banking chain app I have used, and it just works. The USbank app is garbage and their information is similar in security and they are a national chain. If I had a complaint it would be some clunkiness in renewing prescriptions since my doctor is not in the Healthpartners system directly (university system) so coordinating renewals is occasionally a pain. Other than that I can't recommend this app more highly.

title: Can't pay my premium
body:
I only acquired this app in an attempt to pay my premium. That cannot be accomplished with this app.

It does look like a nice app though.

title: Unstable App
body: The app was useful for awhile. I used to be able to make appointments and view test results without an issue until just recently. Now it attempts to log me in and then it logs out right away and says "Session is no longer valid" or something on the lines of that.

title: They'd
body: The new website is not user friendly compared to the previous version

title: Need some app revisions please
body: Two immediate concerns..when one needs to fax their insurance card to a physician office they need to enter the fax number once for the front of the card, switch to the back of the card, hit fax, then enter the number again. That needs to get streamlined. There also needs to be an email option, most physician offices would prefer to get these as images for their emr than a faxed piece of paper.

title: App needs a lot of work!
body: App only shows HP locations (wanted PN location). After trying to update my medical insurance for the 6th time, which it still didn't save, I gave up and deleted the app. Wish I could it less than 1 star.

title: App won't work
body: I have an iPhone 5 and I've downloaded the app three separate times but the app only opens to tile screen then immediately closes.

title: Won't start on my iPod5

title: No lab appt from phone

The app overall is actually pretty slick, but the search tool for finding a clinic is lacking one very important feature: the option to search for a clinic by name. 

In general, I like the app better than the regular website because it's much less clunky than the website is, but not having a fully comprehensive search tool makes the app obsolete - it's easier to go online or call HP directly than it is to scroll through a list of search results.

More of time wasted along with all the phone calls, texts and emails you will be bombarded with it you sign up. Specialty jobs are listed as diagnostic jobs. There is no fixing them and there are many.  Most companies get their job posting from the same source. Save your time.
Update: 2/2026

title: Bait and switch
body:
So I’ve been on this site for over 2 years now. The search filter doesn’t save your preferences. Each time you look it offers a “you looked at these types of jobs: ICU MT, PCU….) but when you click on it, days, nights…. Doesn’t stay. ****(edit) still doesn’t no matter what their response is. 
You also can’t search for only compact states. So you have to sort through all the non compact states to find the ones you want. ***(edit) there is a compact option; maybe it should be removed from the dropdown and made a separate box so people can see it better. 

But the most annoying part is that agencies can post whatever job/pay they want just to get you to talk to them even if it’s not available. Today for instance Rang Healthcare had just posted a job for $3648 for 48 hours days, $76 an hour. Somehow that job was not available. Now they’re offering me a job in a completely different state for $2400 for 36 days, $66 an hour. Now factor in how much I have to pay for on my own, it doesn’t make sense for me personally to take that position. This happens many times. 
****(edit) so I found that Vivian is actually leading this no matter what they say. Apparently recruiting companies will click to remove a position but Vivian will keep it there to build traction for the state. I’ve called out many agencies and they’ve put Vivian under the bus here stating they asked for it to be removed but Vivian had refused to. So if Vivian truly cares about this maybe they should start issuing warnings for jobs to stop this with a ban at the 3rd strike. I’ve read many reviews where this is happening. So if Vivian really cared; why is it still happening?!? 

I posted all of my paperwork, references, immunizations… everything, companies contact you and want you to send them things but have to insiste they get it from Vivian. What’s the point of putting all of these things on Vivian if the companies don’t want to use it? 
**(edit) again: some agencies want you to use theirs even when you insist. It should be agreed that they use the info from Vivian or get off the platform. It takes a lot of work to upload this and fill out all of the applications and skills checklist. 


So my 3 star review still stands. You must submit to 10 jobs to hear back from maybe 6 recruiters and maybe 1 of those postings is accurate.

title: Useless
body: I have been using this app for at least a year and the recruiters on this app continuously ghost me after conducting interviews despite having the qualifications as an RN. This is proving to be an unreliable app to find a new nursing job. I wouldn’t recommend.

title: Good but needs an update
body: I do like this app, it’s a great place to see a very broad view of what contracts are out there but my biggest complaint is you can’t see what specialty the contract is on any of them so when you click interested you have to ask the recruiter and I work pediatrics which is not the specialty it’s an patient age range (most recruiters say that’s the specialty) but there’s still oncology and cardio and neuro or med/surg units within pediatrics and if the recruiters were required to put the specialty in for each listing it would save us all a lot of time. title: Not as advertised, unfortunately
body: So most of these postings are an attempt to get  you lured in. These contracts are usually unavailable by the time you submit everything.  Or sometimes the contracts aren’t available for at least a month even though it’s advertised as an immediate start date. The idea is great. However it’s a bit time consuming for you. It’s embarrassing to start profiles for all these agencies and have my references fill out documents only for the contract to be unavailable. Do as you will with this information.

title: Listed Jobs
body: Vivian makes you list all of your information, resume, licensing, and credentials and the recruiters DO NOT HAVE ACCESS TO ANY OF IT. 95% of the jobs are NOT available and it’s just a ploy to get you talking with recruiters. Recruiters will have you do EVERYTHING to submit you to a job just to tell you it’s no longer available. They do this to have you on board in hopes to try to submit you to whatever jobs they have available. It’s honestly a big waste of time. It’s better to just go on an agency’s website and apply directly through them rather than waste your time and energy with Vivian. With that being said, I DO NOT RECOMMEND THIS APP FOR ANY TRAVEL NURSE LOOKING FOR AN ASSIGNMENT.

title: The app needs work

Most job postings do not include specifics, ie; CVOR.

title: Occupational Therapist
body: Vivian connected me with CoreMedical Group which were some of the greatest, most supportive people I have ever worked with. Vivian technology and CoreMedical Group were supportive through the whole job search process and even dealing with some unusual circumstances, but Vivian kept the communications open and flowing until my position was completely secured. I just can’t give enough positive feedback about Vivian staff and CoreMedical Group personnel professionalism, prompt return of calls, texts, and emails. Vivian is a great tool and it helped me find a job in a few days after looking on my own for a few years. I started my new job and I love it. Choose carefully and if you just want the best choose Vivian and couple that with CoreMedical Group and move forward. Don’t hesitate and stay in a job you don’t like or want, there are tons of jobs out there and Vivian with CoreMedical can help you find the best fit. You will have to do quite a bit of paperwork but it is certainly worth it for more pay, better benefits and a happier work life. Weather your a PT, OT, SLP, RD, LPN, or RN, I strongly urge you to give Vivian a hard look and give CoreMedical Group a chance and see what comes up. You have nothing to lose except a job you don’t want. Take care, Rich Watt OTR/L, ATP

title: Updates

title: Changes
body:
CareRev started out great: it was easy to pick up shifts, the pay was good… but now they’re moving to W2 from 1099 in WA and the pay is dropping significantly. They’re marketing the change like it’s a good thing, but it doesn’t seem to benefit the heathcare providers who are picking up shifts at all. This also means more paperwork, and onboarding with People 2.0… 

Either labor laws in WA forced CareRev to make this change (in which care I wish they would have been honest and said ‘sorry, we have to do this’ OR it’s better for their bottom line. Maybe both. In any case it’s a net-negative for the workers. 

Also, AYA either owns or is affiliated with People 2.0… They seem to have their tendrils everywhere. Not a fan.

title: LPN
body: I’ve gotten all of my assignments from Vivian and the reward program is great. I’ve had some great recruiters throughout my process, which have gone above. I recommend this app.

title: THE app

title: I get SO many recruiter emails despite deleting my account!
body:
I was getting so many unsolicited phone calls, texts, and emails to the point that I just deleted my account and I STILL am getting endless emails. This is ridiculous. I can’t see now because I deleted my account but I’m pretty sure I also checked off an option to request that they only contact me via the app. But that didn’t help. 

Apart from the annoyance, I had tried to reach out to recruiters regarding the posted jobs, but when we started a conversation they all ended up ignoring my request for info on that specific job and pressured me to set up a profile with their company.

title: Technical
body:
Having a lot of technical issues, especially with the app not loading properly. There is a huge delay in notification close out on all platforms, as well! 

Great app for nurses and recruiters, but it just needs more technological advancements right now.

title: Great app
body: I am so amazed at the user friendliness of this app and the simple steps it took me to develop my profile. Thank you Vivian!

title: This app lie so much
body: They put false rates to get you on their company.

title: Scam. Bait and switch
body:
Catfished twice. 
Applied for a travel nurse position. 
Was quoted a weekly rate, after fully applying and allowing reference checks the rate dropped almost in half. Was told by a recruiter that the rate was accidentally listed higher than it should have been by Vivian. Ok we are all human things happen. 
Few days later I applied again through the Vivian app with another travel nurse company. Same thing, was quoted a specific rate, verified before completing application and reference checks. After completing everything the rate dropped significantly! Do not trust these scammers.

title: Good app, just some slight issues
body: Great platform to post jobs and find candidates. Sometimes I get delayed notifications and issues with the notifications clearing, but that’s about it. Support is very responsive and friendly. Been using Vivian for almost a year now and couldn’t imagine recruiting without it!

I have to wait up to 24 hours, if they even respond at all, for a recruiter to message me back to tell me this, when it should be on the posting to begin with. This is a huge waste of time and I’m about to give up on this App.

It is also my litmus test about what the market is doing in areas that I wanna travel to. title: Basically useless
body:
I can't speak to this app's efficacy for RNs, but if
you're a pharmacy tech, don't waste your time.
Most of the job postings are from agencies you've
never heard of, with recruiters who might respond
within a week or might just never respond at all. In
some cases, a recruiter will reach out to you and
ask you for all the information that could easily be
found in the resume you already uploaded, i.e.
"how many years of experience do you have?" or
"Are you nationally certified/which state are you
registered in?"
The app itself is glitchy, as another review mentioned, a job will post as new and then you look at it and it’s already filled. Based on how slow moving these recruiters are, I doubt they filled it that quickly. 
Also, when you mark yourself as interested in a job, it doesn’t show that you’ve already marked it in the job listings. So I’d end up marking my interest on the same job several times without realizing it. 
I spent a little over 2 months constantly checking
this app, actively looking for a job. When I finally
gave up and applied directly to a better agency,
I had a contract in less than a week.
It’s very disappointing that a healthcare service can be this lacking in the year 2022. Do better.
